10_1_1.NKJV: Now it came to pass after the death of Saul, when David had returned from the slaughter of the Amalekites, and David had stayed two days in Ziklag,
10_1_1.KJV: Now it came to pass after the death of Saul, when David was returned from the slaughter of the Amalekites, and David had abode two days in Ziklag;

10_1_1.NASB: Now it came about after the death of Saul, when David had returned from the slaughter of the Amalekites, that David remained two days in Ziklag.
10_1_1.NLT: After the death of Saul, David returned from his victory over the Amalekites and spent two days in Ziklag.
10_1_1.WEB: After the death of Saul, when David had returned from the slaughter of the Amalekites, and David had stayed two days in Ziklag;
10_1_1.CSB17: After the death of Saul, David returned from defeating the Amalekites and stayed at Ziklag two days.

10_1_1.MSG: Shortly after Saul died, David returned to Ziklag from his rout of the Amalekites.

10_1_1.NRSVCE: After the death of Saul, when David had returned from defeating the Amalekites, David remained two days in Ziklag.

10_1_2.NKJV: on the third day, behold, it happened that a man came from Saul’s camp with his clothes torn and dust on his head. So it was, when he came to David, that he fell to the ground and prostrated himself.
10_1_2.KJV: It came even to pass on the third day, that, behold, a man came out of the camp from Saul with his clothes rent, and earth upon his head: and so it was, when he came to David, that he fell to the earth, and did obeisance.

10_1_2.NASB: On the third day, behold, a man came out of the camp from Saul, with his clothes torn and dust on his head. And it came about when he came to David that he fell to the ground and prostrated himself.
10_1_2.NLT: On the third day a man arrived from Saul’s army camp. He had torn his clothes and put dirt on his head to show that he was in mourning. He fell to the ground before David in deep respect.

10_1_2.AMP: On the third day a man came [unexpectedly] from Saul's camp with his clothes torn and dust on his head [as in mourning]. When he came to David, he bowed to the ground and lay himself face down [in an act of great respect and submission].
10_1_2.MSG: Three days later a man showed up unannounced from Saul's army camp.Disheveled and obviously in mourning, he fell to his knees in respect before David.

10_1_3.NKJV: And David said to him, “Where have you come from?” So he said to him, “I have escaped from the camp of Israel.”

10_1_3.NLT: “Where have you come from?” David asked. “I escaped from the Israelite camp,” the man replied.

10_1_4.NKJV: Then David said to him, “How did the matter go? Please tell me.” And he answered, “The people have fled from the battle, many of the people are fallen and dead, and Saul and Jonathan his son are dead also.”

10_1_4.WEB: David said to him, “How did it go? Please tell me.” He answered, “The people have fled from the battle, and many of the people also have fallen and are dead. Saul and Jonathan his son are dead also.”

10_1_4.AMP: David said to him, "How did it go? Please tell me." He answered, "The people have fled from the battle. Also, many of the people have fallen and are dead; Saul and Jonathan his son are also dead."
10_1_4.MSG: "So what happened?" said David. "What's the news?"He said, "The Israelites have fled the battlefield, leaving a lot of their dead comrades behind. And Saul and his son Jonathan are dead."
10_1_4.NIV: “What happened?” David asked. “Tell me.”He said, “The men fled from the battle. Many of them fell and died. And Saul and his son Jonathan are dead.”
10_1_4.NRSVCE: David said to him, “How did things go? Tell me!” He answered, “The army fled from the battle, but also many of the army fell and died; and Saul and his son Jonathan also died.”

10_1_19.NKJV: “The beauty of Israel is slain on your high places! How the mighty have fallen!

10_1_19.ESV: “Your glory, O Israel, is slain on your high places! How the mighty have fallen!

10_1_20.NKJV: Tell it not in Gath, Proclaim it not in the streets of Ashkelon— Lest the daughters of the Philistines rejoice, Lest the daughters of the uncircumcised triumph.

10_1_20.NASB: 'Tell [it] not in Gath, Proclaim it not in the streets of Ashkelon, Or the daughters of the Philistines will rejoice, The daughters of the uncircumcised will exult.
10_1_20.NLT: Don’t announce the news in Gath,don’t proclaim it in the streets of Ashkelon,or the daughters of the Philistines will rejoiceand the pagans will laugh in triumph.
10_1_20.WEB: Don’t tell it in Gath. Don’t publish it in the streets of Ashkelon, lest the daughters of the Philistines rejoice, lest the daughters of the uncircumcised triumph.
10_1_20.CSB17: Do not tell it in Gath, don’t announce it in the marketplaces of Ashkelon, or the daughters of the Philistines will rejoice, and the daughters of the uncircumcised will celebrate.
10_1_20.AMP: "Tell it not in Gath, Proclaim it not in the streets of Ashkelon, Or the daughters of the Philistines will rejoice, The daughters of the uncircumcised (pagans) will exult.
10_1_20.MSG: Don't announce it in the city of Gath,don't post the news in the streets of Ashkelon.Don't give those coarse Philistine girlsone more excuse for a drunken party!
10_1_20.NIV: “Tell it not in Gath,proclaim it not in the streets of Ashkelon,lest the daughters of the Philistines be glad,lest the daughters of the uncircumcised rejoice.
10_1_20.NRSVCE: Tell it not in Gath, proclaim it not in the streets of Ashkelon; or the daughters of the Philistines will rejoice, the daughters of the uncircumcised will exult.

10_1_21.NKJV: “O mountains of Gilboa, Let there be no dew nor rain upon you, Nor fields of offerings. For the shield of the mighty is cast away there! The shield of Saul, not anointed with oil.
10_1_21.KJV: Ye mountains of Gilboa, let there be no dew, neither let there be rain, upon you, nor fields of offerings: for there the shield of the mighty is vilely cast away, the shield of Saul, as though he had not been anointed with oil.
10_1_21.ESV: “You mountains of Gilboa, let there be no dew or rain upon you, nor fields of offerings! For there the shield of the mighty was defiled, the shield of Saul, not anointed with oil.
10_1_21.NASB: 'O mountains of Gilboa, Let not dew or rain be on you, nor fields of offerings; For there the shield of the mighty was defiled, The shield of Saul, not anointed with oil.
10_1_21.NLT: O mountains of Gilboa,let there be no dew or rain upon you,nor fruitful fields producing offerings of grain.For there the shield of the mighty heroes was defiled;the shield of Saul will no longer be anointed with oil.
10_1_21.WEB: You mountains of Gilboa, let there be no dew or rain on you, and no fields of offerings; For there the shield of the mighty was defiled and cast away, The shield of Saul was not anointed with oil.
10_1_21.CSB17: Mountains of Gilboa, let no dew or rain be on you, or fields of offerings, for there the shield of the mighty was defiled — the shield of Saul, no longer anointed with oil.
10_1_21.AMP: " O mountains of Gilboa, Let not dew or rain be upon you, nor fields with offerings; For there the shield of the mighty was defiled, The shield of Saul, [dry, cracked] not anointed with oil.
10_1_21.MSG: No more dew or rain for you, hills of Gilboa,and not a drop from springs and wells,For there the warriors' shields were dragged through the mud,Saul's shield left there to rot.
10_1_21.NIV: “O mountains of Gilboa,may you have neither dew nor rain,nor fields that yield offerings of grain.For there the shield of the mighty was defiled,the shield of Saul—no longer rubbed with oil.
10_1_21.NRSVCE: You mountains of Gilboa, let there be no dew or rain upon you, nor bounteous fields! For there the shield of the mighty was defiled, the shield of Saul, anointed with oil no more.

10_1_22.NKJV: From the blood of the slain, From the fat of the mighty, The bow of Jonathan did not turn back, And the sword of Saul did not return empty.
10_1_22.KJV: From the blood of the slain, from the fat of the mighty, the bow of Jonathan turned not back, and the sword of Saul returned not empty.
10_1_22.ESV: “From the blood of the slain, from the fat of the mighty, the bow of Jonathan turned not back, and the sword of Saul returned not empty.
10_1_22.NASB: 'From the blood of the slain, from the fat of the mighty, The bow of Jonathan did not turn back, And the sword of Saul did not return empty.
10_1_22.NLT: The bow of Jonathan was powerful,and the sword of Saul did its mighty work.They shed the blood of their enemiesand pierced the bodies of mighty heroes.
10_1_22.WEB: From the blood of the slain, from the fat of the mighty, Jonathan’s bow didn’t turn back. Saul’s sword didn’t return empty.
10_1_22.CSB17: Jonathan’s bow never retreated, Saul’s sword never returned unstained, from the blood of the slain, from the flesh of the mighty.
10_1_22.AMP: "From the blood of the slain, from the fat of the mighty, The bow of Jonathan did not turn back, And the sword of Saul did not return empty.
10_1_22.MSG: Jonathan's bow was bold—the bigger they were the harder they fell.Saul's sword was fearless—once out of the scabbard, nothing could stop it.
10_1_22.NIV: From the blood of the slain,from the flesh of the mighty,the bow of Jonathan did not turn back,the sword of Saul did not return unsatisfied.
10_1_22.NRSVCE: From the blood of the slain, from the fat of the mighty, the bow of Jonathan did not turn back, nor the sword of Saul return empty.

10_1_23.NKJV: “Saul and Jonathan were beloved and pleasant in their lives, And in their death they were not divided; They were swifter than eagles, They were stronger than lions.
10_1_23.KJV: Saul and Jonathan were lovely and pleasant in their lives, and in their death they were not divided: they were swifter than eagles, they were stronger than lions. pleasant: or, sweet

10_1_23.NASB: 'Saul and Jonathan, beloved and pleasant in their life, And in their death they were not parted; They were swifter than eagles, They were stronger than lions.
10_1_23.NLT: How beloved and gracious were Saul and Jonathan!They were together in life and in death.They were swifter than eagles,stronger than lions.

10_1_23.CSB17: Saul and Jonathan, loved and delightful, they were not parted in life or in death. They were swifter than eagles, stronger than lions.
10_1_23.AMP: "Saul and Jonathan, beloved and lovely and friends in their lives, And in their death they were not separated; They were swifter than eagles, They were stronger than lions.
10_1_23.MSG: Saul and Jonathan—beloved, beautiful!Together in life, together in death.Swifter than plummeting eagles,stronger than proud lions.
10_1_23.NIV: “Saul and Jonathan—in life they were loved and gracious,and in death they were not parted.They were swifter than eagles,they were stronger than lions.

10_1_25.NIV: “How the mighty have fallen in battle!Jonathan lies slain on your heights.
10_1_25.NRSVCE: How the mighty have fallen in the midst of the battle! Jonathan lies slain upon your high places.

10_1_26.NKJV: I am distressed for you, my brother Jonathan; You have been very pleasant to me; Your love to me was wonderful, Surpassing the love of women.
10_1_26.KJV: I am distressed for thee, my brother Jonathan: very pleasant hast thou been unto me: thy love to me was wonderful, passing the love of women.
10_1_26.ESV: I am distressed for you, my brother Jonathan; very pleasant have you been to me; your love to me was extraordinary, surpassing the love of women.
10_1_26.NASB: 'I am distressed for you, my brother Jonathan; You have been very pleasant to me. Your love to me was more wonderful Than the love of women.

10_2_1.NKJV: It happened after this that David inquired of the LORD, saying, “Shall I go up to any of the cities of Judah?” And the LORD said to him, “Go up.” David said, “Where shall I go up?” And He said, “To Hebron.”
10_2_1.KJV: And it came to pass after this, that David enquired of the LORD, saying, Shall I go up into any of the cities of Judah? And the LORD said unto him, Go up. And David said, Whither shall I go up? And he said, Unto Hebron.
10_2_1.ESV: After this David inquired of the LORD, “Shall I go up into any of the cities of Judah?” And the LORD said to him, “Go up.” David said, “To which shall I go up?” And he said, “To Hebron.”
10_2_1.NASB: Then it came about afterwards that David inquired of the LORD, saying, 'Shall I go up to one of the cities of Judah?' And the LORD said to him, 'Go up.' So David said, 'Where shall I go up?' And He said, 'To Hebron.'
10_2_1.NLT: After this, David asked the LORD, “Should I move back to one of the towns of Judah?” “Yes,” the LORD replied. Then David asked, “Which town should I go to?” “To Hebron,” the LORD answered.
10_2_1.WEB: After this, David inquired of Yahweh, saying, “Shall I go up into any of the cities of Judah?” Yahweh said to him, “Go up.” David said, “Where shall I go up?” He said, “To Hebron.”

10_2_1.AMP: So it happened after this that David inquired of the LORD, saying, "Shall I go up into one of the cities of Judah?" And the LORD said to him, "Go up." David asked, "Where shall I go?" And He said, "To Hebron."

10_2_7.KJV: Therefore now let your hands be strengthened, and be ye valiant : for your master Saul is dead, and also the house of Judah have anointed me king over them. be ye: Heb. be ye the sons of valour
10_2_7.ESV: Now therefore let your hands be strong, and be valiant, for Saul your lord is dead, and the house of Judah has anointed me king over them.”
10_2_7.NASB: 'Now therefore, let your hands be strong and be valiant; for Saul your lord is dead, and also the house of Judah has anointed me king over them.'
10_2_7.NLT: Now that Saul is dead, I ask you to be my strong and loyal subjects like the people of Judah, who have anointed me as their new king.”
10_2_7.WEB: Now therefore let your hands be strong, and be valiant; for Saul your lord is dead, and also the house of Judah have anointed me king over them.”
10_2_7.CSB17: Therefore, be strong and valiant, for though Saul your lord is dead, the house of Judah has anointed me king over them.”
10_2_7.AMP: So now, let your hands be strong and be valiant; for your lord Saul is dead, and also the house of Judah has anointed me king over them."
10_2_7.MSG: Strengthen your resolve and do what must be done. Your master, Saul, is dead. The citizens of Judah have made me their king."
10_2_7.NIV: Now then, be strong and brave, for Saul your master is dead, and the house of Judah has anointed me king over them.”
10_2_7.NRSVCE: Therefore let your hands be strong, and be valiant; for Saul your lord is dead, and the house of Judah has anointed me king over them.”

10_2_8.NKJV: But Abner the son of Ner, commander of Saul’s army, took Ishbosheth the son of Saul and brought him over to Mahanaim;
10_2_8.KJV: But Abner the son of Ner, captain of Saul's host, took Ishbosheth the son of Saul, and brought him over to Mahanaim; Saul's host: Heb. the host which was Saul's Ishbosheth: or, Eshbaal
10_2_8.ESV: But Abner the son of Ner, commander of Saul's army, took Ish-bosheth the son of Saul and brought him over to Mahanaim,
10_2_8.NASB: But Abner the son of Ner, commander of Saul's army, had taken Ish-bosheth the son of Saul and brought him over to Mahanaim.
10_2_8.NLT: But Abner son of Ner, the commander of Saul’s army, had already gone to Mahanaim with Saul’s son Ishbosheth.
10_2_8.WEB: Now Abner the son of Ner, captain of Saul’s army, had taken Ishbosheth the son of Saul, and brought him over to Mahanaim;
10_2_8.CSB17: Abner son of Ner, commander of Saul’s army, took Saul’s son Ish-bosheth and moved him to Mahanaim.
10_2_8.AMP: Now [Saul's cousin] Abner the son of Ner, commander of Saul's army, had taken Ish-bosheth the son of Saul and brought him over to Mahanaim.
10_2_8.MSG: In the meantime, Abner son of Ner, commander of Saul's army, had taken Saul's son Ish-Bosheth to Mahanaim
10_2_8.NIV: War Between the Houses of David and SaulMeanwhile, Abner son of Ner, the commander of Saul’s army, had taken Ish-Bosheth son of Saul and brought him over to Mahanaim.
10_2_8.NRSVCE: But Abner son of Ner, commander of Saul’s army, had taken Ishbaal son of Saul, and brought him over to Mahanaim.

10_2_9.NKJV: and he made him king over Gilead, over the Ashurites, over Jezreel, over Ephraim, over Benjamin, and over all Israel.
10_2_9.KJV: And made him king over Gilead, and over the Ashurites, and over Jezreel, and over Ephraim, and over Benjamin, and over all Israel.
10_2_9.ESV: and he made him king over Gilead and the Ashurites and Jezreel and Ephraim and Benjamin and all Israel.
10_2_9.NASB: He made him king over Gilead, over the Ashurites, over Jezreel, over Ephraim, and over Benjamin, even over all Israel.
10_2_9.NLT: There he proclaimed Ishbosheth king over Gilead, Jezreel, Ephraim, Benjamin, the land of the Ashurites, and all the rest of Israel.
10_2_9.WEB: and he made him king over Gilead, and over the Ashurites, and over Jezreel, and over Ephraim, and over Benjamin, and over all Israel.
10_2_9.CSB17: He made him king over Gilead, Asher, Jezreel, Ephraim, Benjamin — over all Israel.
10_2_9.AMP: He made him king over Gilead, over the Ashurites, over Jezreel, over Ephraim, over Benjamin, even over all Israel [except Judah].
10_2_9.MSG: and made him king over Gilead, over Asher, over Jezreel, over Ephraim, over Benjamin—king, as it turns out, over all Israel.
10_2_9.NIV: He made him king over Gilead, Ashuri and Jezreel, and also over Ephraim, Benjamin and all Israel.
10_2_9.NRSVCE: He made him king over Gilead, the Ashurites, Jezreel, Ephraim, Benjamin, and over all Israel.

10_2_10.NKJV: Ishbosheth, Saul’s son, was forty years old when he began to reign over Israel, and he reigned two years. Only the house of Judah followed David.
10_2_10.KJV: Ishbosheth Saul's son was forty years old when he began to reign over Israel, and reigned two years. But the house of Judah followed David.
10_2_10.ESV: Ish-bosheth, Saul's son, was forty years old when he began to reign over Israel, and he reigned two years. But the house of Judah followed David.
10_2_10.NASB: Ish-bosheth, Saul's son, was forty years old when he became king over Israel, and he was king for two years. The house of Judah, however, followed David.
10_2_10.NLT: Ishbosheth, Saul’s son, was forty years old when he became king, and he ruled from Mahanaim for two years. Meanwhile, the people of Judah remained loyal to David.
10_2_10.WEB: Ishbosheth, Saul’s son, was forty years old when he began to reign over Israel, and he reigned two years. But the house of Judah followed David.
10_2_10.CSB17: Saul’s son Ish-bosheth was forty years old when he became king over Israel; he reigned for two years. The house of Judah, however, followed David.
10_2_10.AMP: Ish-bosheth, Saul's son, was forty years old when he became king over Israel, and he reigned for two years. But the house of Judah followed David.
10_2_10.MSG: Ish-Bosheth, Saul's son, was forty years old when he was made king over Israel. He lasted only two years. But the people of Judah stuck with David.
10_2_10.NIV: Ish-Bosheth son of Saul was forty years old when he became king over Israel, and he reigned two years. The house of Judah, however, followed David.
10_2_10.NRSVCE: Ishbaal, Saul’s son, was forty years old when he began to reign over Israel, and he reigned two years. But the house of Judah followed David.

10_2_11.NKJV: And the time that David was king in Hebron over the house of Judah was seven years and six months.
10_2_11.KJV: And the time that David was king in Hebron over the house of Judah was seven years and six months. time: Heb. number of days
10_2_11.ESV: And the time that David was king in Hebron over the house of Judah was seven years and six months.
10_2_11.NASB: The time that David was king in Hebron over the house of Judah was seven years and six months.
10_2_11.NLT: David made Hebron his capital, and he ruled as king of Judah for seven and a half years.
10_2_11.WEB: The time that David was king in Hebron over the house of Judah was seven years and six months.
10_2_11.CSB17: The length of time that David was king in Hebron over the house of Judah was seven years and six months.
10_2_11.AMP: And the time that David was king in Hebron over the house of Judah was seven years and six months.
10_2_11.MSG: David ruled the people of Judah from Hebron for seven and a half years.
10_2_11.NIV: The length of time David was king in Hebron over the house of Judah was seven years and six months.
10_2_11.NRSVCE: The time that David was king in Hebron over the house of Judah was seven years and six months.

10_2_12.NKJV: Now Abner the son of Ner, and the servants of Ishbosheth the son of Saul, went out from Mahanaim to Gibeon.
10_2_12.KJV: And Abner the son of Ner, and the servants of Ishbosheth the son of Saul, went out from Mahanaim to Gibeon.
10_2_12.ESV: Abner the son of Ner, and the servants of Ish-bosheth the son of Saul, went out from Mahanaim to Gibeon.
10_2_12.NASB: Now Abner the son of Ner, went out from Mahanaim to Gibeon with the servants of Ish-bosheth the son of Saul.
10_2_12.NLT: One day Abner led Ishbosheth’s troops from Mahanaim to Gibeon.
10_2_12.WEB: Abner the son of Ner, and the servants of Ishbosheth the son of Saul, went out from Mahanaim to Gibeon.
10_2_12.CSB17: Abner son of Ner and soldiers of Ish-bosheth son of Saul marched out from Mahanaim to Gibeon.
10_2_12.AMP: Now Abner the son of Ner and the servants of Ish-bosheth the son of Saul went out from Mahanaim to Gibeon.
10_2_12.MSG: One day Abner son of Ner set out from Mahanaim with the soldiers of Ish-Bosheth son of Saul, headed for Gibeon.
10_2_12.NIV: Abner son of Ner, together with the men of Ish-Bosheth son of Saul, left Mahanaim and went to Gibeon.
10_2_12.NRSVCE: Abner son of Ner, and the servants of Ishbaal son of Saul, went out from Mahanaim to Gibeon.

10_2_13.NKJV: And Joab the son of Zeruiah, and the servants of David, went out and met them by the pool of Gibeon. So they sat down, one on one side of the pool and the other on the other side of the pool.
10_2_13.KJV: And Joab the son of Zeruiah, and the servants of David, went out, and met together by the pool of Gibeon: and they sat down, the one on the one side of the pool, and the other on the other side of the pool. together: Heb. them together
10_2_13.ESV: And Joab the son of Zeruiah and the servants of David went out and met them at the pool of Gibeon. And they sat down, the one on the one side of the pool, and the other on the other side of the pool.
10_2_13.NASB: And Joab the son of Zeruiah and the servants of David went out and met them by the pool of Gibeon; and they sat down, one on the one side of the pool and the other on the other side of the pool.
10_2_13.NLT: About the same time, Joab son of Zeruiah led David’s troops out and met them at the pool of Gibeon. The two groups sat down there, facing each other from opposite sides of the pool.
10_2_13.WEB: Joab the son of Zeruiah and David’s servants went out, and met them by the pool of Gibeon; and they sat down, the one on the one side of the pool, and the other on the other side of the pool.
10_2_13.CSB17: So Joab son of Zeruiah and David’s soldiers marched out and met them by the pool of Gibeon. The two groups took up positions on opposite sides of the pool.
10_2_13.AMP: Joab [David's nephew] the son of Zeruiah and the servants of David also went out and met them by the pool of Gibeon. They sat down, with one group on one side of the pool and the other group on the other side of the pool.
10_2_13.MSG: Joab son of Zeruiah, with David's soldiers, also set out. They met at the Pool of Gibeon, Abner's group on one side, Joab's on the other.
10_2_13.NIV: Joab son of Zeruiah and David’s men went out and met them at the pool of Gibeon. One group sat down on one side of the pool and one group on the other side.
10_2_13.NRSVCE: Joab son of Zeruiah, and the servants of David, went out and met them at the pool of Gibeon. One group sat on one side of the pool, while the other sat on the other side of the pool.

10_2_14.NKJV: Then Abner said to Joab, “Let the young men now arise and compete before us.” And Joab said, “Let them arise.”
10_2_14.KJV: And Abner said to Joab, Let the young men now arise, and play before us. And Joab said, Let them arise.
10_2_14.ESV: And Abner said to Joab, “Let the young men arise and compete before us.” And Joab said, “Let them arise.”
10_2_14.NASB: Then Abner said to Joab, 'Now let the young men arise and hold a contest before us.' And Joab said, 'Let them arise.'
10_2_14.NLT: Then Abner suggested to Joab, “Let’s have a few of our warriors fight hand to hand here in front of us.” “All right,” Joab agreed.
10_2_14.WEB: Abner said to Joab, “Please let the young men arise and play before us!” Joab said, “Let them arise!”
10_2_14.CSB17: Then Abner said to Joab, “Let’s have the young men get up and compete in front of us.” “Let them get up,” Joab replied.
10_2_14.AMP: Then Abner said to Joab, "Let the young men now stand and have a contest before us." And Joab said, "Let them stand."
10_2_14.MSG: Abner challenged Joab, "Put up your best fighters. Let's see them do their stuff."Joab said, "Good! Let them go at it!"
10_2_14.NIV: Then Abner said to Joab, “Let’s have some of the young men get up and fight hand to hand in front of us.”“All right, let them do it,” Joab said.
10_2_14.NRSVCE: Abner said to Joab, “Let the young men come forward and have a contest before us.” Joab said, “Let them come forward.”

10_2_15.NKJV: So they arose and went over by number, twelve from Benjamin, followers of Ishbosheth the son of Saul, and twelve from the servants of David.
10_2_15.KJV: Then there arose and went over by number twelve of Benjamin, which pertained to Ishbosheth the son of Saul, and twelve of the servants of David.
10_2_15.ESV: Then they arose and passed over by number, twelve for Benjamin and Ish-bosheth the son of Saul, and twelve of the servants of David.
10_2_15.NASB: So they arose and went over by count, twelve for Benjamin and Ish-bosheth the son of Saul, and twelve of the servants of David.
10_2_15.NLT: So twelve men were chosen to fight from each side — twelve men of Benjamin representing Ishbosheth son of Saul, and twelve representing David.
10_2_15.WEB: Then they arose and went over by number: twelve for Benjamin and for Ishbosheth the son of Saul, and twelve of David’s servants.
10_2_15.CSB17: So they got up and were counted off — twelve for Benjamin and Ish-bosheth son of Saul, and twelve from David’s soldiers.
10_2_15.AMP: So they stood up and went over by number, twelve for Benjamin and Ish-bosheth the son of Saul, and twelve of the servants of David.
10_2_15.MSG: So they lined up for the fight, twelve Benjaminites from the side of Ish-Bosheth son of Saul, and twelve soldiers from David's side.
10_2_15.NIV: So they stood up and were counted off—twelve men for Benjamin and Ish-Bosheth son of Saul, and twelve for David.
10_2_15.NRSVCE: So they came forward and were counted as they passed by, twelve for Benjamin and Ishbaal son of Saul, and twelve of the servants of David.

10_2_16.NKJV: And each one grasped his opponent by the head and thrust his sword in his opponent’s side; so they fell down together. Therefore that place was called the Field of Sharp Swords, which is in Gibeon.
10_2_16.KJV: And they caught every one his fellow by the head, and thrust his sword in his fellow's side; so they fell down together: wherefore that place was called Helkathhazzurim, which is in Gibeon. Helkathhazzurim: that is, The field of strong men
10_2_16.ESV: And each caught his opponent by the head and thrust his sword in his opponent's side, so they fell down together. Therefore that place was called Helkath-hazzurim, which is at Gibeon.
10_2_16.NASB: Each one of them seized his opponent by the head and [thrust] his sword in his opponent's side; so they fell down together. Therefore that place was called Helkath-hazzurim, which is in Gibeon.
10_2_16.NLT: Each one grabbed his opponent by the hair and thrust his sword into the other’s side so that all of them died. So this place at Gibeon has been known ever since as the Field of Swords.
10_2_16.WEB: They each caught his opponent by the head, and thrust his sword in his fellow’s side; so they fell down together: therefore that place in Gibeon was called Helkath Hazzurim.
10_2_16.CSB17: Then each man grabbed his opponent by the head and thrust his sword into his opponent’s side so that they all died together. So this place, which is in Gibeon, is named Field of Blades.
10_2_16.AMP: Each one of them seized his opponent by the head and thrust his sword into his opponents side; so they fell down together. Therefore that place was called Helkath-hazzurim [that is, the Field of Sides], which is in Gibeon.
10_2_16.MSG: The men from each side grabbed their opponents' heads and stabbed them with their daggers. They all fell dead—the whole bunch together. So, they called the place Slaughter Park. It's right there at Gibeon.
10_2_16.NIV: Then each man grabbed his opponent by the head and thrust his dagger into his opponent’s side, and they fell down together. So that place in Gibeon was called Helkath Hazzurim.
10_2_16.NRSVCE: Each grasped his opponent by the head, and thrust his sword in his opponent’s side; so they fell down together. Therefore that place was called Helkath-hazzurim, which is at Gibeon.

10_2_17.NKJV: So there was a very fierce battle that day, and Abner and the men of Israel were beaten before the servants of David.
10_2_17.KJV: And there was a very sore battle that day; and Abner was beaten, and the men of Israel, before the servants of David.
10_2_17.ESV: And the battle was very fierce that day. And Abner and the men of Israel were beaten before the servants of David.
10_2_17.NASB: That day the battle was very severe, and Abner and the men of Israel were beaten before the servants of David.
10_2_17.NLT: A fierce battle followed that day, and Abner and the men of Israel were defeated by the forces of David.
10_2_17.WEB: The battle was very severe that day; and Abner was beaten, and the men of Israel, before David’s servants.
10_2_17.CSB17: The battle that day was extremely fierce, and Abner and the men of Israel were defeated by David’s soldiers.
10_2_17.AMP: There was a very fierce battle that day, and Abner and the men of Israel were beaten before the servants of David.
10_2_17.MSG: The fighting went from bad to worse throughout the day. Abner and the men of Israel were beaten to a pulp by David's men.
10_2_17.NIV: The battle that day was very fierce, and Abner and the men of Israel were defeated by David’s men.
10_2_17.NRSVCE: The battle was very fierce that day; and Abner and the men of Israel were beaten by the servants of David.

10_2_18.NKJV: Now the three sons of Zeruiah were there: Joab and Abishai and Asahel. And Asahel was as fleet of foot as a wild gazelle.
10_2_18.KJV: And there were three sons of Zeruiah there, Joab, and Abishai, and Asahel: and Asahel was as light of foot as a wild roe. of foot: Heb. of his feet as a wild: Heb. as one of the roes that is in the field
10_2_18.ESV: And the three sons of Zeruiah were there, Joab, Abishai, and Asahel. Now Asahel was as swift of foot as a wild gazelle.
10_2_18.NASB: Now the three sons of Zeruiah were there, Joab and Abishai and Asahel; and Asahel [was] [as] swift-footed as one of the gazelles which is in the field.
10_2_18.NLT: Joab, Abishai, and Asahel — the three sons of Zeruiah — were among David’s forces that day. Asahel could run like a gazelle,
10_2_18.WEB: The three sons of Zeruiah were there, Joab, and Abishai, and Asahel: and Asahel was as light of foot as a wild gazelle.
10_2_18.CSB17: The three sons of Zeruiah were there: Joab, Abishai, and Asahel. Asahel was a fast runner, like one of the wild gazelles.
10_2_18.AMP: Three sons of Zeruiah [the half sister of David] were there: Joab, Abishai, and Asahel. Now Asahel was as light and swift-footed as one of the [wild] gazelles in the field.
10_2_18.MSG: The three sons of Zeruiah were present: Joab, Abishai, and Asahel. Asahel, as fast as a wild antelope
10_2_18.NIV: The three sons of Zeruiah were there: Joab, Abishai and Asahel. Now Asahel was as fleet-footed as a wild gazelle.
10_2_18.NRSVCE: The three sons of Zeruiah were there, Joab, Abishai, and Asahel. Now Asahel was as swift of foot as a wild gazelle.

10_2_19.NKJV: So Asahel pursued Abner, and in going he did not turn to the right hand or to the left from following Abner.
10_2_19.KJV: And Asahel pursued after Abner; and in going he turned not to the right hand nor to the left from following Abner. from: Heb. from after Abner
10_2_19.ESV: And Asahel pursued Abner, and as he went, he turned neither to the right hand nor to the left from following Abner.
10_2_19.NASB: Asahel pursued Abner and did not turn to the right or to the left from following Abner.
10_2_19.NLT: and he began chasing Abner. He pursued him relentlessly, not stopping for anything.
10_2_19.WEB: Asahel pursued Abner; and in going he didn’t turn to the right hand or to the left from following Abner.
10_2_19.CSB17: He chased Abner and did not turn to the right or the left in his pursuit of him.
10_2_19.AMP: Asahel pursued Abner and did not turn to the right or to the left as he followed him.
10_2_19.MSG: on the open plain, chased Abner, staying hard on his heels.
10_2_19.NIV: He chased Abner, turning neither to the right nor to the left as he pursued him.
10_2_19.NRSVCE: Asahel pursued Abner, turning neither to the right nor to the left as he followed him.

10_2_20.NKJV: Then Abner looked behind him and said, “Are you Asahel?” He answered, “I am.”
10_2_20.KJV: Then Abner looked behind him, and said, Art thou Asahel? And he answered, I am.
10_2_20.ESV: Then Abner looked behind him and said, “Is it you, Asahel?” And he answered, “It is I.”
10_2_20.NASB: Then Abner looked behind him and said, 'Is that you, Asahel?' And he answered, 'It is I.'
10_2_20.NLT: When Abner looked back and saw him coming, he called out, “Is that you, Asahel?” “Yes, it is,” he replied.
10_2_20.WEB: Then Abner looked behind him, and said, “Is that you, Asahel?” He answered, “It is.”
10_2_20.CSB17: Abner glanced back and said, “Is that you, Asahel?” “Yes it is,” Asahel replied.
10_2_20.AMP: Then Abner looked behind him and said, "Is that you, Asahel?" He answered, "It is I."
10_2_20.MSG: Abner turned and said, "Is that you, Asahel?""It surely is," he said.
10_2_20.NIV: Abner looked behind him and asked, “Is that you, Asahel?”“It is,” he answered.
10_2_20.NRSVCE: Then Abner looked back and said, “Is it you, Asahel?” He answered, “Yes, it is.”

10_2_21.NKJV: And Abner said to him, “Turn aside to your right hand or to your left, and lay hold on one of the young men and take his armor for yourself.” But Asahel would not turn aside from following him.
10_2_21.KJV: And Abner said to him, Turn thee aside to thy right hand or to thy left, and lay thee hold on one of the young men, and take thee his armour. But Asahel would not turn aside from following of him. armour: or, spoil
10_2_21.ESV: Abner said to him, “Turn aside to your right hand or to your left, and seize one of the young men and take his spoil.” But Asahel would not turn aside from following him.
10_2_21.NASB: So Abner said to him, 'Turn to your right or to your left, and take hold of one of the young men for yourself, and take for yourself his spoil.' But Asahel was not willing to turn aside from following him.
10_2_21.NLT: “Go fight someone else!” Abner warned. “Take on one of the younger men, and strip him of his weapons.” But Asahel kept right on chasing Abner.
10_2_21.WEB: Abner said to him, “Turn aside to your right hand or to your left, and grab one of the young men, and take his armor.” But Asahel would not turn aside from following him.
10_2_21.CSB17: Abner said to him, “Turn to your right or left, seize one of the young soldiers, and take whatever you can get from him.” But Asahel would not stop chasing him.
10_2_21.AMP: So Abner said to him, "Turn to your right or to your left, and grab one of the young men and take his armor for yourself." But Asahel was not willing to turn away from pursuing Abner.
10_2_21.MSG: Abner said, "Let up on me. Pick on someone you have a chance of beating and be content with those spoils!" But Asahel wouldn't let up.
10_2_21.NIV: Then Abner said to him, “Turn aside to the right or to the left; take on one of the young men and strip him of his weapons.” But Asahel would not stop chasing him.
10_2_21.NRSVCE: Abner said to him, “Turn to your right or to your left, and seize one of the young men, and take his spoil.” But Asahel would not turn away from following him.

10_2_22.NKJV: So Abner said again to Asahel, “Turn aside from following me. Why should I strike you to the ground? How then could I face your brother Joab?”
10_2_22.KJV: And Abner said again to Asahel, Turn thee aside from following me: wherefore should I smite thee to the ground? how then should I hold up my face to Joab thy brother?
10_2_22.ESV: And Abner said again to Asahel, “Turn aside from following me. Why should I strike you to the ground? How then could I lift up my face to your brother Joab?”
10_2_22.NASB: Abner repeated again to Asahel, 'Turn aside from following me. Why should I strike you to the ground? How then could I lift up my face to your brother Joab?'
10_2_22.NLT: Again Abner shouted to him, “Get away from here! I don’t want to kill you. How could I ever face your brother Joab again?”
10_2_22.WEB: Abner said again to Asahel, “Turn aside from following me. Why should I strike you to the ground? How then could I look Joab your brother in the face?”
10_2_22.CSB17: Once again, Abner warned Asahel, “Stop chasing me. Why should I strike you to the ground? How could I ever look your brother Joab in the face?”
10_2_22.AMP: Abner repeated again, "Turn away from following me. Why should I have to strike you to the ground? How would I be able to face Joab your brother [and look him in the eye]?"
10_2_22.MSG: Abner tried again, "Turn back. Don't force me to kill you. How would I face your brother Joab?"
10_2_22.NIV: Again Abner warned Asahel, “Stop chasing me! Why should I strike you down? How could I look your brother Joab in the face?”
10_2_22.NRSVCE: Abner said again to Asahel, “Turn away from following me; why should I strike you to the ground? How then could I show my face to your brother Joab?”

10_2_23.NKJV: However, he refused to turn aside. Therefore Abner struck him in the stomach with the blunt end of the spear, so that the spear came out of his back; and he fell down there and died on the spot. So it was that as many as came to the place where Asahel fell down and died, stood still.
10_2_23.KJV: Howbeit he refused to turn aside: wherefore Abner with the hinder end of the spear smote him under the fifth rib, that the spear came out behind him; and he fell down there, and died in the same place: and it came to pass, that as many as came to the place where Asahel fell down and died stood still.
10_2_23.ESV: But he refused to turn aside. Therefore Abner struck him in the stomach with the butt of his spear, so that the spear came out at his back. And he fell there and died where he was. And all who came to the place where Asahel had fallen and died, stood still.
10_2_23.NASB: However, he refused to turn aside; therefore Abner struck him in the belly with the butt end of the spear, so that the spear came out at his back. And he fell there and died on the spot. And it came about that all who came to the place where Asahel had fallen and died, stood still.
10_2_23.NLT: But Asahel refused to turn back, so Abner thrust the butt end of his spear through Asahel’s stomach, and the spear came out through his back. He stumbled to the ground and died there. And everyone who came by that spot stopped and stood still when they saw Asahel lying there.
10_2_23.WEB: However he refused to turn aside. Therefore Abner with the back end of the spear struck him in the body, so that the spear came out behind him; and he fell down there, and died in the same place. As many as came to the place where Asahel fell down and died stood still.
10_2_23.CSB17: But Asahel refused to turn away, so Abner hit him in the stomach with the butt of his spear. The spear went through his body, and he fell and died right there. As they all came to the place where Asahel had fallen and died, they stopped,
10_2_23.AMP: But Asahel refused to turn away; so Abner struck him in the abdomen with the butt end of his spear, and the spear came out his back; and he fell there and died on that spot. And it came about that everyone who came to the place where Asahel fell and died, stood still [and reflected].
10_2_23.MSG: When he refused to quit, Abner struck him in the belly with the blunt end of his spear so hard that it came out his back. Asahel fell to the ground and died at once. Everyone who arrived at the spot where Asahel fell and died stood and gaped—Asahel dead!
10_2_23.NIV: But Asahel refused to give up the pursuit; so Abner thrust the butt of his spear into Asahel’s stomach, and the spear came out through his back. He fell there and died on the spot. And every man stopped when he came to the place where Asahel had fallen and died.
10_2_23.NRSVCE: But he refused to turn away. So Abner struck him in the stomach with the butt of his spear, so that the spear came out at his back. He fell there, and died where he lay. And all those who came to the place where Asahel had fallen and died, stood still.

10_2_24.NKJV: Joab and Abishai also pursued Abner. And the sun was going down when they came to the hill of Ammah, which is before Giah by the road to the Wilderness of Gibeon.
10_2_24.KJV: Joab also and Abishai pursued after Abner: and the sun went down when they were come to the hill of Ammah, that lieth before Giah by the way of the wilderness of Gibeon.
10_2_24.ESV: But Joab and Abishai pursued Abner. And as the sun was going down they came to the hill of Ammah, which lies before Giah on the way to the wilderness of Gibeon.
10_2_24.NASB: But Joab and Abishai pursued Abner, and when the sun was going down, they came to the hill of Ammah, which is in front of Giah by the way of the wilderness of Gibeon.
10_2_24.NLT: When Joab and Abishai found out what had happened, they set out after Abner. The sun was just going down as they arrived at the hill of Ammah near Giah, along the road to the wilderness of Gibeon.
10_2_24.WEB: But Joab and Abishai pursued Abner. The sun went down when they had come to the hill of Ammah, that lies before Giah by the way of the wilderness of Gibeon.
10_2_24.CSB17: but Joab and Abishai pursued Abner. By sunset, they had gone as far as the hill of Ammah, which is opposite Giah on the way to the wilderness of Gibeon.
10_2_24.AMP: But Joab and Abishai [Asahel's brothers] pursued Abner. When the sun was going down, they came to the hill of Ammah, which is in front of Giah on the way to the wilderness of Gibeon.
10_2_24.MSG: But Joab and Abishai kept up the chase after Abner. As the sun began to set, they came to the hill of Ammah that faced Giah on the road to the backcountry of Gibeon.
10_2_24.NIV: But Joab and Abishai pursued Abner, and as the sun was setting, they came to the hill of Ammah, near Giah on the way to the wasteland of Gibeon.
10_2_24.NRSVCE: But Joab and Abishai pursued Abner. As the sun was going down they came to the hill of Ammah, which lies before Giah on the way to the wilderness of Gibeon.

10_2_25.NKJV: Now the children of Benjamin gathered together behind Abner and became a unit, and took their stand on top of a hill.
10_2_25.KJV: And the children of Benjamin gathered themselves together after Abner, and became one troop, and stood on the top of an hill.
10_2_25.ESV: And the people of Benjamin gathered themselves together behind Abner and became one group and took their stand on the top of a hill.
10_2_25.NASB: The sons of Benjamin gathered together behind Abner and became one band, and they stood on the top of a certain hill.
10_2_25.NLT: Abner’s troops from the tribe of Benjamin regrouped there at the top of the hill to take a stand.
10_2_25.WEB: The children of Benjamin gathered themselves together after Abner, and became one band, and stood on the top of a hill.
10_2_25.CSB17: The Benjaminites rallied to Abner; they formed a unit and took their stand on top of a hill.
10_2_25.AMP: And the sons of [the tribe of] Benjamin gathered together behind Abner and became one troop, and took their stand on the top of a hill.
10_2_25.MSG: The Benjaminites had taken their stand with Abner there, deployed strategically on a hill.
10_2_25.NIV: Then the men of Benjamin rallied behind Abner. They formed themselves into a group and took their stand on top of a hill.
10_2_25.NRSVCE: The Benjaminites rallied around Abner and formed a single band; they took their stand on the top of a hill.

10_2_26.NKJV: Then Abner called to Joab and said, “Shall the sword devour forever? Do you not know that it will be bitter in the latter end? How long will it be then until you tell the people to return from pursuing their brethren?”
10_2_26.KJV: Then Abner called to Joab, and said, Shall the sword devour for ever? knowest thou not that it will be bitterness in the latter end? how long shall it be then, ere thou bid the people return from following their brethren?
10_2_26.ESV: Then Abner called to Joab, “Shall the sword devour forever? Do you not know that the end will be bitter? How long will it be before you tell your people to turn from the pursuit of their brothers?”
10_2_26.NASB: Then Abner called to Joab and said, 'Shall the sword devour forever? Do you not know that it will be bitter in the end? How long will you refrain from telling the people to turn back from following their brothers?'
10_2_26.NLT: Abner shouted down to Joab, “Must we always be killing each other? Don’t you realize that bitterness is the only result? When will you call off your men from chasing their Israelite brothers?”
10_2_26.WEB: Then Abner called to Joab, and said, “Shall the sword devour forever? Don’t you know that it will be bitterness in the latter end? How long will it be then, before you ask the people to return from following their brothers?”
10_2_26.CSB17: Then Abner called out to Joab: “Must the sword devour forever? Don’t you realize this will only end in bitterness? How long before you tell the troops to stop pursuing their brothers?”
10_2_26.AMP: Then Abner called to Joab, "Must the sword devour forever? Do you not know that it will be bitter in the end? How long [will it be] before you tell the people to stop pursuing their brothers?"
10_2_26.MSG: Abner called out to Joab, "Are we going to keep killing each other till doomsday? Don't you know that nothing but bitterness will come from this? How long before you call off your men from chasing their brothers?"
10_2_26.NIV: Abner called out to Joab, “Must the sword devour forever? Don’t you realize that this will end in bitterness? How long before you order your men to stop pursuing their brothers?”
10_2_26.NRSVCE: Then Abner called to Joab, “Is the sword to keep devouring forever? Do you not know that the end will be bitter? How long will it be before you order your people to turn from the pursuit of their kinsmen?”

10_2_27.NKJV: And Joab said, “As God lives, unless you had spoken, surely then by morning all the people would have given up pursuing their brethren.”
10_2_27.KJV: And Joab said, As God liveth, unless thou hadst spoken, surely then in the morning the people had gone up every one from following his brother. in the: Heb. from the morning gone: or, gone away
10_2_27.ESV: And Joab said, “As God lives, if you had not spoken, surely the men would not have given up the pursuit of their brothers until the morning.”
10_2_27.NASB: Joab said, 'As God lives, if you had not spoken, surely then the people would have gone away in the morning, each from following his brother.'
10_2_27.NLT: Then Joab said, “God only knows what would have happened if you hadn’t spoken, for we would have chased you all night if necessary.”
10_2_27.WEB: Joab said, “As God lives, if you had not spoken, surely then in the morning the people would have gone away, and not each followed his brother.”
10_2_27.CSB17: “As God lives,” Joab replied, “if you had not spoken up, the troops wouldn’t have stopped pursuing their brothers until morning.”
10_2_27.AMP: Joab said, "As God lives, if you had not spoken, then the people certainly would have stopped pursuing their brothers in the morning."
10_2_27.MSG: "As God lives," said Joab, "if you hadn't spoken up, we'd have kept up the chase until morning!"
10_2_27.NIV: Joab answered, “As surely as God lives, if you had not spoken, the men would have continued the pursuit of their brothers until morning.”
10_2_27.NRSVCE: Joab said, “As God lives, if you had not spoken, the people would have continued to pursue their kinsmen, not stopping until morning.”

10_2_28.NKJV: So Joab blew a trumpet; and all the people stood still and did not pursue Israel anymore, nor did they fight anymore.
10_2_28.KJV: So Joab blew a trumpet, and all the people stood still, and pursued after Israel no more, neither fought they any more.
10_2_28.ESV: So Joab blew the trumpet, and all the men stopped and pursued Israel no more, nor did they fight anymore.
10_2_28.NASB: So Joab blew the trumpet; and all the people halted and pursued Israel no longer, nor did they continue to fight anymore.
10_2_28.NLT: So Joab blew the ram’s horn, and his men stopped chasing the troops of Israel.
10_2_28.WEB: So Joab blew the trumpet; and all the people stood still, and pursued Israel no more, and they fought no more.
10_2_28.CSB17: Then Joab blew the ram’s horn, and all the troops stopped; they no longer pursued Israel or continued to fight.
10_2_28.AMP: So Joab blew the trumpet; and all the people halted and no longer pursued Israel, nor did they fight anymore.
10_2_28.MSG: Then he blew the ram's horn trumpet and the whole army of Judah stopped in its tracks. They quit chasing Israel and called off the fighting.
10_2_28.NIV: So Joab blew the trumpet, and all the men came to a halt; they no longer pursued Israel, nor did they fight anymore.
10_2_28.NRSVCE: Joab sounded the trumpet and all the people stopped; they no longer pursued Israel or engaged in battle any further.

10_2_29.NKJV: Then Abner and his men went on all that night through the plain, crossed over the Jordan, and went through all Bithron; and they came to Mahanaim.
10_2_29.KJV: And Abner and his men walked all that night through the plain, and passed over Jordan, and went through all Bithron, and they came to Mahanaim.
10_2_29.ESV: And Abner and his men went all that night through the Arabah. They crossed the Jordan, and marching the whole morning, they came to Mahanaim.
10_2_29.NASB: Abner and his men then went through the Arabah all that night; so they crossed the Jordan, walked all morning, and came to Mahanaim.
10_2_29.NLT: All that night Abner and his men retreated through the Jordan Valley. They crossed the Jordan River, traveling all through the morning, and didn’t stop until they arrived at Mahanaim.
10_2_29.WEB: Abner and his men went all that night through the Arabah; and they passed over the Jordan, and went through all Bithron, and came to Mahanaim.
10_2_29.CSB17: So Abner and his men marched through the Arabah all that night. They crossed the Jordan, marched all morning, and arrived at Mahanaim.
10_2_29.AMP: Then Abner and his men went through the Arabah (desert region) all that night, crossed the Jordan, went through Bithron and walked the whole morning and came to Mahanaim [where Ish-bosheth ruled Israel].
10_2_29.MSG: Abner and his soldiers marched all that night up the Arabah Valley. They crossed the Jordan and, after a long morning's march, arrived at Mahanaim.
10_2_29.NIV: All that night Abner and his men marched through the Arabah. They crossed the Jordan, continued through the whole Bithron and came to Mahanaim.
10_2_29.NRSVCE: Abner and his men traveled all that night through the Arabah; they crossed the Jordan, and, marching the whole forenoon, they came to Mahanaim.

10_2_30.NKJV: So Joab returned from pursuing Abner. And when he had gathered all the people together, there were missing of David’s servants nineteen men and Asahel.
10_2_30.KJV: And Joab returned from following Abner: and when he had gathered all the people together, there lacked of David's servants nineteen men and Asahel.
10_2_30.ESV: Joab returned from the pursuit of Abner. And when he had gathered all the people together, there were missing from David's servants nineteen men besides Asahel.
10_2_30.NASB: Then Joab returned from following Abner; when he had gathered all the people together, nineteen of David's servants besides Asahel were missing.
10_2_30.NLT: Meanwhile, Joab and his men also returned home. When Joab counted his casualties, he discovered that only 19 men were missing in addition to Asahel.
10_2_30.WEB: Joab returned from following Abner; and when he had gathered all the people together, nineteen men of David’s and Asahel were missing.
10_2_30.CSB17: When Joab had turned back from pursuing Abner, he gathered all the troops. In addition to Asahel, nineteen of David’s soldiers were missing,
10_2_30.AMP: Joab returned from pursuing Abner; and when he had gathered all the people together, nineteen of David's servants were missing, besides Asahel.
10_2_30.MSG: After Joab returned from chasing Abner, he took a head count of the army. Nineteen of David's men (besides Asahel) were missing.
10_2_30.NIV: Then Joab returned from pursuing Abner and assembled all his men. Besides Asahel, nineteen of David’s men were found missing.
10_2_30.NRSVCE: Joab returned from the pursuit of Abner; and when he had gathered all the people together, there were missing of David’s servants nineteen men besides Asahel.

10_2_31.NKJV: But the servants of David had struck down, of Benjamin and Abner’s men, three hundred and sixty men who died.
10_2_31.KJV: But the servants of David had smitten of Benjamin, and of Abner's men, so that three hundred and threescore men died.
10_2_31.ESV: But the servants of David had struck down of Benjamin 360 of Abner's men.
10_2_31.NASB: But the servants of David had struck down many of Benjamin and Abner's men, [so that] three hundred and sixty men died.
10_2_31.NLT: But 360 of Abner’s men had been killed, all from the tribe of Benjamin.
10_2_31.WEB: But David’s servants had struck Benjamin and of Abner’s men so that three hundred sixty men died.
10_2_31.CSB17: but they had killed 360 of the Benjaminites and Abner’s men.
10_2_31.AMP: But the servants of David had struck down many of Benjamin and Abner's men; so that three hundred and sixty men died.
10_2_31.MSG: David's men had cut down three hundred and sixty of Abner's men, all Benjaminites—all dead.
10_2_31.NIV: But David’s men had killed three hundred and sixty Benjamites who were with Abner.
10_2_31.NRSVCE: But the servants of David had killed of Benjamin three hundred sixty of Abner’s men.

10_2_32.NKJV: Then they took up Asahel and buried him in his father’s tomb, which was in Bethlehem. And Joab and his men went all night, and they came to Hebron at daybreak.
10_2_32.KJV: And they took up Asahel, and buried him in the sepulchre of his father, which was in Bethlehem. And Joab and his men went all night, and they came to Hebron at break of day.
10_2_32.ESV: And they took up Asahel and buried him in the tomb of his father, which was at Bethlehem. And Joab and his men marched all night, and the day broke upon them at Hebron.
10_2_32.NASB: And they took up Asahel and buried him in his father's tomb which was in Bethlehem. Then Joab and his men went all night until the day dawned at Hebron.
10_2_32.NLT: Joab and his men took Asahel’s body to Bethlehem and buried him there in his father’s tomb. Then they traveled all night and reached Hebron at daybreak.
10_2_32.WEB: They took up Asahel, and buried him in the tomb of his father, which was in Bethlehem. Joab and his men went all night, and the day broke on them at Hebron.
10_2_32.CSB17: Afterward, they carried Asahel to his father’s tomb in Bethlehem and buried him. Then Joab and his men marched all night and reached Hebron at dawn.
10_2_32.AMP: And they picked up [the body of] Asahel and buried him in the tomb of his father in Bethlehem. Then Joab and his men walked all night and they arrived in Hebron at daybreak.
10_2_32.MSG: They brought Asahel and buried him in the family tomb in Bethlehem. Joab and his men then marched all night, arriving in Hebron as the dawn broke.
10_2_32.NIV: They took Asahel and buried him in his father’s tomb at Bethlehem. Then Joab and his men marched all night and arrived at Hebron by daybreak.
10_2_32.NRSVCE: They took up Asahel and buried him in the tomb of his father, which was at Bethlehem. Joab and his men marched all night, and the day broke upon them at Hebron.

10_3_1.NKJV: Now there was a long war between the house of Saul and the house of David. But David grew stronger and stronger, and the house of Saul grew weaker and weaker.
10_3_1.KJV: Now there was long war between the house of Saul and the house of David: but David waxed stronger and stronger, and the house of Saul waxed weaker and weaker.
10_3_1.ESV: There was a long war between the house of Saul and the house of David. And David grew stronger and stronger, while the house of Saul became weaker and weaker.
10_3_1.NASB: Now there was a long war between the house of Saul and the house of David; and David grew steadily stronger, but the house of Saul grew weaker continually.
10_3_1.NLT: That was the beginning of a long war between those who were loyal to Saul and those loyal to David. As time passed David became stronger and stronger, while Saul’s dynasty became weaker and weaker.
10_3_1.WEB: Now there was long war between Saul’s house and David’s house. David grew stronger and stronger, but Saul’s house grew weaker and weaker.
10_3_1.CSB17: During the long war between the house of Saul and the house of David, David was growing stronger and the house of Saul was becoming weaker.
10_3_1.AMP: There was a long war between the house of Saul and the house of David; but David grew steadily stronger, while the house of Saul grew weaker and weaker [to the point of being powerless].
10_3_1.MSG: The war between the house of Saul and the house of David dragged on and on. The longer it went on the stronger David became, with the house of Saul getting weaker.
10_3_1.NIV: The war between the house of Saul and the house of David lasted a long time. David grew stronger and stronger, while the house of Saul grew weaker and weaker.
10_3_1.NRSVCE: There was a long war between the house of Saul and the house of David; David grew stronger and stronger, while the house of Saul became weaker and weaker.

10_3_6.NKJV: Now it was so, while there was war between the house of Saul and the house of David, that Abner was strengthening his hold on the house of Saul.
10_3_6.KJV: And it came to pass, while there was war between the house of Saul and the house of David, that Abner made himself strong for the house of Saul.
10_3_6.ESV: While there was war between the house of Saul and the house of David, Abner was making himself strong in the house of Saul.
10_3_6.NASB: It came about while there was war between the house of Saul and the house of David that Abner was making himself strong in the house of Saul.
10_3_6.NLT: As the war between the house of Saul and the house of David went on, Abner became a powerful leader among those loyal to Saul.
10_3_6.WEB: While there was war between Saul’s house and David’s house, Abner made himself strong in Saul’s house.

10_3_6.AMP: Now while war continued between the houses of Saul and David, Abner was proving himself strong in the house of Saul.
10_3_6.MSG: Abner took advantage of the continuing war between the house of Saul and the house of David to gain power for himself.

10_3_6.NRSVCE: While there was war between the house of Saul and the house of David, Abner was making himself strong in the house of Saul.

10_3_8.NKJV: Then Abner became very angry at the words of Ishbosheth, and said, “Am I a dog’s head that belongs to Judah? Today I show loyalty to the house of Saul your father, to his brothers, and to his friends, and have not delivered you into the hand of David; and you charge me today with a fault concerning this woman?
10_3_8.KJV: Then was Abner very wroth for the words of Ishbosheth, and said, Am I a dog's head, which against Judah do shew kindness this day unto the house of Saul thy father, to his brethren, and to his friends, and have not delivered thee into the hand of David, that thou chargest me to day with a fault concerning this woman?
10_3_8.ESV: Then Abner was very angry over the words of Ish-bosheth and said, “Am I a dog's head of Judah? To this day I keep showing steadfast love to the house of Saul your father, to his brothers, and to his friends, and have not given you into the hand of David. And yet you charge me today with a fault concerning a woman.
10_3_8.NASB: Then Abner was very angry over the words of Ish-bosheth and said, 'Am I a dog's head that belongs to Judah? Today I show kindness to the house of Saul your father, to his brothers and to his friends, and have not delivered you into the hands of David; and yet today you charge me with a guilt concerning the woman.
10_3_8.NLT: Abner was furious. “Am I some Judean dog to be kicked around like this?” he shouted. “After all I have done for your father, Saul, and his family and friends by not handing you over to David, is this my reward — that you find fault with me about this woman?
10_3_8.WEB: Then Abner was very angry about Ishbosheth’s words, and said, “Am I a dog’s head that belongs to Judah? Today I show kindness to Saul’s house your father, to his brothers, and to his friends, and have not delivered you into the hand of David; and yet you charge me today with a fault concerning this woman!
10_3_8.CSB17: Abner was very angry about Ish-bosheth’s accusation. “Am I a dog’s head who belongs to Judah?” he asked. “All this time I’ve been loyal to the family of your father Saul, to his brothers, and to his friends and haven’t betrayed you to David, but now you accuse me of wrongdoing with this woman!
10_3_8.AMP: Then Abner was very angry at the words of Ish-bosheth, and he said, "Am I a dog's head [a despicable traitor] that belongs to Judah? Today I show loyalty and kindness to the house of Saul your father, to his brothers and his friends, by not having you handed over to David; and yet you charge me today with guilt concerning this woman.
10_3_8.MSG: Abner lost his temper with Ish-Bosheth, "Treat me like a dog, will you! Is this the thanks I get for sticking by the house of your father, Saul, and all his family and friends? I personally saved you from certain capture by David, and you make an issue out of my going to bed with a woman!
10_3_8.NIV: Abner was very angry because of what Ish-Bosheth said and he answered, “Am I a dog’s head—on Judah’s side? This very day I am loyal to the house of your father Saul and to his family and friends. I haven’t handed you over to David. Yet now you accuse me of an offense involving this woman!
10_3_8.NRSVCE: The words of Ishbaal made Abner very angry; he said, “Am I a dog’s head for Judah? Today I keep showing loyalty to the house of your father Saul, to his brothers, and to his friends, and have not given you into the hand of David; and yet you charge me now with a crime concerning this woman.

10_3_9.NRSVCE: So may God do to Abner and so may he add to it! For just what the LORD has sworn to David, that will I accomplish for him,

10_3_10.NKJV: to transfer the kingdom from the house of Saul, and set up the throne of David over Israel and over Judah, from Dan to Beersheba.”

10_3_12.NKJV: Then Abner sent messengers on his behalf to David, saying, “Whose is the land?” saying also, “Make your covenant with me, and indeed my hand shall be with you to bring all Israel to you.”

10_3_21.NLT: Then Abner said to David, “Let me go and call an assembly of all Israel to support my lord the king. They will make a covenant with you to make you their king, and you will rule over everything your heart desires.” So David sent Abner safely on his way.

10_3_21.CSB17: Abner said to David, “Let me now go and I will gather all Israel to my lord the king. They will make a covenant with you, and you will reign over all you desire.” So David dismissed Abner, and he went in peace.
10_3_21.AMP: Abner said to David, "Let me stand up and go, and gather all Israel to my lord the king, so that they may make a covenant (treaty) with you, and that you may reign over all that your soul desires." So David sent Abner away, and he went in peace.
10_3_21.MSG: Abner then said, "I'm ready. Let me go now to rally everyone in Israel for my master, the king. They'll make a treaty with you, authorizing you to rule them however you see fit." Abner was sent off with David's blessing.
10_3_21.NIV: Then Abner said to David, “Let me go at once and assemble all Israel for my lord the king, so that they may make a compact with you, and that you may rule over all that your heart desires.” So David sent Abner away, and he went in peace.

10_3_22.NKJV: At that moment the servants of David and Joab came from a raid and brought much spoil with them. But Abner was not with David in Hebron, for he had sent him away, and he had gone in peace.

10_3_22.NASB: And behold, the servants of David and Joab came from a raid and brought much spoil with them; but Abner was not with David in Hebron, for he had sent him away, and he had gone in peace.
10_3_22.NLT: But just after David had sent Abner away in safety, Joab and some of David’s troops returned from a raid, bringing much plunder with them.
10_3_22.WEB: Behold, David’s servants and Joab came from a raid, and brought in a great plunder with them; but Abner was not with David in Hebron; for he had sent him away, and he had gone in peace.
10_3_22.CSB17: Just then David’s soldiers and Joab returned from a raid and brought a large amount of plundered goods with them. Abner was not with David in Hebron because David had dismissed him, and he had gone in peace.
10_3_22.AMP: Then the servants of David came with Joab from a raid and brought a great quantity of spoil with them; but Abner was not with David at Hebron, because David had sent him away, and he had gone in peace.

10_3_23.NKJV: When Joab and all the troops that were with him had come, they told Joab, saying, “Abner the son of Ner came to the king, and he sent him away, and he has gone in peace.”
10_3_23.KJV: When Joab and all the host that was with him were come, they told Joab, saying, Abner the son of Ner came to the king, and he hath sent him away, and he is gone in peace.
10_3_23.ESV: When Joab and all the army that was with him came, it was told Joab, “Abner the son of Ner came to the king, and he has let him go, and he has gone in peace.”
10_3_23.NASB: When Joab and all the army that was with him arrived, they told Joab, saying, 'Abner the son of Ner came to the king, and he has sent him away, and he has gone in peace.'
10_3_23.NLT: When Joab arrived, he was told that Abner had just been there visiting the king and had been sent away in safety.
10_3_23.WEB: When Joab and all the army who was with him had come, they told Joab, “Abner the son of Ner came to the king, and he has sent him away, and he has gone in peace.”
10_3_23.CSB17: When Joab and his whole army arrived, Joab was informed, “Abner son of Ner came to see the king, the king dismissed him, and he went in peace.”
10_3_23.AMP: When Joab and all the army that was with him arrived, they told Joab, "Abner the son of Ner came to the king, and he has sent him away, and he has gone in peace."
10_3_23.MSG: As Joab and his raiding party arrived, they were told that Abner the son of Ner had been there with David and had been sent off with David's blessing.

10_3_23.NRSVCE: When Joab and all the army that was with him came, it was told Joab, “Abner son of Ner came to the king, and he has dismissed him, and he has gone away in peace.”

10_3_24.NKJV: Then Joab came to the king and said, “What have you done? Look, Abner came to you; why is it that you sent him away, and he has already gone?

10_3_24.NASB: Then Joab came to the king and said, 'What have you done? Behold, Abner came to you; why then have you sent him away and he is already gone?
10_3_24.NLT: Joab rushed to the king and demanded, “What have you done? What do you mean by letting Abner get away?
10_3_24.WEB: Then Joab came to the king, and said, “What have you done? Behold, Abner came to you. Why is it that you have sent him away, and he is already gone?
10_3_24.CSB17: Joab went to the king and said, “What have you done? Look here, Abner came to you. Why did you dismiss him? Now he’s getting away.

10_3_24.MSG: Joab went straight to the king: "What's this you've done? Abner shows up, and you let him walk away scot-free?
10_3_24.NIV: So Joab went to the king and said, “What have you done? Look, Abner came to you. Why did you let him go? Now he is gone!
10_3_24.NRSVCE: Then Joab went to the king and said, “What have you done? Abner came to you; why did you dismiss him, so that he got away?

10_3_25.NKJV: Surely you realize that Abner the son of Ner came to deceive you, to know your going out and your coming in, and to know all that you are doing.”
10_3_25.KJV: Thou knowest Abner the son of Ner, that he came to deceive thee, and to know thy going out and thy coming in , and to know all that thou doest.
10_3_25.ESV: You know that Abner the son of Ner came to deceive you and to know your going out and your coming in, and to know all that you are doing.”
10_3_25.NASB: 'You know Abner the son of Ner, that he came to deceive you and to learn of your going out and coming in and to find out all that you are doing.'
10_3_25.NLT: You know perfectly well that he came to spy on you and find out everything you’re doing!”
10_3_25.WEB: You know Abner the son of Ner. He came to deceive you, and to know your going out and your coming in, and to know all that you do.”
10_3_25.CSB17: You know that Abner son of Ner came to deceive you and to find out about your military activities and everything you’re doing.”
10_3_25.AMP: You know Abner the son of Ner, that he [only] came to deceive you [with flattering words] and to learn of your going out and coming in, and to find out what you are doing."
10_3_25.MSG: You know Abner son of Ner better than that. This was no friendly visit. He was here to spy on you, figure out your comings and goings, find out what you're up to."
10_3_25.NIV: You know Abner son of Ner; he came to deceive you and observe your movements and find out everything you are doing.”
10_3_25.NRSVCE: You know that Abner son of Ner came to deceive you, and to learn your comings and goings and to learn all that you are doing.”

10_3_26.NKJV: And when Joab had gone from David’s presence, he sent messengers after Abner, who brought him back from the well of Sirah. But David did not know it.

10_3_26.ESV: When Joab came out from David's presence, he sent messengers after Abner, and they brought him back from the cistern of Sirah. But David did not know about it.
10_3_26.NASB: When Joab came out from David, he sent messengers after Abner, and they brought him back from the well of Sirah; but David did not know [it].
10_3_26.NLT: Joab then left David and sent messengers to catch up with Abner, asking him to return. They found him at the well of Sirah and brought him back, though David knew nothing about it.
10_3_26.WEB: When Joab had come out from David, he sent messengers after Abner, and they brought him back from the well of Sirah; but David didn’t know it.
10_3_26.CSB17: Then Joab left David and sent messengers after Abner. They brought him back from the well of Sirah, but David was unaware of it.
10_3_26.AMP: When Joab left David, he sent messengers after Abner, and they brought him back from the well of Sirah; but David knew nothing [about Joab's action].
10_3_26.MSG: Joab left David and went into action. He sent messengers after Abner; they caught up with him at the well at Sirah and brought him back. David knew nothing of all this.
10_3_26.NIV: Joab then left David and sent messengers after Abner, and they brought him back from the well of Sirah. But David did not know it.
10_3_26.NRSVCE: When Joab came out from David’s presence, he sent messengers after Abner, and they brought him back from the cistern of Sirah; but David did not know about it.

10_3_27.NKJV: Now when Abner had returned to Hebron, Joab took him aside in the gate to speak with him privately, and there stabbed him in the stomach, so that he died for the blood of Asahel his brother.

10_3_27.CSB17: When Abner returned to Hebron, Joab pulled him aside to the middle of the city gate, as if to speak to him privately, and there Joab stabbed him in the stomach. So Abner died in revenge for the death of Asahel, Joab’s brother.

10_3_28.NKJV: Afterward, when David heard it, he said, “My kingdom and I are guiltless before the LORD forever of the blood of Abner the son of Ner.

10_3_28.NLT: When David heard about it, he declared, “I vow by the LORD that I and my kingdom are forever innocent of this crime against Abner son of Ner.

10_3_29.NKJV: Let it rest on the head of Joab and on all his father’s house; and let there never fail to be in the house of Joab one who has a discharge or is a leper, who leans on a staff or falls by the sword, or who lacks bread.”
10_3_29.KJV: Let it rest on the head of Joab, and on all his father's house; and let there not fail from the house of Joab one that hath an issue, or that is a leper, or that leaneth on a staff, or that falleth on the sword, or that lacketh bread. fail: Heb. be cut off
10_3_29.ESV: May it fall upon the head of Joab and upon all his father's house, and may the house of Joab never be without one who has a discharge or who is leprous or who holds a spindle or who falls by the sword or who lacks bread!”
10_3_29.NASB: 'May it fall on the head of Joab and on all his father's house; and may there not fail from the house of Joab one who has a discharge, or who is a leper, or who takes hold of a distaff, or who falls by the sword, or who lacks bread.'
10_3_29.NLT: Joab and his family are the guilty ones. May the family of Joab be cursed in every generation with a man who has open sores or leprosy or who walks on crutches or dies by the sword or begs for food!”
10_3_29.WEB: Let it fall on the head of Joab, and on all his father’s house. Let there not fail from the house of Joab one who has an issue, or who is a leper, or who leans on a staff, or who falls by the sword, or who lacks bread.”
10_3_29.CSB17: May it hang over Joab’s head and his father’s whole family, and may the house of Joab never be without someone who has a discharge or a skin disease, or a man who can only work a spindle, or someone who falls by the sword or starves.”
10_3_29.AMP: Let the guilt fall on the head of Joab and on all his father's house (family); and may there never disappear from the house of Joab one who suffers with a discharge or one who is a leper or one who walks with a crutch [being unfit for war], or one who falls by the sword, or one who lacks food."
10_3_29.MSG: Joab and his entire family will always be under the curse of this bloodguilt. May they forever be victims of crippling diseases, violence, and famine."
10_3_29.NIV: May his blood fall upon the head of Joab and upon all his father’s house! May Joab’s house never be without someone who has a running sore or leprosy or who leans on a crutch or who falls by the sword or who lacks food.”
10_3_29.NRSVCE: May the guilt fall on the head of Joab, and on all his father’s house; and may the house of Joab never be without one who has a discharge, or who is leprous, or who holds a spindle, or who falls by the sword, or who lacks food!”

10_3_31.ESV: Then David said to Joab and to all the people who were with him, “Tear your clothes and put on sackcloth and mourn before Abner.” And King David followed the bier.
10_3_31.NASB: Then David said to Joab and to all the people who were with him, 'Tear your clothes and gird on sackcloth and lament before Abner.' And King David walked behind the bier.

10_3_32.NKJV: So they buried Abner in Hebron; and the king lifted up his voice and wept at the grave of Abner, and all the people wept.
10_3_32.KJV: And they buried Abner in Hebron: and the king lifted up his voice, and wept at the grave of Abner; and all the people wept.
10_3_32.ESV: They buried Abner at Hebron. And the king lifted up his voice and wept at the grave of Abner, and all the people wept.
10_3_32.NASB: Thus they buried Abner in Hebron; and the king lifted up his voice and wept at the grave of Abner, and all the people wept.
10_3_32.NLT: They buried Abner in Hebron, and the king and all the people wept at his graveside.
10_3_32.WEB: They buried Abner in Hebron; and the king lifted up his voice, and wept at Abner’s grave; and all the people wept.
10_3_32.CSB17: When they buried Abner in Hebron, the king wept aloud at Abner’s tomb. All the people wept,
10_3_32.AMP: They buried Abner in Hebron; and the king raised his voice and wept at the grave of Abner, and all the people wept.

10_3_33.NLT: Then the king sang this funeral song for Abner: “Should Abner have died as fools die?
10_3_33.WEB: The king lamented for Abner, and said, “Should Abner die as a fool dies?
10_3_33.CSB17: and the king sang a lament for Abner: Should Abner die as a fool dies?
