10_8_1.NKJV: After this it came to pass that David attacked the Philistines and subdued them. And David took Metheg Ammah from the hand of the Philistines.
10_8_1.KJV: And after this it came to pass, that David smote the Philistines, and subdued them: and David took Methegammah out of the hand of the Philistines. Methegammah: or, the bridle of Ammah
10_8_1.ESV: After this David defeated the Philistines and subdued them, and David took Metheg-ammah out of the hand of the Philistines.
10_8_1.NASB: Now after this it came about that David defeated the Philistines and subdued them; and David took control of the chief city from the hand of the Philistines.
10_8_1.NLT: After this, David defeated and subdued the Philistines by conquering Gath, their largest town.
10_8_1.WEB: After this, David struck the Philistines and subdued them; and David took the bridle of the mother city out of the hand of the Philistines.
10_8_1.CSB17: After this, David defeated the Philistines, subdued them, and took Metheg-ammah from Philistine control.
10_8_1.AMP: Now it came about after this that David defeated the Philistines and subdued (humbled) them, and he took control of Metheg-ammah [the main city] from the hand of the Philistines.
10_8_1.MSG: In the days that followed, David struck hard at the Philistines—brought them to their knees and took control of the countryside.
10_8_1.NIV: David’s VictoriesIn the course of time, David defeated the Philistines and subdued them, and he took Metheg Ammah from the control of the Philistines.
10_8_1.NRSVCE: Some time afterward, David attacked the Philistines and subdued them; David took Metheg-ammah out of the hand of the Philistines.

10_8_2.NKJV: Then he defeated Moab. Forcing them down to the ground, he measured them off with a line. With two lines he measured off those to be put to death, and with one full line those to be kept alive. So the Moabites became David’s servants, and brought tribute.
10_8_2.KJV: And he smote Moab, and measured them with a line, casting them down to the ground; even with two lines measured he to put to death, and with one full line to keep alive. And so the Moabites became David's servants, and brought gifts.
10_8_2.ESV: And he defeated Moab and he measured them with a line, making them lie down on the ground. Two lines he measured to be put to death, and one full line to be spared. And the Moabites became servants to David and brought tribute.
10_8_2.NASB: He defeated Moab, and measured them with the line, making them lie down on the ground; and he measured two lines to put to death and one full line to keep alive. And the Moabites became servants to David, bringing tribute.
10_8_2.NLT: David also conquered the land of Moab. He made the people lie down on the ground in a row, and he measured them off in groups with a length of rope. He measured off two groups to be executed for every one group to be spared. The Moabites who were spared became David’s subjects and paid him tribute money.
10_8_2.WEB: He struck Moab, and measured them with the line, making them to lie down on the ground; and he measured two lines to put to death, and one full line to keep alive. The Moabites became servants to David, and brought tribute.
10_8_2.CSB17: He also defeated the Moabites, and after making them lie down on the ground, he measured them off with a cord. He measured every two cord lengths of those to be put to death and one full length of those to be kept alive. So the Moabites became David’s subjects and brought tribute.
10_8_2.AMP: He defeated Moab, and measured them with a length of rope, making them lie down on the ground; he measured two lengths to [choose those to] put to death, and one full length to [choose those to] be kept alive. And the [surviving] Moabites became servants to David, bringing tribute.
10_8_2.MSG: He also fought and defeated Moab. He chose two-thirds of them randomly and executed them. The other third he spared. So the Moabites fell under David's rule and were forced to bring tribute.
10_8_2.NIV: David also defeated the Moabites. He made them lie down on the ground and measured them off with a length of cord. Every two lengths of them were put to death, and the third length was allowed to live. So the Moabites became subject to David and brought tribute.
10_8_2.NRSVCE: He also defeated the Moabites and, making them lie down on the ground, measured them off with a cord; he measured two lengths of cord for those who were to be put to death, and one length for those who were to be spared. And the Moabites became servants to David and brought tribute.

10_8_3.NKJV: David also defeated Hadadezer the son of Rehob, king of Zobah, as he went to recover his territory at the River Euphrates.
10_8_3.KJV: David smote also Hadadezer, the son of Rehob, king of Zobah, as he went to recover his border at the river Euphrates. Hadadezer: or, Hadarezer
10_8_3.ESV: David also defeated Hadadezer the son of Rehob, king of Zobah, as he went to restore his power at the river Euphrates.
10_8_3.NASB: Then David defeated Hadadezer, the son of Rehob king of Zobah, as he went to restore his rule at the River.
10_8_3.NLT: David also destroyed the forces of Hadadezer son of Rehob, king of Zobah, when Hadadezer marched out to strengthen his control along the Euphrates River.
10_8_3.WEB: David struck also Hadadezer the son of Rehob, king of Zobah, as he went to recover his dominion at the River.
10_8_3.CSB17: David also defeated Hadadezer son of Rehob, king of Zobah, when he went to restore his control at the Euphrates River.
10_8_3.AMP: Then David defeated Hadadezer the son of Rehob, king of Zobah, as he went to restore his power at the River [Euphrates].
10_8_3.MSG: On his way to restore his sovereignty at the River Euphrates, David next defeated Hadadezer son of Rehob the king of Zobah.
10_8_3.NIV: Moreover, David fought Hadadezer son of Rehob, king of Zobah, when he went to restore his control along the Euphrates River.
10_8_3.NRSVCE: David also struck down King Hadadezer son of Rehob of Zobah, as he went to restore his monument at the river Euphrates.

10_8_4.NKJV: David took from him one thousand chariots, seven hundred horsemen, and twenty thousand foot soldiers. Also David hamstrung all the chariot horses, except that he spared enough of them for one hundred chariots.
10_8_4.KJV: And David took from him a thousand chariots, and seven hundred horsemen, and twenty thousand footmen : and David houghed all the chariot horses, but reserved of them for an hundred chariots. from: or, of his. chariots: as 1.Chr.18.4
10_8_4.ESV: And David took from him 1,700 horsemen, and 20,000 foot soldiers. And David hamstrung all the chariot horses but left enough for 100 chariots.
10_8_4.NASB: David captured from him 1,700 horsemen and 20,000 foot soldiers; and David hamstrung the chariot horses, but reserved [enough] of them for 100 chariots.
10_8_4.NLT: David captured 1,000 chariots, 7,000 charioteers, and 20,000 foot soldiers. He crippled all the chariot horses except enough for 100 chariots.
10_8_4.WEB: David took from him one thousand seven hundred horsemen and twenty thousand footmen. David hamstrung all the chariot horses, but reserved of them for one hundred chariots.
10_8_4.CSB17: David captured seventeen hundred horsemen and twenty thousand foot soldiers from him, and he hamstrung all the horses and kept a hundred chariots.
10_8_4.AMP: David captured from him 1,700 horsemen and 20,000 foot soldiers. David also hamstrung all the chariot horses (making them lame), but reserved enough of them for a hundred chariots.
10_8_4.MSG: He captured from him a thousand chariots, seven thousand cavalry, and twenty thousand infantry. He hamstrung all the chariot horses, but saved back a hundred.
10_8_4.NIV: David captured a thousand of his chariots, seven thousand charioteers and twenty thousand foot soldiers. He hamstrung all but a hundred of the chariot horses.
10_8_4.NRSVCE: David took from him one thousand seven hundred horsemen, and twenty thousand foot soldiers. David hamstrung all the chariot horses, but left enough for a hundred chariots.

10_8_5.NKJV: When the Syrians of Damascus came to help Hadadezer king of Zobah, David killed twenty-two thousand of the Syrians.
10_8_5.KJV: And when the Syrians of Damascus came to succour Hadadezer king of Zobah, David slew of the Syrians two and twenty thousand men.
10_8_5.ESV: And when the Syrians of Damascus came to help Hadadezer king of Zobah, David struck down 22,000 men of the Syrians.
10_8_5.NASB: When the Arameans of Damascus came to help Hadadezer, king of Zobah, David killed 22,000 Arameans.
10_8_5.NLT: When Arameans from Damascus arrived to help King Hadadezer, David killed 22,000 of them.
10_8_5.WEB: When the Syrians of Damascus came to help Hadadezer king of Zobah, David struck twenty two thousand men of the Syrians.
10_8_5.CSB17: When the Arameans of Damascus came to assist King Hadadezer of Zobah, David struck down twenty-two thousand Aramean men.
10_8_5.AMP: When the Arameans (Syrians) of Damascus came to help Hadadezer, king of Zobah, David struck down 22,000 Arameans.
10_8_5.MSG: When the Arameans from Damascus came to the aid of Hadadezer king of Zobah, David killed twenty-two thousand of them.
10_8_5.NIV: When the Arameans of Damascus came to help Hadadezer king of Zobah, David struck down twenty-two thousand of them.
10_8_5.NRSVCE: When the Arameans of Damascus came to help King Hadadezer of Zobah, David killed twenty-two thousand men of the Arameans.

10_8_6.NKJV: Then David put garrisons in Syria of Damascus; and the Syrians became David’s servants, and brought tribute. So the LORD preserved David wherever he went.
10_8_6.KJV: Then David put garrisons in Syria of Damascus: and the Syrians became servants to David, and brought gifts. And the LORD preserved David whithersoever he went.
10_8_6.ESV: Then David put garrisons in Aram of Damascus, and the Syrians became servants to David and brought tribute. And the LORD gave victory to David wherever he went.
10_8_6.NASB: Then David put garrisons among the Arameans of Damascus, and the Arameans became servants to David, bringing tribute. And the LORD helped David wherever he went.
10_8_6.NLT: Then he placed several army garrisons in Damascus, the Aramean capital, and the Arameans became David’s subjects and paid him tribute money. So the LORD made David victorious wherever he went.
10_8_6.WEB: Then David put garrisons in Syria of Damascus; and the Syrians became servants to David, and brought tribute. Yahweh gave victory to David wherever he went.
10_8_6.CSB17: Then he placed garrisons in Aram of Damascus, and the Arameans became David’s subjects and brought tribute. The LORD made David victorious wherever he went.
10_8_6.AMP: Then David put garrisons among the Arameans in Damascus, and the Arameans became his servants and brought tribute. The LORD helped David wherever he went.
10_8_6.MSG: David set up a puppet government in Aram-Damascus. The Arameans became subjects of David and were forced to bring tribute. God gave victory to David wherever he marched.
10_8_6.NIV: He put garrisons in the Aramean kingdom of Damascus, and the Arameans became subject to him and brought tribute. The Lord gave David victory wherever he went.
10_8_6.NRSVCE: Then David put garrisons among the Arameans of Damascus; and the Arameans became servants to David and brought tribute. The LORD gave victory to David wherever he went.

10_8_7.NKJV: And David took the shields of gold that had belonged to the servants of Hadadezer, and brought them to Jerusalem.
10_8_7.KJV: And David took the shields of gold that were on the servants of Hadadezer, and brought them to Jerusalem.
10_8_7.ESV: And David took the shields of gold that were carried by the servants of Hadadezer and brought them to Jerusalem.
10_8_7.NASB: David took the shields of gold which were carried by the servants of Hadadezer and brought them to Jerusalem.
10_8_7.NLT: David brought the gold shields of Hadadezer’s officers to Jerusalem,
10_8_7.WEB: David took the shields of gold that were on the servants of Hadadezer, and brought them to Jerusalem.
10_8_7.CSB17: David took the gold shields of Hadadezer’s officers and brought them to Jerusalem.
10_8_7.AMP: David took the shields of gold that were carried by the servants of Hadadezer, and brought them to Jerusalem.
10_8_7.MSG: David plundered the gold shields that belonged to the servants of Hadadezer and brought them to Jerusalem.
10_8_7.NIV: David took the gold shields that belonged to the officers of Hadadezer and brought them to Jerusalem.
10_8_7.NRSVCE: David took the gold shields that were carried by the servants of Hadadezer, and brought them to Jerusalem.

10_8_8.NKJV: Also from Betah and from Berothai, cities of Hadadezer, King David took a large amount of bronze.
10_8_8.KJV: And from Betah, and from Berothai, cities of Hadadezer, king David took exceeding much brass.
10_8_8.ESV: And from Betah and from Berothai, cities of Hadadezer, King David took very much bronze.
10_8_8.NASB: From Betah and from Berothai, cities of Hadadezer, King David took a very large amount of bronze.
10_8_8.NLT: along with a large amount of bronze from Hadadezer’s towns of Tebah and Berothai.
10_8_8.WEB: From Betah and from Berothai, cities of Hadadezer, king David took a great quantity of brass.
10_8_8.CSB17: King David also took huge quantities of bronze from Betah and Berothai, Hadadezer’s cities.
10_8_8.AMP: And from Betah and Berothai, cities of Hadadezer, King David took an immense quantity of bronze.
10_8_8.MSG: He also looted a great quantity of bronze from Tebah and Berothai, cities of Hadadezer.
10_8_8.NIV: From Tebah and Berothai, towns that belonged to Hadadezer, King David took a great quantity of bronze.
10_8_8.NRSVCE: From Betah and from Berothai, towns of Hadadezer, King David took a great amount of bronze.

10_8_9.NKJV: When Toi king of Hamath heard that David had defeated all the army of Hadadezer,
10_8_9.KJV: When Toi king of Hamath heard that David had smitten all the host of Hadadezer,
10_8_9.ESV: When Toi king of Hamath heard that David had defeated the whole army of Hadadezer,
10_8_9.NASB: Now when Toi king of Hamath heard that David had defeated all the army of Hadadezer,
10_8_9.NLT: When King Toi of Hamath heard that David had destroyed the entire army of Hadadezer,
10_8_9.WEB: When Toi king of Hamath heard that David had struck all the army of Hadadezer,
10_8_9.CSB17: When King Toi of Hamath heard that David had defeated the entire army of Hadadezer,
10_8_9.AMP: When Toi king of Hamath heard about David's defeat of all the forces of Hadadezer,
10_8_9.MSG: Toi, king of Hamath, heard that David had struck down the entire army of Hadadezer.
10_8_9.NIV: When Tou king of Hamath heard that David had defeated the entire army of Hadadezer,
10_8_9.NRSVCE: When King Toi of Hamath heard that David had defeated the whole army of Hadadezer,

10_8_10.NKJV: then Toi sent Joram his son to King David, to greet him and bless him, because he had fought against Hadadezer and defeated him (for Hadadezer had been at war with Toi); and Joram brought with him articles of silver, articles of gold, and articles of bronze.
10_8_10.KJV: Then Toi sent Joram his son unto king David, to salute him, and to bless him, because he had fought against Hadadezer, and smitten him: for Hadadezer had wars with Toi. And Joram brought with him vessels of silver, and vessels of gold, and vessels of brass: salute: Heb. ask him of peace had wars: Heb. was a man of wars with brought: Heb. in his hand were
10_8_10.ESV: Toi sent his son Joram to King David, to ask about his health and to bless him because he had fought against Hadadezer and defeated him, for Hadadezer had often been at war with Toi. And Joram brought with him articles of silver, of gold, and of bronze.
10_8_10.NASB: Toi sent Joram his son to King David to greet him and bless him, because he had fought against Hadadezer and defeated him; for Hadadezer had been at war with Toi. And [Joram] brought with him articles of silver, of gold and of bronze.
10_8_10.NLT: he sent his son Joram to congratulate King David for his successful campaign. Hadadezer and Toi had been enemies and were often at war. Joram presented David with many gifts of silver, gold, and bronze.
10_8_10.WEB: then Toi sent Joram his son to king David, to greet him, and to bless him, because he had fought against Hadadezer and struck him; for Hadadezer had wars with Toi. Joram brought with him vessels of silver, vessels of gold, and vessels of brass:
10_8_10.CSB17: he sent his son Joram to King David to greet him and to congratulate him because David had fought against Hadadezer and defeated him, for Toi and Hadadezer had fought many wars. Joram had items of silver, gold, and bronze with him.
10_8_10.AMP: Toi sent Joram his son to King David to greet and congratulate him for his battle and defeat of Hadadezer; for Hadadezer had been at war with Toi. Joram brought with him articles of silver, gold, and bronze [as gifts].
10_8_10.MSG: So he sent his son Joram to King David to greet and congratulate him for fighting and defeating them, for Toi and Hadadezer were old enemies. He brought with him gifts of silver, gold, and bronze.
10_8_10.NIV: he sent his son Joram to King David to greet him and congratulate him on his victory in battle over Hadadezer, who had been at war with Tou. Joram brought with him articles of silver and gold and bronze.
10_8_10.NRSVCE: Toi sent his son Joram to King David, to greet him and to congratulate him because he had fought against Hadadezer and defeated him. Now Hadadezer had often been at war with Toi. Joram brought with him articles of silver, gold, and bronze;

10_8_11.NKJV: King David also dedicated these to the LORD, along with the silver and gold that he had dedicated from all the nations which he had subdued—
10_8_11.KJV: Which also king David did dedicate unto the LORD, with the silver and gold that he had dedicated of all nations which he subdued;
10_8_11.ESV: These also King David dedicated to the LORD, together with the silver and gold that he dedicated from all the nations he subdued,
10_8_11.NASB: King David also dedicated these to the LORD, with the silver and gold that he had dedicated from all the nations which he had subdued:
10_8_11.NLT: King David dedicated all these gifts to the LORD, as he did with the silver and gold from the other nations he had defeated —
10_8_11.WEB: King David also dedicated these to Yahweh, with the silver and gold that he dedicated of all the nations which he subdued;
10_8_11.CSB17: King David also dedicated these to the LORD, along with the silver and gold he had dedicated from all the nations he had subdued —
10_8_11.AMP: King David also dedicated these [gifts] to the LORD, along with the silver and gold that he had dedicated from all the nations which he subdued:
10_8_11.MSG: King David consecrated these along with the silver and gold from all the nations he had conquered
10_8_11.NIV: King David dedicated these articles to the Lord, as he had done with the silver and gold from all the nations he had subdued:
10_8_11.NRSVCE: these also King David dedicated to the LORD, together with the silver and gold that he dedicated from all the nations he subdued,

10_8_12.NKJV: from Syria, from Moab, from the people of Ammon, from the Philistines, from Amalek, and from the spoil of Hadadezer the son of Rehob, king of Zobah.
10_8_12.KJV: Of Syria, and of Moab, and of the children of Ammon, and of the Philistines, and of Amalek, and of the spoil of Hadadezer, son of Rehob, king of Zobah.

10_8_12.NASB: from Aram and Moab and the sons of Ammon and the Philistines and Amalek, and from the spoil of Hadadezer, son of Rehob, king of Zobah.
10_8_12.NLT: from Edom, Moab, Ammon, Philistia, and Amalek — and from Hadadezer son of Rehob, king of Zobah.
10_8_12.WEB: of Syria, of Moab, of the children of Ammon, of the Philistines, of Amalek, and of the plunder of Hadadezer, son of Rehob, king of Zobah.
10_8_12.CSB17: from Edom, Moab, the Ammonites, the Philistines, the Amalekites, and the spoil of Hadadezer son of Rehob, king of Zobah.
10_8_12.AMP: from Aram (Syria), Moab, the Ammonites, the Philistines, and Amalek, and from the spoil of Hadadezer the son of Rehob, king of Zobah.
10_8_12.MSG: —from Aram, Moab, the Ammonites, the Philistines, and from Amalek, along with the plunder from Hadadezer son of Rehob king of Zobah.
10_8_12.NIV: Edom and Moab, the Ammonites and the Philistines, and Amalek. He also dedicated the plunder taken from Hadadezer son of Rehob, king of Zobah.
10_8_12.NRSVCE: from Edom, Moab, the Ammonites, the Philistines, Amalek, and from the spoil of King Hadadezer son of Rehob of Zobah.

10_8_13.NKJV: And David made himself a name when he returned from killing eighteen thousand Syrians in the Valley of Salt.
10_8_13.KJV: And David gat him a name when he returned from smiting of the Syrians in the valley of salt, being eighteen thousand men. smiting: Heb. his smiting
10_8_13.ESV: And David made a name for himself when he returned from striking down 18,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt.
10_8_13.NASB: So David made a name [for himself] when he returned from killing 18,000 Arameans in the Valley of Salt.
10_8_13.NLT: So David became even more famous when he returned from destroying 18,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt.
10_8_13.WEB: David earned a reputation when he returned from striking down eighteen thousand men of the Syrians in the Valley of Salt.
10_8_13.CSB17: David made a reputation for himself when he returned from striking down eighteen thousand Edomites in Salt Valley.
10_8_13.AMP: So David made a name for himself when he returned from killing 18,000 Arameans (Syrians) in the Valley of Salt.
10_8_13.MSG: David built a victory monument on his return from defeating the Arameans.Abishai son of Zeruiah fought and defeated the Edomites in the Salt Valley. Eighteen thousand of them were killed.
10_8_13.NIV: And David became famous after he returned from striking down eighteen thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt.
10_8_13.NRSVCE: David won a name for himself. When he returned, he killed eighteen thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt.

10_8_14.KJV: And he put garrisons in Edom; throughout all Edom put he garrisons, and all they of Edom became David's servants. And the LORD preserved David whithersoever he went.

10_8_14.NASB: He put garrisons in Edom. In all Edom he put garrisons, and all the Edomites became servants to David. And the LORD helped David wherever he went.
10_8_14.NLT: He placed army garrisons throughout Edom, and all the Edomites became David’s subjects. In fact, the LORD made David victorious wherever he went.
10_8_14.WEB: He put garrisons in Edom. Throughout all Edom put he garrisons, and all the Edomites became servants to David. Yahweh gave victory to David wherever he went.
10_8_14.CSB17: He placed garrisons throughout Edom, and all the Edomites were subject to David. The LORD made David victorious wherever he went.
10_8_14.AMP: He put garrisons in Edom; in all Edom he put garrisons, and all the Edomites became servants to David. And the LORD helped him wherever he went.

10_8_14.NRSVCE: He put garrisons in Edom; throughout all Edom he put garrisons, and all the Edomites became David’s servants. And the LORD gave victory to David wherever he went.

10_8_15.NKJV: So David reigned over all Israel; and David administered judgment and justice to all his people.
10_8_15.KJV: And David reigned over all Israel; and David executed judgment and justice unto all his people.
10_8_15.ESV: So David reigned over all Israel. And David administered justice and equity to all his people.
10_8_15.NASB: So David reigned over all Israel; and David administered justice and righteousness for all his people.
10_8_15.NLT: So David reigned over all Israel and did what was just and right for all his people.
10_8_15.WEB: David reigned over all Israel; and David executed justice and righteousness for all his people.
10_8_15.CSB17: So David reigned over all Israel, administering justice and righteousness for all his people.
10_8_15.AMP: So David reigned over all Israel, and continued to administer justice and righteousness for all his people.
10_8_15.MSG: Thus David ruled over all of Israel. He ruled well—fair and evenhanded in all his duties and relationships.
10_8_15.NIV: David’s OfficialsDavid reigned over all Israel, doing what was just and right for all his people.
10_8_15.NRSVCE: So David reigned over all Israel; and David administered justice and equity to all his people.

10_8_16.NKJV: Joab the son of Zeruiah was over the army; Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud was recorder;
10_8_16.KJV: And Joab the son of Zeruiah was over the host; and Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud was recorder; recorder: or, remembrancer, or, writer of chronicles
10_8_16.ESV: Joab the son of Zeruiah was over the army, and Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud was recorder,
10_8_16.NASB: Joab the son of Zeruiah [was] over the army, and Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud [was] recorder.
10_8_16.NLT: Joab son of Zeruiah was commander of the army. Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was the royal historian.

10_8_16.NRSVCE: Joab son of Zeruiah was over the army; Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was recorder;

10_8_17.NKJV: Zadok the son of Ahitub and Ahimelech the son of Abiathar were the priests; Seraiah was the scribe;
10_8_17.KJV: And Zadok the son of Ahitub, and Ahimelech the son of Abiathar, were the priests; and Seraiah was the scribe; scribe: or, secretary

10_9_1.NKJV: Now David said, “Is there still anyone who is left of the house of Saul, that I may show him kindness for Jonathan’s sake?”

10_9_2.NASB: Now there was a servant of the house of Saul whose name was Ziba, and they called him to David; and the king said to him, 'Are you Ziba?' And he said, '[I am] your servant.'
10_9_2.NLT: He summoned a man named Ziba, who had been one of Saul’s servants. “Are you Ziba?” the king asked. “Yes sir, I am,” Ziba replied.

10_9_2.MSG: It happened that a servant from Saul's household named Ziba was there. They called him into David's presence. The king asked him, "Are you Ziba?""Yes sir," he replied.

10_9_2.NRSVCE: Now there was a servant of the house of Saul whose name was Ziba, and he was summoned to David. The king said to him, “Are you Ziba?” And he said, “At your service!”

10_9_3.NKJV: Then the king said, “Is there not still someone of the house of Saul, to whom I may show the kindness of God?” And Ziba said to the king, “There is still a son of Jonathan who is lame in his feet.”

10_9_6.NKJV: Now when Mephibosheth the son of Jonathan, the son of Saul, had come to David, he fell on his face and prostrated himself. Then David said, “Mephibosheth?” And he answered, “Here is your servant!”
10_9_6.KJV: Now when Mephibosheth, the son of Jonathan, the son of Saul, was come unto David, he fell on his face, and did reverence. And David said, Mephibosheth. And he answered, Behold thy servant!

10_9_7.NKJV: So David said to him, “Do not fear, for I will surely show you kindness for Jonathan your father’s sake, and will restore to you all the land of Saul your grandfather; and you shall eat bread at my table continually.”
10_9_7.KJV: And David said unto him, Fear not: for I will surely shew thee kindness for Jonathan thy father's sake, and will restore thee all the land of Saul thy father; and thou shalt eat bread at my table continually.
10_9_7.ESV: And David said to him, “Do not fear, for I will show you kindness for the sake of your father Jonathan, and I will restore to you all the land of Saul your father, and you shall eat at my table always.”
10_9_7.NASB: David said to him, 'Do not fear, for I will surely show kindness to you for the sake of your father Jonathan, and will restore to you all the land of your grandfather Saul; and you shall eat at my table regularly.'
10_9_7.NLT: “Don’t be afraid!” David said. “I intend to show kindness to you because of my promise to your father, Jonathan. I will give you all the property that once belonged to your grandfather Saul, and you will eat here with me at the king’s table!”
10_9_7.WEB: David said to him, “Don’t be afraid of him; for I will surely show you kindness for Jonathan your father’s sake, and will restore to you all the land of Saul your father. You will eat bread at my table continually.”
10_9_7.CSB17: “Don’t be afraid,” David said to him, “since I intend to show you kindness for the sake of your father Jonathan. I will restore to you all your grandfather Saul’s fields, and you will always eat meals at my table.”
10_9_7.AMP: David said to him, "Do not be afraid, for I will certainly show you kindness for the sake of your father Jonathan, and will restore to you all the land of your grandfather Saul; and you shall always eat at my table."
10_9_7.MSG: "Don't be frightened," said David. "I'd like to do something special for you in memory of your father Jonathan. To begin with, I'm returning to you all the properties of your grandfather Saul. Furthermore, from now on you'll take all your meals at my table."
10_9_7.NIV: “Don’t be afraid,” David said to him, “for I will surely show you kindness for the sake of your father Jonathan. I will restore to you all the land that belonged to your grandfather Saul, and you will always eat at my table.”
10_9_7.NRSVCE: David said to him, “Do not be afraid, for I will show you kindness for the sake of your father Jonathan; I will restore to you all the land of your grandfather Saul, and you yourself shall eat at my table always.”

10_9_8.NKJV: Then he bowed himself, and said, “What is your servant, that you should look upon such a dead dog as I?”

10_9_8.NASB: Again he prostrated himself and said, 'What is your servant, that you should regard a dead dog like me?'

10_9_10.ESV: And you and your sons and your servants shall till the land for him and shall bring in the produce, that your master's grandson may have bread to eat. But Mephibosheth your master's grandson shall always eat at my table.” Now Ziba had fifteen sons and twenty servants.

10_9_11.NKJV: Then Ziba said to the king, “According to all that my lord the king has commanded his servant, so will your servant do.” “As for Mephibosheth,” said the king, “he shall eat at my table like one of the king’s sons.”

10_9_13.MSG: Mephibosheth lived in Jerusalem, taking all his meals at the king's table. He was lame in both feet.
10_9_13.NIV: And Mephibosheth lived in Jerusalem, because he always ate at the king’s table, and he was crippled in both feet.
10_9_13.NRSVCE: Mephibosheth lived in Jerusalem, for he always ate at the king’s table. Now he was lame in both his feet.

10_10_1.NKJV: It happened after this that the king of the people of Ammon died, and Hanun his son reigned in his place.
10_10_1.KJV: And it came to pass after this, that the king of the children of Ammon died, and Hanun his son reigned in his stead.
10_10_1.ESV: After this the king of the Ammonites died, and Hanun his son reigned in his place.
10_10_1.NASB: Now it happened afterwards that the king of the Ammonites died, and Hanun his son became king in his place.
10_10_1.NLT: Some time after this, King Nahash of the Ammonites died, and his son Hanun became king.
10_10_1.WEB: After this, the king of the children of Ammon died, and Hanun his son reigned in his place.
10_10_1.CSB17: Some time later, the king of the Ammonites died, and his son Hanun became king in his place.
10_10_1.AMP: Now it happened later that [Nahash] the king of the Ammonites died, and his son Hanun became king in his place.
10_10_1.MSG: Sometime after this, the king of the Ammonites died and Hanun, his son, succeeded him as king.
10_10_1.NIV: David Defeats the AmmonitesIn the course of time, the king of the Ammonites died, and his son Hanun succeeded him as king.
10_10_1.NRSVCE: Some time afterward, the king of the Ammonites died, and his son Hanun succeeded him.

10_10_2.NKJV: Then David said, “I will show kindness to Hanun the son of Nahash, as his father showed kindness to me.” So David sent by the hand of his servants to comfort him concerning his father. And David’s servants came into the land of the people of Ammon.
10_10_2.KJV: Then said David, I will shew kindness unto Hanun the son of Nahash, as his father shewed kindness unto me. And David sent to comfort him by the hand of his servants for his father. And David's servants came into the land of the children of Ammon.
10_10_2.ESV: And David said, “I will deal loyally with Hanun the son of Nahash, as his father dealt loyally with me.” So David sent by his servants to console him concerning his father. And David's servants came into the land of the Ammonites.
10_10_2.NASB: Then David said, 'I will show kindness to Hanun the son of Nahash, just as his father showed kindness to me.' So David sent some of his servants to console him concerning his father. But when David's servants came to the land of the Ammonites,
10_10_2.NLT: David said, “I am going to show loyalty to Hanun just as his father, Nahash, was always loyal to me.” So David sent ambassadors to express sympathy to Hanun about his father’s death. But when David’s ambassadors arrived in the land of Ammon,
10_10_2.WEB: David said, “I will show kindness to Hanun the son of Nahash, as his father showed kindness to me.” So David sent by his servants to comfort him concerning his father. David’s servants came into the land of the children of Ammon.
10_10_2.CSB17: Then David said, “I’ll show kindness to Hanun son of Nahash, just as his father showed kindness to me.” So David sent his emissaries to console Hanun concerning his father. However, when they arrived in the land of the Ammonites,
10_10_2.AMP: Then David said, "I will show kindness to Hanun the son of Nahash, just as his father did to me." So David sent [a letter along with] some of his servants to console him in regard to his father's death; and David's servants came into the land of the Ammonites.
10_10_2.MSG: David said, "I'd like to show some kindness to Hanun, the son of Nahash—treat him as well and as kindly as his father treated me." So David sent Hanun condolences regarding his father.But when David's servants got to the land of the Ammonites,
10_10_2.NIV: David thought, “I will show kindness to Hanun son of Nahash, just as his father showed kindness to me.” So David sent a delegation to express his sympathy to Hanun concerning his father.When David’s men came to the land of the Ammonites,
10_10_2.NRSVCE: David said, “I will deal loyally with Hanun son of Nahash, just as his father dealt loyally with me.” So David sent envoys to console him concerning his father. When David’s envoys came into the land of the Ammonites,

10_10_3.NKJV: And the princes of the people of Ammon said to Hanun their lord, “Do you think that David really honors your father because he has sent comforters to you? Has David not rather sent his servants to you to search the city, to spy it out, and to overthrow it?”
10_10_3.KJV: And the princes of the children of Ammon said unto Hanun their lord, Thinkest thou that David doth honour thy father, that he hath sent comforters unto thee? hath not David rather sent his servants unto thee, to search the city, and to spy it out, and to overthrow it? Thinkest: Heb. In thine eyes doth David?
10_10_3.ESV: But the princes of the Ammonites said to Hanun their lord, “Do you think, because David has sent comforters to you, that he is honoring your father? Has not David sent his servants to you to search the city and to spy it out and to overthrow it?”
10_10_3.NASB: the princes of the Ammonites said to Hanun their lord, 'Do you think that David is honoring your father because he has sent consolers to you? Has David not sent his servants to you in order to search the city, to spy it out and overthrow it?'
10_10_3.NLT: the Ammonite commanders said to Hanun, their master, “Do you really think these men are coming here to honor your father? No! David has sent them to spy out the city so they can come in and conquer it!”
10_10_3.WEB: But the princes of the children of Ammon said to Hanun their lord, “Do you think that David honors your father, in that he has sent comforters to you? Hasn’t David sent his servants to you to search the city, to spy it out, and to overthrow it?”
10_10_3.CSB17: the Ammonite leaders said to Hanun their lord, “Just because David has sent men with condolences for you, do you really believe he’s showing respect for your father? Instead, hasn’t David sent his emissaries in order to scout out the city, spy on it, and demolish it?”
10_10_3.AMP: But the princes of the Ammonites [were suspicious and] said to Hanun their lord, "Do you think that David is honoring your father because he has sent comforters to you? Has David not sent his servants to you in order to search the city, to spy it out and overthrow it?"
10_10_3.MSG: the Ammonite leaders warned Hanun, their head delegate, "Do you for a minute suppose that David is honoring your father by sending you comforters? Don't you think it's because he wants to snoop around the city and size it up that David has sent his emissaries to you?"
10_10_3.NIV: the Ammonite nobles said to Hanun their lord, “Do you think David is honoring your father by sending men to you to express sympathy? Hasn’t David sent them to you to explore the city and spy it out and overthrow it?”
10_10_3.NRSVCE: the princes of the Ammonites said to their lord Hanun, “Do you really think that David is honoring your father just because he has sent messengers with condolences to you? Has not David sent his envoys to you to search the city, to spy it out, and to overthrow it?”

10_10_4.NKJV: Therefore Hanun took David’s servants, shaved off half of their beards, cut off their garments in the middle, at their buttocks, and sent them away.
10_10_4.KJV: Wherefore Hanun took David's servants, and shaved off the one half of their beards, and cut off their garments in the middle, even to their buttocks, and sent them away.
10_10_4.ESV: So Hanun took David's servants and shaved off half the beard of each and cut off their garments in the middle, at their hips, and sent them away.
10_10_4.NASB: So Hanun took David's servants and shaved off half of their beards, and cut off their garments in the middle as far as their hips, and sent them away.
10_10_4.NLT: So Hanun seized David’s ambassadors and shaved off half of each man’s beard, cut off their robes at the buttocks, and sent them back to David in shame.
10_10_4.WEB: So Hanun took David’s servants, shaved off one half of their beards, and cut off their garments in the middle, even to their buttocks, and sent them away.
10_10_4.CSB17: So Hanun took David’s emissaries, shaved off half their beards, cut their clothes in half at the hips, and sent them away.
10_10_4.AMP: So Hanun took David's servants and shaved off half their beards, and cut off their robes in the middle as far as their hips, and sent them away.
10_10_4.MSG: So Hanun seized David's men, shaved off half their beards, cut off their robes halfway up their buttocks, and sent them packing.
10_10_4.NIV: So Hanun seized David’s men, shaved off half of each man’s beard, cut off their garments in the middle at the buttocks, and sent them away.
10_10_4.NRSVCE: So Hanun seized David’s envoys, shaved off half the beard of each, cut off their garments in the middle at their hips, and sent them away.

10_10_5.NKJV: When they told David, he sent to meet them, because the men were greatly ashamed. And the king said, “Wait at Jericho until your beards have grown, and then return.”
10_10_5.KJV: When they told it unto David, he sent to meet them, because the men were greatly ashamed: and the king said, Tarry at Jericho until your beards be grown, and then return.
10_10_5.ESV: When it was told David, he sent to meet them, for the men were greatly ashamed. And the king said, “Remain at Jericho until your beards have grown and then return.”
10_10_5.NASB: When they told [it] to David, he sent to meet them, for the men were greatly humiliated. And the king said, 'Stay at Jericho until your beards grow, and [then] return.'
10_10_5.NLT: When David heard what had happened, he sent messengers to tell the men, “Stay at Jericho until your beards grow out, and then come back.” For they felt deep shame because of their appearance.
10_10_5.WEB: When they told David this, he sent to meet them, for the men were greatly ashamed. The king said, “Wait at Jericho until your beards have grown, and then return.”
10_10_5.CSB17: When this was reported to David, he sent someone to meet them, since they were deeply humiliated. The king said, “Stay in Jericho until your beards grow back; then return.”
10_10_5.AMP: When David was informed, he sent [messengers] to meet them [before they reached Jerusalem], for the men were greatly ashamed and humiliated. And the king said, "Stay at Jericho until your beards grow, and then return."
10_10_5.MSG: When all this was reported to David, he sent someone to meet them, for they were seriously humiliated. The king told them, "Stay in Jericho until your beards grow out. Only then come back."
10_10_5.NIV: When David was told about this, he sent messengers to meet the men, for they were greatly humiliated. The king said, “Stay at Jericho till your beards have grown, and then come back.”
10_10_5.NRSVCE: When David was told, he sent to meet them, for the men were greatly ashamed. The king said, “Remain at Jericho until your beards have grown, and then return.”

10_10_6.NKJV: When the people of Ammon saw that they had made themselves repulsive to David, the people of Ammon sent and hired the Syrians of Beth Rehob and the Syrians of Zoba, twenty thousand foot soldiers; and from the king of Maacah one thousand men, and from Ish-Tob twelve thousand men.
10_10_6.KJV: And when the children of Ammon saw that they stank before David, the children of Ammon sent and hired the Syrians of Bethrehob, and the Syrians of Zoba, twenty thousand footmen, and of king Maacah a thousand men, and of Ishtob twelve thousand men. Ishtob: or, the men of Tob
10_10_6.ESV: When the Ammonites saw that they had become a stench to David, the Ammonites sent and hired the Syrians of Beth-rehob, and the Syrians of Zobah, 20,000 foot soldiers, and the king of Maacah with 1,000 men, and the men of Tob, 12,000 men.
10_10_6.NASB: Now when the sons of Ammon saw that they had become odious to David, the sons of Ammon sent and hired the Arameans of Beth-rehob and the Arameans of Zobah, 20,000 foot soldiers, and the king of Maacah with 1,000 men, and the men of Tob with 12,000 men.
10_10_6.NLT: When the people of Ammon realized how seriously they had angered David, they sent and hired 20,000 Aramean foot soldiers from the lands of Beth-rehob and Zobah, 1,000 from the king of Maacah, and 12,000 from the land of Tob.
10_10_6.WEB: When the children of Ammon saw that they had become odious to David, the children of Ammon sent and hired the Syrians of Beth Rehob, and the Syrians of Zobah, twenty thousand footmen, and the king of Maacah with one thousand men, and the men of Tob twelve thousand men.
10_10_6.CSB17: When the Ammonites realized they had become repulsive to David, they hired twenty thousand foot soldiers from the Arameans of Beth-rehob and Zobah, one thousand men from the king of Maacah, and twelve thousand men from Tob.
10_10_6.AMP: When the Ammonites saw that they had become an object of hatred to David, they sent word and hired the Arameans (Syrians) of Beth-rehob and the Arameans of Zobah, 20,000 foot soldiers, and the king of Maacah with 1,000 men, and the men of Tob with 12,000 men [to fight for them].
10_10_6.MSG: When it dawned on the Ammonites that as far as David was concerned they stunk to high heaven, they hired Aramean soldiers from Beth-Rehob and Zobah—twenty thousand infantry—and a thousand men from the king of Maacah, and twelve thousand men from Tob.
10_10_6.NIV: When the Ammonites realized that they had become a stench in David’s nostrils, they hired twenty thousand Aramean foot soldiers from Beth Rehob and Zobah, as well as the king of Maacah with a thousand men, and also twelve thousand men from Tob.
10_10_6.NRSVCE: When the Ammonites saw that they had become odious to David, the Ammonites sent and hired the Arameans of Beth-rehob and the Arameans of Zobah, twenty thousand foot soldiers, as well as the king of Maacah, one thousand men, and the men of Tob, twelve thousand men.

10_10_7.NKJV: Now when David heard of it, he sent Joab and all the army of the mighty men.
10_10_7.KJV: And when David heard of it, he sent Joab, and all the host of the mighty men.
10_10_7.ESV: And when David heard of it, he sent Joab and all the host of the mighty men.
10_10_7.NASB: When David heard [of it], he sent Joab and all the army, the mighty men.
10_10_7.NLT: When David heard about this, he sent Joab and all his warriors to fight them.
10_10_7.WEB: When David heard of it, he sent Joab, and all the army of the mighty men.
10_10_7.CSB17: David heard about it and sent Joab and all the elite troops.
10_10_7.AMP: When David heard about it, he sent Joab and the entire army, the strong and brave men.
10_10_7.MSG: When David heard of this, he dispatched Joab with his strongest fighters in full force.
10_10_7.NIV: On hearing this, David sent Joab out with the entire army of fighting men.
10_10_7.NRSVCE: When David heard of it, he sent Joab and all the army with the warriors.

10_10_8.NKJV: Then the people of Ammon came out and put themselves in battle array at the entrance of the gate. And the Syrians of Zoba, Beth Rehob, Ish-Tob, and Maacah were by themselves in the field.
10_10_8.KJV: And the children of Ammon came out, and put the battle in array at the entering in of the gate: and the Syrians of Zoba, and of Rehob, and Ishtob, and Maacah, were by themselves in the field.

10_10_8.NASB: The sons of Ammon came out and drew up in battle array at the entrance of the city, while the Arameans of Zobah and of Rehob and the men of Tob and Maacah [were] by themselves in the field.
10_10_8.NLT: The Ammonite troops came out and drew up their battle lines at the entrance of the city gate, while the Arameans from Zobah and Rehob and the men from Tob and Maacah positioned themselves to fight in the open fields.

10_10_8.NIV: The Ammonites came out and drew up in battle formation at the entrance to their city gate, while the Arameans of Zobah and Rehob and the men of Tob and Maacah were by themselves in the open country.

10_10_9.NKJV: When Joab saw that the battle line was against him before and behind, he chose some of Israel’s best and put them in battle array against the Syrians.
10_10_9.KJV: When Joab saw that the front of the battle was against him before and behind, he chose of all the choice men of Israel, and put them in array against the Syrians:

10_10_9.NASB: Now when Joab saw that the battle was set against him in front and in the rear, he selected from all the choice men of Israel, and arrayed [them] against the Arameans.
10_10_9.NLT: When Joab saw that he would have to fight on both the front and the rear, he chose some of Israel’s elite troops and placed them under his personal command to fight the Arameans in the fields.
10_10_9.WEB: Now when Joab saw that the battle was set against him before and behind, he chose of all the choice men of Israel, and put them in array against the Syrians.
10_10_9.CSB17: When Joab saw that there was a battle line in front of him and another behind him, he chose some of Israel’s finest young men and lined up in formation to engage the Arameans.

10_10_10.NKJV: And the rest of the people he put under the command of Abishai his brother, that he might set them in battle array against the people of Ammon.

10_10_10.ESV: The rest of his men he put in the charge of Abishai his brother, and he arrayed them against the Ammonites.
10_10_10.NASB: But the remainder of the people he placed in the hand of Abishai his brother, and he arrayed [them] against the sons of Ammon.
10_10_10.NLT: He left the rest of the army under the command of his brother Abishai, who was to attack the Ammonites.

10_10_10.CSB17: He placed the rest of the forces under the command of his brother Abishai. They lined up in formation to engage the Ammonites.

10_10_10.NRSVCE: the rest of his men he put in the charge of his brother Abishai, and he arrayed them against the Ammonites.

10_10_11.NKJV: Then he said, “If the Syrians are too strong for me, then you shall help me; but if the people of Ammon are too strong for you, then I will come and help you.

10_10_11.ESV: And he said, “If the Syrians are too strong for me, then you shall help me, but if the Ammonites are too strong for you, then I will come and help you.
10_10_11.NASB: He said, 'If the Arameans are too strong for me, then you shall help me, but if the sons of Ammon are too strong for you, then I will come to help you.

10_10_12.NKJV: Be of good courage, and let us be strong for our people and for the cities of our God. And may the LORD do what is good in His sight.”

10_10_13.NKJV: So Joab and the people who were with him drew near for the battle against the Syrians, and they fled before him.

10_10_13.NASB: So Joab and the people who were with him drew near to the battle against the Arameans, and they fled before him.
10_10_13.NLT: When Joab and his troops attacked, the Arameans began to run away.

10_10_13.MSG: But when Joab and his soldiers moved in to fight the Arameans, they ran off in full retreat.
10_10_13.NIV: Then Joab and the troops with him advanced to fight the Arameans, and they fled before him.
10_10_13.NRSVCE: So Joab and the people who were with him moved forward into battle against the Arameans; and they fled before him.

10_10_14.NKJV: When the people of Ammon saw that the Syrians were fleeing, they also fled before Abishai, and entered the city. So Joab returned from the people of Ammon and went to Jerusalem.
10_10_14.KJV: And when the children of Ammon saw that the Syrians were fled, then fled they also before Abishai, and entered into the city. So Joab returned from the children of Ammon, and came to Jerusalem.
10_10_14.ESV: And when the Ammonites saw that the Syrians fled, they likewise fled before Abishai and entered the city. Then Joab returned from fighting against the Ammonites and came to Jerusalem.
10_10_14.NASB: When the sons of Ammon saw that the Arameans fled, they [also] fled before Abishai and entered the city. Then Joab returned from [fighting] against the sons of Ammon and came to Jerusalem.
10_10_14.NLT: And when the Ammonites saw the Arameans running, they ran from Abishai and retreated into the city. After the battle was over, Joab returned to Jerusalem.
10_10_14.WEB: When the children of Ammon saw that the Syrians had fled, they likewise fled before Abishai, and entered into the city. Then Joab returned from the children of Ammon, and came to Jerusalem.
10_10_14.CSB17: When the Ammonites saw that the Arameans had fled, they too fled before Abishai and entered the city. So Joab withdrew from the attack against the Ammonites and went to Jerusalem.
10_10_14.AMP: When the Ammonites saw that the Arameans had fled, they also fled before Abishai and entered the city. So Joab returned from battling against the Ammonites and came to Jerusalem.
10_10_14.MSG: Then the Ammonites, seeing the Arameans run for dear life, took to their heels from Abishai and went into the city.So Joab left off fighting the Ammonites and returned to Jerusalem.
10_10_14.NIV: When the Ammonites saw that the Arameans were fleeing, they fled before Abishai and went inside the city. So Joab returned from fighting the Ammonites and came to Jerusalem.
10_10_14.NRSVCE: When the Ammonites saw that the Arameans fled, they likewise fled before Abishai, and entered the city. Then Joab returned from fighting against the Ammonites, and came to Jerusalem.

10_10_15.NKJV: When the Syrians saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they gathered together.
10_10_15.KJV: And when the Syrians saw that they were smitten before Israel, they gathered themselves together.
10_10_15.ESV: But when the Syrians saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they gathered themselves together.
10_10_15.NASB: When the Arameans saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they gathered themselves together.
10_10_15.NLT: The Arameans now realized that they were no match for Israel. So when they regrouped,
10_10_15.WEB: When the Syrians saw that they were defeated by Israel, they gathered themselves together.
10_10_15.CSB17: When the Arameans saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they regrouped.
10_10_15.AMP: When the Arameans saw that they were defeated by Israel, they gathered together.
10_10_15.MSG: When the Arameans saw how badly they'd been beaten by Israel, they picked up the pieces and regrouped.
10_10_15.NIV: After the Arameans saw that they had been routed by Israel, they regrouped.
10_10_15.NRSVCE: But when the Arameans saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they gathered themselves together.

10_10_16.NKJV: Then Hadadezer sent and brought out the Syrians who were beyond the River, and they came to Helam. And Shobach the commander of Hadadezer’s army went before them.
10_10_16.KJV: And Hadarezer sent, and brought out the Syrians that were beyond the river: and they came to Helam; and Shobach the captain of the host of Hadarezer went before them. the river: that is, Euphrates Shobach: or, Shophach
10_10_16.ESV: And Hadadezer sent and brought out the Syrians who were beyond the Euphrates. They came to Helam, with Shobach the commander of the army of Hadadezer at their head.
10_10_16.NASB: And Hadadezer sent and brought out the Arameans who were beyond the River, and they came to Helam; and Shobach the commander of the army of Hadadezer led them.
10_10_16.NLT: they were joined by additional Aramean troops summoned by Hadadezer from the other side of the Euphrates River. These troops arrived at Helam under the command of Shobach, the commander of Hadadezer’s forces.
10_10_16.WEB: Hadadezer sent, and brought out the Syrians who were beyond the River: and they came to Helam, with Shobach the captain of the army of Hadadezer at their head.
10_10_16.CSB17: Hadadezer sent messengers to bring the Arameans who were beyond the Euphrates River, and they came to Helam with Shobach, commander of Hadadezer’s army, leading them.
10_10_16.AMP: Hadadezer sent word and brought out the Arameans who were beyond the River [Euphrates]; and they came to Helam; and Shobach the commander of the army of Hadadezer led them.
10_10_16.MSG: Hadadezer sent for the Arameans who were across the River. They came to Helam. Shobach, commander of Hadadezer's army, led them.
10_10_16.NIV: Hadadezer had Arameans brought from beyond the River; they went to Helam, with Shobach the commander of Hadadezer’s army leading them.
10_10_16.NRSVCE: Hadadezer sent and brought out the Arameans who were beyond the Euphrates; and they came to Helam, with Shobach the commander of the army of Hadadezer at their head.

10_10_17.NKJV: When it was told David, he gathered all Israel, crossed over the Jordan, and came to Helam. And the Syrians set themselves in battle array against David and fought with him.
10_10_17.KJV: And when it was told David, he gathered all Israel together, and passed over Jordan, and came to Helam. And the Syrians set themselves in array against David, and fought with him.
10_10_17.ESV: And when it was told David, he gathered all Israel together and crossed the Jordan and came to Helam. The Syrians arrayed themselves against David and fought with him.
10_10_17.NASB: Now when it was told David, he gathered all Israel together and crossed the Jordan, and came to Helam. And the Arameans arrayed themselves to meet David and fought against him.
10_10_17.NLT: When David heard what was happening, he mobilized all Israel, crossed the Jordan River, and led the army to Helam. The Arameans positioned themselves in battle formation and fought against David.
10_10_17.WEB: David was told that; and he gathered all Israel together, passed over the Jordan, and came to Helam. The Syrians set themselves in array against David, and fought with him.
10_10_17.CSB17: When this was reported to David, he gathered all Israel, crossed the Jordan, and went to Helam. Then the Arameans lined up to engage David in battle and fought against him.
10_10_17.AMP: When David was informed, he gathered all Israel together and crossed the Jordan, and came to Helam. Then the Arameans assembled in battle formation to meet David and fought against him.
10_10_17.MSG: All this was reported to David.So David mustered Israel, crossed the Jordan, and came to Helam. The Arameans went into battle formation, ready for David, and the fight was on.
10_10_17.NIV: When David was told of this, he gathered all Israel, crossed the Jordan and went to Helam. The Arameans formed their battle lines to meet David and fought against him.
10_10_17.NRSVCE: When it was told David, he gathered all Israel together, and crossed the Jordan, and came to Helam. The Arameans arrayed themselves against David and fought with him.

10_10_18.NKJV: Then the Syrians fled before Israel; and David killed seven hundred charioteers and forty thousand horsemen of the Syrians, and struck Shobach the commander of their army, who died there.
10_10_18.KJV: And the Syrians fled before Israel; and David slew the men of seven hundred chariots of the Syrians, and forty thousand horsemen, and smote Shobach the captain of their host, who died there.
10_10_18.ESV: And the Syrians fled before Israel, and David killed of the Syrians the men of 700 chariots, and 40,000 horsemen, and wounded Shobach the commander of their army, so that he died there.
10_10_18.NASB: But the Arameans fled before Israel, and David killed 700 charioteers of the Arameans and 40,000 horsemen and struck down Shobach the commander of their army, and he died there.
10_10_18.NLT: But again the Arameans fled from the Israelites. This time David’s forces killed 700 charioteers and 40,000 foot soldiers, including Shobach, the commander of their army.
10_10_18.WEB: The Syrians fled before Israel; and David killed seven hundred charioteers of the Syrians, and forty thousand horsemen, and struck Shobach the captain of their army, so that he died there.
10_10_18.CSB17: But the Arameans fled before Israel, and David killed seven hundred of their charioteers and forty thousand foot soldiers. He also struck down Shobach commander of their army, who died there.
10_10_18.AMP: But the Arameans fled before Israel, and David killed 700 Aramean charioteers and 40,000 horsemen, and struck Shobach the commander of their army, and he died there.
10_10_18.MSG: But the Arameans again scattered before Israel. David killed seven hundred chariot drivers and forty thousand cavalry. And he mortally wounded Shobach, the army commander, who died on the battlefield.
10_10_18.NIV: But they fled before Israel, and David killed seven hundred of their charioteers and forty thousand of their foot soldiers. He also struck down Shobach the commander of their army, and he died there.
10_10_18.NRSVCE: The Arameans fled before Israel; and David killed of the Arameans seven hundred chariot teams, and forty thousand horsemen, and wounded Shobach the commander of their army, so that he died there.

10_10_19.NKJV: And when all the kings who were servants to Hadadezer saw that they were defeated by Israel, they made peace with Israel and served them. So the Syrians were afraid to help the people of Ammon anymore.
10_10_19.KJV: And when all the kings that were servants to Hadarezer saw that they were smitten before Israel, they made peace with Israel, and served them. So the Syrians feared to help the children of Ammon any more.
10_10_19.ESV: And when all the kings who were servants of Hadadezer saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they made peace with Israel and became subject to them. So the Syrians were afraid to save the Ammonites anymore.
10_10_19.NASB: When all the kings, servants of Hadadezer, saw that they were defeated by Israel, they made peace with Israel and served them. So the Arameans feared to help the sons of Ammon anymore.
10_10_19.NLT: When all the kings allied with Hadadezer saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they surrendered to Israel and became their subjects. After that, the Arameans were afraid to help the Ammonites.
10_10_19.WEB: When all the kings who were servants to Hadadezer saw that they were defeated before Israel, they made peace with Israel, and served them. So the Syrians were afraid to help the children of Ammon any more.
10_10_19.CSB17: When all the kings who were Hadadezer’s subjects saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they made peace with Israel and became their subjects. After this, the Arameans were afraid to ever help the Ammonites again.
10_10_19.AMP: When all the kings serving Hadadezer saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they made peace with Israel and served them. So the Arameans (Syrians) were afraid to help the Ammonites anymore.
10_10_19.MSG: When all the kings who were vassals of Hadadezer saw that they had been routed by Israel, they made peace and became Israel's vassals. The Arameans were afraid to help the Ammonites ever again.
10_10_19.NIV: When all the kings who were vassals of Hadadezer saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they made peace with the Israelites and became subject to them.So the Arameans were afraid to help the Ammonites anymore.
10_10_19.NRSVCE: When all the kings who were servants of Hadadezer saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they made peace with Israel, and became subject to them. So the Arameans were afraid to help the Ammonites any more.

10_11_1.NKJV: It happened in the spring of the year, at the time when kings go out to battle, that David sent Joab and his servants with him, and all Israel; and they destroyed the people of Ammon and besieged Rabbah. But David remained at Jerusalem.
10_11_1.KJV: And it came to pass, after the year was expired, at the time when kings go forth to battle, that David sent Joab, and his servants with him, and all Israel; and they destroyed the children of Ammon, and besieged Rabbah. But David tarried still at Jerusalem. after: Heb. at the return of the year
10_11_1.ESV: In the spring of the year, the time when kings go out to battle, David sent Joab, and his servants with him, and all Israel. And they ravaged the Ammonites and besieged Rabbah. But David remained at Jerusalem.
10_11_1.NASB: Then it happened in the spring, at the time when kings go out [to battle], that David sent Joab and his servants with him and all Israel, and they destroyed the sons of Ammon and besieged Rabbah. But David stayed at Jerusalem.
10_11_1.NLT: In the spring of the year, when kings normally go out to war, David sent Joab and the Israelite army to fight the Ammonites. They destroyed the Ammonite army and laid siege to the city of Rabbah. However, David stayed behind in Jerusalem.
10_11_1.WEB: At the return of the year, at the time when kings go out, David sent Joab, and his servants with him, and all Israel; and they destroyed the children of Ammon, and besieged Rabbah. But David stayed at Jerusalem.
10_11_1.CSB17: In the spring when kings march out to war, David sent Joab with his officers and all Israel. They destroyed the Ammonites and besieged Rabbah, but David remained in Jerusalem.
10_11_1.AMP: Then it happened in the spring, at the time when the kings go out to battle, that David sent Joab and his servants with him, and all [the fighting men of] Israel, and they destroyed the Ammonites and besieged Rabbah. But David remained in Jerusalem.
10_11_1.MSG: When that time of year came around again, the anniversary of the Ammonite aggression, David dispatched Joab and his fighting men of Israel in full force to destroy the Ammonites for good. They laid siege to Rabbah, but David stayed in Jerusalem.
10_11_1.NIV: David and BathshebaIn the spring, at the time when kings go off to war, David sent Joab out with the king’s men and the whole Israelite army. They destroyed the Ammonites and besieged Rabbah. But David remained in Jerusalem.
10_11_1.NRSVCE: In the spring of the year, the time when kings go out to battle, David sent Joab with his officers and all Israel with him; they ravaged the Ammonites, and besieged Rabbah. But David remained at Jerusalem.

10_11_2.NKJV: Then it happened one evening that David arose from his bed and walked on the roof of the king’s house. And from the roof he saw a woman bathing, and the woman was very beautiful to behold.
10_11_2.KJV: And it came to pass in an eveningtide , that David arose from off his bed, and walked upon the roof of the king's house: and from the roof he saw a woman washing herself; and the woman was very beautiful to look upon.
10_11_2.ESV: It happened, late one afternoon, when David arose from his couch and was walking on the roof of the king's house, that he saw from the roof a woman bathing; and the woman was very beautiful.
10_11_2.NASB: Now when evening came David arose from his bed and walked around on the roof of the king's house, and from the roof he saw a woman bathing; and the woman was very beautiful in appearance.
10_11_2.NLT: Late one afternoon, after his midday rest, David got out of bed and was walking on the roof of the palace. As he looked out over the city, he noticed a woman of unusual beauty taking a bath.
10_11_2.WEB: At evening, David arose from his bed and walked on the roof of the king’s house. From the roof, he saw a woman bathing, and the woman was very beautiful to look at.
10_11_2.CSB17: One evening David got up from his bed and strolled around on the roof of the palace. From the roof he saw a woman bathing — a very beautiful woman.
10_11_2.AMP: One evening David got up from his couch and was walking on the [flat] roof of the king's palace, and from there he saw a woman bathing; and she was very beautiful in appearance.
10_11_2.MSG: One late afternoon, David got up from taking his nap and was strolling on the roof of the palace. From his vantage point on the roof he saw a woman bathing. The woman was stunningly beautiful.
10_11_2.NIV: One evening David got up from his bed and walked around on the roof of the palace. From the roof he saw a woman bathing. The woman was very beautiful,
10_11_2.NRSVCE: It happened, late one afternoon, when David rose from his couch and was walking about on the roof of the king’s house, that he saw from the roof a woman bathing; the woman was very beautiful.

10_11_3.NKJV: So David sent and inquired about the woman. And someone said, “Is this not Bathsheba, the daughter of Eliam, the wife of Uriah the Hittite?”
10_11_3.KJV: And David sent and enquired after the woman. And one said, Is not this Bathsheba, the daughter of Eliam, the wife of Uriah the Hittite?
10_11_3.ESV: And David sent and inquired about the woman. And one said, “Is not this Bathsheba, the daughter of Eliam, the wife of Uriah the Hittite?”
10_11_3.NASB: So David sent and inquired about the woman. And one said, 'Is this not Bathsheba, the daughter of Eliam, the wife of Uriah the Hittite?'
10_11_3.NLT: He sent someone to find out who she was, and he was told, “She is Bathsheba, the daughter of Eliam and the wife of Uriah the Hittite.”
10_11_3.WEB: David sent and inquired after the woman. One said, “Isn’t this Bathsheba, the daughter of Eliam, Uriah the Hittite’s wife?”
10_11_3.CSB17: So David sent someone to inquire about her, and he said, “Isn’t this Bathsheba, daughter of Eliam and wife of Uriah the Hethite?”
10_11_3.AMP: David sent word and inquired about the woman. Someone said, "Is this not Bathsheba, the daughter of Eliam, the wife of Uriah the Hittite?"
10_11_3.MSG: David sent to ask about her, and was told, "Isn't this Bathsheba, daughter of Eliam and wife of Uriah the Hittite?"
10_11_3.NIV: and David sent someone to find out about her. The man said, “Isn’t this Bathsheba, the daughter of Eliam and the wife of Uriah the Hittite?”
10_11_3.NRSVCE: David sent someone to inquire about the woman. It was reported, “This is Bathsheba daughter of Eliam, the wife of Uriah the Hittite.”

10_11_4.NKJV: Then David sent messengers, and took her; and she came to him, and he lay with her, for she was cleansed from her impurity; and she returned to her house.
10_11_4.KJV: And David sent messengers, and took her; and she came in unto him, and he lay with her; for she was purified from her uncleanness: and she returned unto her house. for she: or, and when she had purified herself, etc, she returned
10_11_4.ESV: So David sent messengers and took her, and she came to him, and he lay with her. (Now she had been purifying herself from her uncleanness.) Then she returned to her house.
10_11_4.NASB: David sent messengers and took her, and when she came to him, he lay with her; and when she had purified herself from her uncleanness, she returned to her house.
10_11_4.NLT: Then David sent messengers to get her; and when she came to the palace, he slept with her. She had just completed the purification rites after having her menstrual period. Then she returned home.
10_11_4.WEB: David sent messengers, and took her; and she came in to him, and he lay with her (for she was purified from her uncleanness); and she returned to her house.
10_11_4.CSB17: David sent messengers to get her, and when she came to him, he slept with her. Now she had just been purifying herself from her uncleanness. Afterward, she returned home.
10_11_4.AMP: David sent messengers and took her. When she came to him, he lay with her. And when she was purified from her uncleanness, she returned to her house.
10_11_4.MSG: David sent his agents to get her. After she arrived, he went to bed with her. (This occurred during the time of "purification" following her period.) Then she returned home.
10_11_4.NIV: Then David sent messengers to get her. She came to him, and he slept with her. (She had purified herself from her uncleanness.) Then she went back home.
10_11_4.NRSVCE: So David sent messengers to get her, and she came to him, and he lay with her. (Now she was purifying herself after her period.) Then she returned to her house.

10_11_5.NKJV: And the woman conceived; so she sent and told David, and said, “I am with child.”
10_11_5.KJV: And the woman conceived, and sent and told David, and said, I am with child.
10_11_5.ESV: And the woman conceived, and she sent and told David, “I am pregnant.”
10_11_5.NASB: The woman conceived; and she sent and told David, and said, 'I am pregnant.'
10_11_5.NLT: Later, when Bathsheba discovered that she was pregnant, she sent David a message, saying, “I’m pregnant.”
10_11_5.WEB: The woman conceived; and she sent and told David, and said, “I am with child.”
10_11_5.CSB17: The woman conceived and sent word to inform David: “I am pregnant.”
10_11_5.AMP: The woman conceived; and she sent word and told David, "I am pregnant."
10_11_5.MSG: Before long she realized she was pregnant.Later she sent word to David: "I'm pregnant."
10_11_5.NIV: The woman conceived and sent word to David, saying, “I am pregnant.”
10_11_5.NRSVCE: The woman conceived; and she sent and told David, “I am pregnant.”

10_11_6.NKJV: Then David sent to Joab, saying, “Send me Uriah the Hittite.” And Joab sent Uriah to David.
10_11_6.KJV: And David sent to Joab, saying, Send me Uriah the Hittite. And Joab sent Uriah to David.
10_11_6.ESV: So David sent word to Joab, “Send me Uriah the Hittite.” And Joab sent Uriah to David.
10_11_6.NASB: Then David sent to Joab, [saying], 'Send me Uriah the Hittite.' So Joab sent Uriah to David.
10_11_6.NLT: Then David sent word to Joab: “Send me Uriah the Hittite.” So Joab sent him to David.
10_11_6.WEB: David sent to Joab, “Send me Uriah the Hittite.” Joab sent Uriah to David.
10_11_6.CSB17: David sent orders to Joab: “Send me Uriah the Hethite.” So Joab sent Uriah to David.
10_11_6.AMP: Then David sent word to Joab, saying, "Send me Uriah the Hittite." So Joab sent Uriah to David.
10_11_6.MSG: David then got in touch with Joab: "Send Uriah the Hittite to me." Joab sent him.
10_11_6.NIV: So David sent this word to Joab: “Send me Uriah the Hittite.” And Joab sent him to David.
10_11_6.NRSVCE: So David sent word to Joab, “Send me Uriah the Hittite.” And Joab sent Uriah to David.

10_11_7.NKJV: When Uriah had come to him, David asked how Joab was doing, and how the people were doing, and how the war prospered.
10_11_7.KJV: And when Uriah was come unto him, David demanded of him how Joab did, and how the people did, and how the war prospered. how Joab: Heb. of the peace of, etc
10_11_7.ESV: When Uriah came to him, David asked how Joab was doing and how the people were doing and how the war was going.
10_11_7.NASB: When Uriah came to him, David asked concerning the welfare of Joab and the people and the state of the war.
10_11_7.NLT: When Uriah arrived, David asked him how Joab and the army were getting along and how the war was progressing.
10_11_7.WEB: When Uriah had come to him, David asked him how Joab did, and how the people fared, and how the war prospered.
10_11_7.CSB17: When Uriah came to him, David asked how Joab and the troops were doing and how the war was going.
10_11_7.AMP: When Uriah came to him, David asked him how Joab was, how the people were doing, and how the war was progressing.
10_11_7.MSG: When he arrived, David asked him for news from the front—how things were going with Joab and the troops and with the fighting.
10_11_7.NIV: When Uriah came to him, David asked him how Joab was, how the soldiers were and how the war was going.
10_11_7.NRSVCE: When Uriah came to him, David asked how Joab and the people fared, and how the war was going.

10_11_8.NKJV: And David said to Uriah, “Go down to your house and wash your feet.” So Uriah departed from the king’s house, and a gift of food from the king followed him.
10_11_8.KJV: And David said to Uriah, Go down to thy house, and wash thy feet. And Uriah departed out of the king's house, and there followed him a mess of meat from the king. followed: Heb. went out after him
10_11_8.ESV: Then David said to Uriah, “Go down to your house and wash your feet.” And Uriah went out of the king's house, and there followed him a present from the king.
10_11_8.NASB: Then David said to Uriah, 'Go down to your house, and wash your feet.' And Uriah went out of the king's house, and a present from the king was sent out after him.
10_11_8.NLT: Then he told Uriah, “Go on home and relax. ” David even sent a gift to Uriah after he had left the palace.
10_11_8.WEB: David said to Uriah, “Go down to your house and wash your feet.” Uriah departed out of the king’s house, and a gift from the king was sent after him.
10_11_8.CSB17: Then he said to Uriah, “Go down to your house and wash your feet.” So Uriah left the palace, and a gift from the king followed him.
10_11_8.AMP: Then David said to Uriah, "Go down to your house, and wash your feet (spend time at home)." Uriah left the king's palace, and a gift from the king was sent out after him.
10_11_8.MSG: Then he said to Uriah, "Go home. Have a refreshing bath and a good night's rest."After Uriah left the palace, an informant of the king was sent after him.
10_11_8.NIV: Then David said to Uriah, “Go down to your house and wash your feet.” So Uriah left the palace, and a gift from the king was sent after him.
10_11_8.NRSVCE: Then David said to Uriah, “Go down to your house, and wash your feet.” Uriah went out of the king’s house, and there followed him a present from the king.

10_11_9.NKJV: But Uriah slept at the door of the king’s house with all the servants of his lord, and did not go down to his house.
10_11_9.KJV: But Uriah slept at the door of the king's house with all the servants of his lord, and went not down to his house.
10_11_9.ESV: But Uriah slept at the door of the king's house with all the servants of his lord, and did not go down to his house.
10_11_9.NASB: But Uriah slept at the door of the king's house with all the servants of his lord, and did not go down to his house.
10_11_9.NLT: But Uriah didn’t go home. He slept that night at the palace entrance with the king’s palace guard.
10_11_9.WEB: But Uriah slept at the door of the king’s house with all the servants of his lord, and didn’t go down to his house.
10_11_9.CSB17: But Uriah slept at the door of the palace with all his master’s servants; he did not go down to his house.
10_11_9.AMP: But Uriah slept at the entrance of the king's palace with all the servants of his lord, and did not go down to his house.
10_11_9.MSG: But Uriah didn't go home. He slept that night at the palace entrance, along with the king's servants.
10_11_9.NIV: But Uriah slept at the entrance to the palace with all his master’s servants and did not go down to his house.
10_11_9.NRSVCE: But Uriah slept at the entrance of the king’s house with all the servants of his lord, and did not go down to his house.

10_11_10.NKJV: So when they told David, saying, “Uriah did not go down to his house,” David said to Uriah, “Did you not come from a journey? Why did you not go down to your house?”
10_11_10.KJV: And when they had told David, saying, Uriah went not down unto his house, David said unto Uriah, Camest thou not from thy journey? why then didst thou not go down unto thine house?
10_11_10.ESV: When they told David, “Uriah did not go down to his house,” David said to Uriah, “Have you not come from a journey? Why did you not go down to your house?”
10_11_10.NASB: Now when they told David, saying, 'Uriah did not go down to his house,' David said to Uriah, 'Have you not come from a journey? Why did you not go down to your house?'
10_11_10.NLT: When David heard that Uriah had not gone home, he summoned him and asked, “What’s the matter? Why didn’t you go home last night after being away for so long?”
10_11_10.WEB: When they had told David, saying, “Uriah didn’t go down to his house,” David said to Uriah, “Haven’t you come from a journey? Why didn’t you go down to your house?”
10_11_10.CSB17: When it was reported to David, “Uriah didn’t go home,” David questioned Uriah, “Haven’t you just come from a journey? Why didn’t you go home?”
10_11_10.AMP: When they told David, "Uriah did not go down to his house," David said to Uriah, "Have you not [just] come from a [long] journey? Why did you not go to your house?"
10_11_10.MSG: David was told that Uriah had not gone home. He asked Uriah, "Didn't you just come off a hard trip? So why didn't you go home?"
10_11_10.NIV: When David was told, “Uriah did not go home,” he asked him, “Haven’t you just come from a distance? Why didn’t you go home?”
10_11_10.NRSVCE: When they told David, “Uriah did not go down to his house,” David said to Uriah, “You have just come from a journey. Why did you not go down to your house?”

10_11_11.NKJV: And Uriah said to David, “The ark and Israel and Judah are dwelling in tents, and my lord Joab and the servants of my lord are encamped in the open fields. Shall I then go to my house to eat and drink, and to lie with my wife? As you live, and as your soul lives, I will not do this thing.”
10_11_11.KJV: And Uriah said unto David, The ark, and Israel, and Judah, abide in tents; and my lord Joab, and the servants of my lord, are encamped in the open fields; shall I then go into mine house, to eat and to drink, and to lie with my wife? as thou livest, and as thy soul liveth, I will not do this thing.
10_11_11.ESV: Uriah said to David, “The ark and Israel and Judah dwell in booths, and my lord Joab and the servants of my lord are camping in the open field. Shall I then go to my house, to eat and to drink and to lie with my wife? As you live, and as your soul lives, I will not do this thing.”

Shall I then go to my house to eat and to drink and to lie with my wife? By your life and the life of your soul, I will not do this thing.'
