10_18_23.NLT: “Yes, but let me go anyway,” he begged. Joab finally said, “All right, go ahead.” So Ahimaaz took the less demanding route by way of the plain and ran to Mahanaim ahead of the Ethiopian.
10_18_23.WEB: “But come what may,” he said, “I will run.” He said to him, “Run!” Then Ahimaaz ran by the way of the Plain, and outran the Cushite.
10_18_23.CSB17: “No matter what, I want to run!” “Then run!” Joab said to him. So Ahimaaz ran by way of the plain and outran the Cushite.
10_18_23.AMP: "But whatever happens, Let me run." So Joab said to him, "Run." Then Ahimaaz ran by the way of the plain [of the Jordan River] and outran the Cushite.
10_18_23.MSG: "I don't care; let me run.""Okay," said Joab, "run." So Ahimaaz ran, taking the lower valley road, and passed the Cushite.
10_18_23.NIV: He said, “Come what may, I want to run.”So Joab said, “Run!” Then Ahimaaz ran by way of the plain and outran the Cushite.
10_18_23.NRSVCE: “Come what may,” he said, “I will run.” So he said to him, “Run.” Then Ahimaaz ran by the way of the Plain, and outran the Cushite.

10_18_24.NKJV: Now David was sitting between the two gates. And the watchman went up to the roof over the gate, to the wall, lifted his eyes and looked, and there was a man, running alone.
10_18_24.KJV: And David sat between the two gates: and the watchman went up to the roof over the gate unto the wall, and lifted up his eyes, and looked, and behold a man running alone.
10_18_24.ESV: Now David was sitting between the two gates, and the watchman went up to the roof of the gate by the wall, and when he lifted up his eyes and looked, he saw a man running alone.
10_18_24.NASB: Now David was sitting between the two gates; and the watchman went up to the roof of the gate by the wall, and raised his eyes and looked, and behold, a man running by himself.
10_18_24.NLT: While David was sitting between the inner and outer gates of the town, the watchman climbed to the roof of the gateway by the wall. As he looked, he saw a lone man running toward them.
10_18_24.WEB: Now David was sitting between the two gates; and the watchman went up to the roof of the gate to the wall, and lifted up his eyes, and looked, and, behold, a man running alone.
10_18_24.CSB17: David was sitting between the city gates when the watchman went up to the roof of the city gate and over to the wall. The watchman looked out and saw a man running alone.
10_18_24.AMP: Now David was sitting between the two gates; and the lookout went up to the roof of the gate by the wall, and when he raised his eyes and looked, he saw a man running alone.
10_18_24.MSG: David was sitting between the two gates. The sentry had gone up to the top of the gate on the wall and looked around. He saw a solitary runner.
10_18_24.NIV: While David was sitting between the inner and outer gates, the watchman went up to the roof of the gateway by the wall. As he looked out, he saw a man running alone.
10_18_24.NRSVCE: Now David was sitting between the two gates. The sentinel went up to the roof of the gate by the wall, and when he looked up, he saw a man running alone.

10_18_25.NKJV: Then the watchman cried out and told the king. And the king said, “If he is alone, there is news in his mouth.” And he came rapidly and drew near.
10_18_25.KJV: And the watchman cried, and told the king. And the king said, If he be alone, there is tidings in his mouth. And he came apace, and drew near.
10_18_25.ESV: The watchman called out and told the king. And the king said, “If he is alone, there is news in his mouth.” And he drew nearer and nearer.
10_18_25.NASB: The watchman called and told the king. And the king said, 'If he is by himself there is good news in his mouth.' And he came nearer and nearer.
10_18_25.NLT: He shouted the news down to David, and the king replied, “If he is alone, he has news.” As the messenger came closer,
10_18_25.WEB: The watchman cried, and told the king. The king said, “If he is alone, there is news in his mouth.” He came closer and closer.
10_18_25.CSB17: He called out and told the king. The king said, “If he’s alone, he bears good news.” As the first runner came closer,
10_18_25.AMP: The lookout called down and told the king. The king said, "If he is alone, he has good news to tell." And he came nearer and nearer.
10_18_25.MSG: The sentry called down and told the king. The king said, "If he's alone, it must be good news!"As the runner came closer,
10_18_25.NIV: The watchman called out to the king and reported it.The king said, “If he is alone, he must have good news.” And the man came closer and closer.
10_18_25.NRSVCE: The sentinel shouted and told the king. The king said, “If he is alone, there are tidings in his mouth.” He kept coming, and drew near.

10_18_26.NKJV: Then the watchman saw another man running, and the watchman called to the gatekeeper and said, “There is another man, running alone!” And the king said, “He also brings news.”
10_18_26.KJV: And the watchman saw another man running: and the watchman called unto the porter, and said, Behold another man running alone. And the king said, He also bringeth tidings.
10_18_26.ESV: The watchman saw another man running. And the watchman called to the gate and said, “See, another man running alone!” The king said, “He also brings news.”
10_18_26.NASB: Then the watchman saw another man running; and the watchman called to the gatekeeper and said, 'Behold, [another] man running by himself.' And the king said, 'This one also is bringing good news.'
10_18_26.NLT: the watchman saw another man running toward them. He shouted down, “Here comes another one!” The king replied, “He also will have news.”
10_18_26.WEB: The watchman saw another man running; and the watchman called to the porter, and said, “Behold, a man running alone!” The king said, “He also brings news.”
10_18_26.CSB17: the watchman saw another man running. He called out to the gatekeeper, “Look! Another man is running alone!” “This one is also bringing good news,” said the king.
10_18_26.AMP: Then the lookout saw another man running, and he called to the gatekeeper and said, "Look, another man running alone." The king said, "He also is bringing good news."
10_18_26.MSG: the sentry saw another runner and called down to the gate, "Another runner all by himself."And the king said, "This also must be good news."
10_18_26.NIV: Then the watchman saw another man running, and he called down to the gatekeeper, “Look, another man running alone!”The king said, “He must be bringing good news, too.”
10_18_26.NRSVCE: Then the sentinel saw another man running; and the sentinel called to the gatekeeper and said, “See, another man running alone!” The king said, “He also is bringing tidings.”

10_18_27.NKJV: So the watchman said, “I think the running of the first is like the running of Ahimaaz the son of Zadok.” And the king said, “He is a good man, and comes with good news.”
10_18_27.KJV: And the watchman said, Me thinketh the running of the foremost is like the running of Ahimaaz the son of Zadok. And the king said, He is a good man, and cometh with good tidings. Me: Heb. I see the running
10_18_27.ESV: The watchman said, “I think the running of the first is like the running of Ahimaaz the son of Zadok.” And the king said, “He is a good man and comes with good news.”
10_18_27.NASB: The watchman said, 'I think the running of the first one is like the running of Ahimaaz the son of Zadok.' And the king said, 'This is a good man and comes with good news.'
10_18_27.NLT: “The first man runs like Ahimaaz son of Zadok,” the watchman said. “He is a good man and comes with good news,” the king replied.
10_18_27.WEB: The watchman said, “I think the running of the first one is like the running of Ahimaaz the son of Zadok.” The king said, “He is a good man, and comes with good news.”
10_18_27.CSB17: The watchman said, “The way the first man runs looks to me like the way Ahimaaz son of Zadok runs.” “This is a good man; he comes with good news,” the king commented.
10_18_27.AMP: The lookout said, "I think the man in front runs like Ahimaaz the son of Zadok." The king said, "He is a good man and is coming with good news."
10_18_27.MSG: Then the sentry said, "I can see the first man now; he runs like Ahimaaz son of Zadok.""He's a good man," said the king. "He's bringing good news for sure."
10_18_27.NIV: The watchman said, “It seems to me that the first one runs like Ahimaaz son of Zadok.”“He’s a good man,” the king said. “He comes with good news.”
10_18_27.NRSVCE: The sentinel said, “I think the running of the first one is like the running of Ahimaaz son of Zadok.” The king said, “He is a good man, and comes with good tidings.”

10_18_28.NKJV: So Ahimaaz called out and said to the king, “All is well!” Then he bowed down with his face to the earth before the king, and said, “Blessed be the LORD your God, who has delivered up the men who raised their hand against my lord the king!”
10_18_28.KJV: And Ahimaaz called, and said unto the king, All is well. And he fell down to the earth upon his face before the king, and said, Blessed be the LORD thy God, which hath delivered up the men that lifted up their hand against my lord the king. All: or, Peace be to thee: Heb. Peace delivered: Heb. shut up
10_18_28.ESV: Then Ahimaaz cried out to the king, “All is well.” And he bowed before the king with his face to the earth and said, “Blessed be the LORD your God, who has delivered up the men who raised their hand against my lord the king.”
10_18_28.NASB: Ahimaaz called and said to the king, 'All is well.' And he prostrated himself before the king with his face to the ground. And he said, 'Blessed is the LORD your God, who has delivered up the men who lifted their hands against my lord the king.'
10_18_28.NLT: Then Ahimaaz cried out to the king, “Everything is all right!” He bowed before the king with his face to the ground and said, “Praise to the LORD your God, who has handed over the rebels who dared to stand against my lord the king.”
10_18_28.WEB: Ahimaaz called, and said to the king, “All is well.” He bowed himself before the king with his face to the earth, and said, “Blessed is Yahweh your God, who has delivered up the men who lifted up their hand against my lord the king!”
10_18_28.CSB17: Ahimaaz called out to the king, “All is well,” and paid homage to the king with his face to the ground. He continued, “Blessed be the LORD your God! He delivered up the men who rebelled against my lord the king.”
10_18_28.AMP: And Ahimaaz called out and said to the king, " All is well." And he bowed before the king with his face to the ground and said, "Blessed be the LORD your God, who has handed over the men who lifted up their hands [to fight] against my lord the king."
10_18_28.MSG: Then Ahimaaz called out and said to the king, "Peace!" Then he bowed deeply before the king, his face to the ground. "Blessed be your God; he has handed over the men who rebelled against my master the king."
10_18_28.NIV: Then Ahimaaz called out to the king, “All is well!” He bowed down before the king with his face to the ground and said, “Praise be to the Lord your God! He has delivered up the men who lifted their hands against my lord the king.”
10_18_28.NRSVCE: Then Ahimaaz cried out to the king, “All is well!” He prostrated himself before the king with his face to the ground, and said, “Blessed be the LORD your God, who has delivered up the men who raised their hand against my lord the king.”

10_18_29.NKJV: The king said, “Is the young man Absalom safe?” Ahimaaz answered, “When Joab sent the king’s servant and me your servant, I saw a great tumult, but I did not know what it was about.”
10_18_29.KJV: And the king said, Is the young man Absalom safe? And Ahimaaz answered, When Joab sent the king's servant, and me thy servant, I saw a great tumult, but I knew not what it was. Is: Heb. Is there peace?
10_18_29.ESV: And the king said, “Is it well with the young man Absalom?” Ahimaaz answered, “When Joab sent the king's servant, your servant, I saw a great commotion, but I do not know what it was.”
10_18_29.NASB: The king said, 'Is it well with the young man Absalom?' And Ahimaaz answered, 'When Joab sent the king's servant, and your servant, I saw a great tumult, but I did not know what [it was].'
10_18_29.NLT: “What about young Absalom?” the king demanded. “Is he all right?” Ahimaaz replied, “When Joab told me to come, there was a lot of commotion. But I didn’t know what was happening.”
10_18_29.WEB: The king said, “Is it well with the young man Absalom?” Ahimaaz answered, “When Joab sent the king’s servant, even me your servant, I saw a great tumult, but I don’t know what it was.”
10_18_29.CSB17: The king asked, “Is the young man Absalom all right?” Ahimaaz replied, “When Joab sent the king’s servant and your servant, I saw a big disturbance, but I don’t know what it was.”
10_18_29.AMP: The king asked, "Is the young man Absalom safe?" Ahimaaz answered, "When Joab sent the king's servant, and your servant, I saw a great turmoil, but I do not know what it was about."
10_18_29.MSG: The king asked, "But is the young man Absalom all right?"Ahimaaz said, "I saw a huge ruckus just as Joab was sending me off, but I don't know what it was about."
10_18_29.NIV: The king asked, “Is the young man Absalom safe?”Ahimaaz answered, “I saw great confusion just as Joab was about to send the king’s servant and me, your servant, but I don’t know what it was.”
10_18_29.NRSVCE: The king said, “Is it well with the young man Absalom?” Ahimaaz answered, “When Joab sent your servant, I saw a great tumult, but I do not know what it was.”

10_18_30.NKJV: And the king said, “Turn aside and stand here.” So he turned aside and stood still.
10_18_30.KJV: And the king said unto him, Turn aside, and stand here. And he turned aside, and stood still.
10_18_30.ESV: And the king said, “Turn aside and stand here.” So he turned aside and stood still.
10_18_30.NASB: Then the king said, 'Turn aside and stand here.' So he turned aside and stood still.
10_18_30.NLT: “Wait here,” the king told him. So Ahimaaz stepped aside.
10_18_30.WEB: The king said, “Turn aside, and stand here.” He turned aside, and stood still.
10_18_30.CSB17: The king said, “Move aside and stand here.” So he stood to one side.
10_18_30.AMP: The king told him, "Step aside; stand here." And he stepped aside and stood still.
10_18_30.MSG: The king said, "Step aside and stand over there." So he stepped aside.
10_18_30.NIV: The king said, “Stand aside and wait here.” So he stepped aside and stood there.
10_18_30.NRSVCE: The king said, “Turn aside, and stand here.” So he turned aside, and stood still.

10_18_31.NKJV: Just then the Cushite came, and the Cushite said, “There is good news, my lord the king! For the LORD has avenged you this day of all those who rose against you.”
10_18_31.KJV: And, behold, Cushi came; and Cushi said, Tidings, my lord the king: for the LORD hath avenged thee this day of all them that rose up against thee. Tidings: Heb. Tidings is brought
10_18_31.ESV: And behold, the Cushite came, and the Cushite said, “Good news for my lord the king! For the LORD has delivered you this day from the hand of all who rose up against you.”
10_18_31.NASB: Behold, the Cushite arrived, and the Cushite said, 'Let my lord the king receive good news, for the LORD has freed you this day from the hand of all those who rose up against you.'
10_18_31.NLT: Then the man from Ethiopia arrived and said, “I have good news for my lord the king. Today the LORD has rescued you from all those who rebelled against you.”
10_18_31.WEB: Behold, the Cushite came. The Cushite said, “News for my lord the king, for Yahweh has avenged you today of all those who rose up against you.”
10_18_31.CSB17: Just then the Cushite came and said, “May my lord the king hear the good news: The LORD has vindicated you today by freeing you from all who rise against you!”
10_18_31.AMP: Behold, the Cushite (Ethiopian) arrived, and said, "Let my lord the king receive good news, for the LORD has vindicated you today by rescuing you from the hand (power) of all those who stood against you."
10_18_31.MSG: Then the Cushite arrived and said, "Good news, my master and king! God has given victory today over all those who rebelled against you!"
10_18_31.NIV: Then the Cushite arrived and said, “My lord the king, hear the good news! The Lord has delivered you today from all who rose up against you.”
10_18_31.NRSVCE: Then the Cushite came; and the Cushite said, “Good tidings for my lord the king! For the LORD has vindicated you this day, delivering you from the power of all who rose up against you.”

10_18_32.NKJV: And the king said to the Cushite, “Is the young man Absalom safe?” So the Cushite answered, “May the enemies of my lord the king, and all who rise against you to do harm, be like that young man!”
10_18_32.KJV: And the king said unto Cushi, Is the young man Absalom safe? And Cushi answered, The enemies of my lord the king, and all that rise against thee to do thee hurt, be as that young man is.
10_18_32.ESV: The king said to the Cushite, “Is it well with the young man Absalom?” And the Cushite answered, “May the enemies of my lord the king and all who rise up against you for evil be like that young man.”
10_18_32.NASB: Then the king said to the Cushite, 'Is it well with the young man Absalom?' And the Cushite answered, 'Let the enemies of my lord the king, and all who rise up against you for evil, be as that young man!'
10_18_32.NLT: “What about young Absalom?” the king demanded. “Is he all right?” And the Ethiopian replied, “May all of your enemies, my lord the king, both now and in the future, share the fate of that young man!”
10_18_32.WEB: The king said to the Cushite, “Is it well with the young man Absalom?” The Cushite answered, “May the enemies of my lord the king, and all who rise up against you to do you harm, be as that young man is.”
10_18_32.CSB17: The king asked the Cushite, “Is the young man Absalom all right?” The Cushite replied, “I wish that the enemies of my lord the king, along with all who rise up against you with evil intent, would become like that young man.”
10_18_32.AMP: The king asked the Cushite, "Is the young man Absalom [my son] safe?" The Cushite replied, "May the enemies of my lord the king, and all those who rise against you to do evil, be [dead] like that young man is."
10_18_32.MSG: "But," said the king, "is the young man Absalom all right?"And the Cushite replied, "Would that all of the enemies of my master the king and all who maliciously rose against you end up like that young man."
10_18_32.NIV: The king asked the Cushite, “Is the young man Absalom safe?”The Cushite replied, “May the enemies of my lord the king and all who rise up to harm you be like that young man.”
10_18_32.NRSVCE: The king said to the Cushite, “Is it well with the young man Absalom?” The Cushite answered, “May the enemies of my lord the king, and all who rise up to do you harm, be like that young man.”

10_18_33.NKJV: Then the king was deeply moved, and went up to the chamber over the gate, and wept. And as he went, he said thus: “O my son Absalom—my son, my son Absalom—if only I had died in your place! O Absalom my son, my son!”
10_18_33.KJV: And the king was much moved, and went up to the chamber over the gate, and wept: and as he went, thus he said, O my son Absalom, my son, my son Absalom! would God I had died for thee, O Absalom, my son, my son!
10_18_33.ESV: And the king was deeply moved and went up to the chamber over the gate and wept. And as he went, he said, “O my son Absalom, my son, my son Absalom! Would I had died instead of you, O Absalom, my son, my son!”
10_18_33.NASB: The king was deeply moved and went up to the chamber over the gate and wept. And thus he said as he walked, 'O my son Absalom, my son, my son Absalom! Would I had died instead of you, O Absalom, my son, my son!'
10_18_33.NLT: The king was overcome with emotion. He went up to the room over the gateway and burst into tears. And as he went, he cried, “O my son Absalom! My son, my son Absalom! If only I had died instead of you! O Absalom, my son, my son.”
10_18_33.WEB: The king was much moved, and went up to the room over the gate, and wept. As he went, he said, “My son Absalom! My son, my son Absalom! I wish I had died for you, Absalom, my son, my son!”
10_18_33.CSB17: The king was deeply moved and went up to the chamber above the city gate and wept. As he walked, he cried, “My son Absalom! My son, my son Absalom! If only I had died instead of you, Absalom, my son, my son!”
10_18_33.AMP: The king was deeply moved and went to the upper room over the gate and wept [in sorrow]. And this is what he said as he walked: "O my son Absalom, my son, my son Absalom! How I wish that I had died instead of you, O Absalom, my son, my son!"
10_18_33.MSG: The king was stunned. Heartbroken, he went up to the room over the gate and wept. As he wept he cried out,O my son Absalom, my dear, dear son Absalom!Why not me rather than you, my death and not yours,O Absalom, my dear, dear son!
10_18_33.NIV: The king was shaken. He went up to the room over the gateway and wept. As he went, he said: “O my son Absalom! My son, my son Absalom! If only I had died instead of you—O Absalom, my son, my son!”
10_18_33.NRSVCE: The king was deeply moved, and went up to the chamber over the gate, and wept; and as he went, he said, “O my son Absalom, my son, my son Absalom! Would I had died instead of you, O Absalom, my son, my son!”

10_19_1.NKJV: And Joab was told, “Behold, the king is weeping and mourning for Absalom.”
10_19_1.KJV: And it was told Joab, Behold, the king weepeth and mourneth for Absalom.
10_19_1.ESV: It was told Joab, “Behold, the king is weeping and mourning for Absalom.”
10_19_1.NASB: Then it was told Joab, 'Behold, the king is weeping and mourns for Absalom.'
10_19_1.NLT: Word soon reached Joab that the king was weeping and mourning for Absalom.
10_19_1.WEB: Joab was told, “Behold, the king weeps and mourns for Absalom.”
10_19_1.CSB17: It was reported to Joab, “The king is weeping. He’s mourning over Absalom.”
10_19_1.AMP: It was told to Joab, "Behold, the king is weeping and mourning for Absalom."
10_19_1.MSG: Joab was told that David was weeping and lamenting over Absalom.
10_19_1.NIV: Joab was told, “The king is weeping and mourning for Absalom.”
10_19_1.NRSVCE: It was told Joab, “The king is weeping and mourning for Absalom.”

10_19_2.NKJV: So the victory that day was turned into mourning for all the people. For the people heard it said that day, “The king is grieved for his son.”
10_19_2.KJV: And the victory that day was turned into mourning unto all the people: for the people heard say that day how the king was grieved for his son. victory: Heb. salvation, or, deliverance
10_19_2.ESV: So the victory that day was turned into mourning for all the people, for the people heard that day, “The king is grieving for his son.”
10_19_2.NASB: The victory that day was turned to mourning for all the people, for the people heard [it] said that day, 'The king is grieved for his son.'
10_19_2.NLT: As all the people heard of the king’s deep grief for his son, the joy of that day’s victory was turned into deep sadness.
10_19_2.WEB: The victory that day was turned into mourning among all the people; for the people heard it said that day, “The king grieves for his son.”
10_19_2.CSB17: That day’s victory was turned into mourning for all the troops because on that day the troops heard, “The king is grieving over his son.”
10_19_2.AMP: So the victory on that day was turned into mourning for all the people, for the people heard it said on that day, "The king grieves for his son."
10_19_2.MSG: The day's victory turned into a day of mourning as word passed through the army, "David is grieving over his son."
10_19_2.NIV: And for the whole army the victory that day was turned into mourning, because on that day the troops heard it said, “The king is grieving for his son.”
10_19_2.NRSVCE: So the victory that day was turned into mourning for all the troops; for the troops heard that day, “The king is grieving for his son.”

10_19_3.NKJV: And the people stole back into the city that day, as people who are ashamed steal away when they flee in battle.
10_19_3.KJV: And the people gat them by stealth that day into the city, as people being ashamed steal away when they flee in battle.
10_19_3.ESV: And the people stole into the city that day as people steal in who are ashamed when they flee in battle.
10_19_3.NASB: So the people went by stealth into the city that day, as people who are humiliated steal away when they flee in battle.
10_19_3.NLT: They crept back into the town that day as though they were ashamed and had deserted in battle.
10_19_3.WEB: The people sneaked into the city that day, as people who are ashamed steal away when they flee in battle.
10_19_3.CSB17: So they returned to the city quietly that day like troops come in when they are humiliated after fleeing in battle.
10_19_3.AMP: The people stole into the city [of Mahanaim] that day, as people who are humiliated and ashamed steal away when they retreat in battle.
10_19_3.MSG: The army straggled back to the city that day demoralized, dragging their tails.
10_19_3.NIV: The men stole into the city that day as men steal in who are ashamed when they flee from battle.
10_19_3.NRSVCE: The troops stole into the city that day as soldiers steal in who are ashamed when they flee in battle.

10_19_4.NKJV: But the king covered his face, and the king cried out with a loud voice, “O my son Absalom! O Absalom, my son, my son!”
10_19_4.KJV: But the king covered his face, and the king cried with a loud voice, O my son Absalom, O Absalom, my son, my son!
10_19_4.ESV: The king covered his face, and the king cried with a loud voice, “O my son Absalom, O Absalom, my son, my son!”
10_19_4.NASB: The king covered his face and cried out with a loud voice, 'O my son Absalom, O Absalom, my son, my son!'
10_19_4.NLT: The king covered his face with his hands and kept on crying, “O my son Absalom! O Absalom, my son, my son!”
10_19_4.WEB: The king covered his face, and the king cried with a loud voice, “My son Absalom, Absalom, my son, my son!”
10_19_4.CSB17: But the king covered his face and cried loudly, “My son Absalom! Absalom, my son, my son!”
10_19_4.AMP: But the king covered his face and cried out with a loud voice, "O my son Absalom, O Absalom, my son, my son!"
10_19_4.MSG: And the king held his face in his hands and lamented loudly,O my son Absalom,Absalom my dear, dear son!
10_19_4.NIV: The king covered his face and cried aloud, “O my son Absalom! O Absalom, my son, my son!”
10_19_4.NRSVCE: The king covered his face, and the king cried with a loud voice, “O my son Absalom, O Absalom, my son, my son!”

10_19_5.NKJV: Then Joab came into the house to the king, and said, “Today you have disgraced all your servants who today have saved your life, the lives of your sons and daughters, the lives of your wives and the lives of your concubines,
10_19_5.KJV: And Joab came into the house to the king, and said, Thou hast shamed this day the faces of all thy servants, which this day have saved thy life, and the lives of thy sons and of thy daughters, and the lives of thy wives, and the lives of thy concubines;
10_19_5.ESV: Then Joab came into the house to the king and said, “You have today covered with shame the faces of all your servants, who have this day saved your life and the lives of your sons and your daughters and the lives of your wives and your concubines,
10_19_5.NASB: Then Joab came into the house to the king and said, 'Today you have covered with shame the faces of all your servants, who today have saved your life and the lives of your sons and daughters, the lives of your wives, and the lives of your concubines,
10_19_5.NLT: Then Joab went to the king’s room and said to him, “We saved your life today and the lives of your sons, your daughters, and your wives and concubines. Yet you act like this, making us feel ashamed of ourselves.
10_19_5.WEB: Joab came into the house to the king, and said, “Today you have shamed the faces of all your servants, who today have saved your life, and the lives of your sons and of your daughters, and the lives of your wives, and the lives of your concubines;
10_19_5.CSB17: Then Joab went into the house to the king and said, “Today you have shamed all your soldiers — those who saved your life as well as your sons, your wives, and your concubines —
10_19_5.AMP: Then Joab came into the house to the king and said, "Today you have put all your servants to shame who this day have saved your life and the lives of your sons and your daughters, and the lives of your wives and concubines.
10_19_5.MSG: But in private Joab rebuked the king: "Now you've done it—knocked the wind out of your loyal servants who have just saved your life, to say nothing of the lives of your sons and daughters, wives and concubines.
10_19_5.NIV: Then Joab went into the house to the king and said, “Today you have humiliated all your men, who have just saved your life and the lives of your sons and daughters and the lives of your wives and concubines.
10_19_5.NRSVCE: Then Joab came into the house to the king, and said, “Today you have covered with shame the faces of all your officers who have saved your life today, and the lives of your sons and your daughters, and the lives of your wives and your concubines,

10_19_6.NKJV: in that you love your enemies and hate your friends. For you have declared today that you regard neither princes nor servants; for today I perceive that if Absalom had lived and all of us had died today, then it would have pleased you well.
10_19_6.KJV: In that thou lovest thine enemies, and hatest thy friends. For thou hast declared this day, that thou regardest neither princes nor servants: for this day I perceive, that if Absalom had lived, and all we had died this day, then it had pleased thee well . in: Heb. By loving, etc that thou regardest: Heb. that princes or servants are not to thee
10_19_6.ESV: because you love those who hate you and hate those who love you. For you have made it clear today that commanders and servants are nothing to you, for today I know that if Absalom were alive and all of us were dead today, then you would be pleased.
10_19_6.NASB: by loving those who hate you, and by hating those who love you. For you have shown today that princes and servants are nothing to you; for I know this day that if Absalom were alive and all of us were dead today, then you would be pleased.
10_19_6.NLT: You seem to love those who hate you and hate those who love you. You have made it clear today that your commanders and troops mean nothing to you. It seems that if Absalom had lived and all of us had died, you would be pleased.
10_19_6.WEB: in that you love those who hate you, and hate those who love you. For you have declared today, that princes and servants are nothing to you. For today I perceive that if Absalom had lived, and all we had died today, then it would have pleased you well.
10_19_6.CSB17: by loving your enemies and hating those who love you! Today you have made it clear that the commanders and soldiers mean nothing to you. In fact, today I know that if Absalom were alive and all of us were dead, it would be fine with you!
10_19_6.AMP: For you love those who hate you and hate those who love you. For you have shown today that commanders and servants are nothing to you; for today I know that if Absalom had lived and all the rest of us had died today, then you would be pleased.
10_19_6.MSG: What is this—loving those who hate you and hating those who love you? Your actions give a clear message: officers and soldiers mean nothing to you. You know that if Absalom were alive right now, we'd all be dead—would that make you happy?
10_19_6.NIV: You love those who hate you and hate those who love you. You have made it clear today that the commanders and their men mean nothing to you. I see that you would be pleased if Absalom were alive today and all of us were dead.
10_19_6.NRSVCE: for love of those who hate you and for hatred of those who love you. You have made it clear today that commanders and officers are nothing to you; for I perceive that if Absalom were alive and all of us were dead today, then you would be pleased.

10_19_7.NKJV: Now therefore, arise, go out and speak comfort to your servants. For I swear by the LORD, if you do not go out, not one will stay with you this night. And that will be worse for you than all the evil that has befallen you from your youth until now.”
10_19_7.KJV: Now therefore arise, go forth, and speak comfortably unto thy servants: for I swear by the LORD, if thou go not forth, there will not tarry one with thee this night: and that will be worse unto thee than all the evil that befell thee from thy youth until now. comfortably: Heb. to the heart of thy servants
10_19_7.ESV: Now therefore arise, go out and speak kindly to your servants, for I swear by the LORD, if you do not go, not a man will stay with you this night, and this will be worse for you than all the evil that has come upon you from your youth until now.”
10_19_7.NASB: 'Now therefore arise, go out and speak kindly to your servants, for I swear by the LORD, if you do not go out, surely not a man will pass the night with you, and this will be worse for you than all the evil that has come upon you from your youth until now.'
10_19_7.NLT: Now go out there and congratulate your troops, for I swear by the LORD that if you don’t go out, not a single one of them will remain here tonight. Then you will be worse off than ever before.”
10_19_7.WEB: Now therefore arise, go out, and speak to comfort your servants; for I swear by Yahweh, if you don’t go out, not a man will stay with you this night. That would be worse to you than all the evil that has happened to you from your youth until now.”
10_19_7.CSB17: “Now get up! Go out and encourage your soldiers, for I swear by the LORD that if you don’t go out, not a man will remain with you tonight. This will be worse for you than all the trouble that has come to you from your youth until now!”
10_19_7.AMP: So now stand up, go out and speak kindly and encouragingly to your servants; for I swear by the LORD that if you do not go out, not a man will stay with you tonight. And this will be worse for you than all the evil that has come upon you from your youth until now."
10_19_7.MSG: Get hold of yourself; get out there and put some heart into your servants! I swear to God that if you don't go to them they'll desert; not a soldier will be left here by nightfall. And that will be the worst thing that has happened yet."
10_19_7.NIV: Now go out and encourage your men. I swear by the Lord that if you don’t go out, not a man will be left with you by nightfall. This will be worse for you than all the calamities that have come upon you from your youth till now.”
10_19_7.NRSVCE: So go out at once and speak kindly to your servants; for I swear by the LORD, if you do not go, not a man will stay with you this night; and this will be worse for you than any disaster that has come upon you from your youth until now.”

10_19_8.NKJV: Then the king arose and sat in the gate. And they told all the people, saying, “There is the king, sitting in the gate.” So all the people came before the king. For everyone of Israel had fled to his tent.
10_19_8.KJV: Then the king arose, and sat in the gate. And they told unto all the people, saying, Behold, the king doth sit in the gate. And all the people came before the king: for Israel had fled every man to his tent.
10_19_8.ESV: Then the king arose and took his seat in the gate. And the people were all told, “Behold, the king is sitting in the gate.” And all the people came before the king. David Returns to Jerusalem Now Israel had fled every man to his own home.
10_19_8.NASB: So the king arose and sat in the gate. When they told all the people, saying, 'Behold, the king is sitting in the gate,' then all the people came before the king. Now Israel had fled, each to his tent.
10_19_8.NLT: So the king went out and took his seat at the town gate, and as the news spread throughout the town that he was there, everyone went to him. Meanwhile, the Israelites who had supported Absalom fled to their homes.
10_19_8.WEB: Then the king arose, and sat in the gate. They told to all the people, saying, “Behold, the king is sitting in the gate.” All the people came before the king. Now Israel had fled every man to his tent.
10_19_8.CSB17: So the king got up and sat in the city gate, and all the people were told: “Look, the king is sitting in the city gate.” Then they all came into the king’s presence. Meanwhile, each Israelite had fled to his tent.
10_19_8.AMP: Then the king stood and sat at the gate [of Mahanaim]. And they told all the people, "The king is sitting at the gate," and all the people came before the king. But Israel [Absalom's troops] had fled, every man to his tent.
10_19_8.MSG: So the king came out and took his place at the city gate. Soon everyone knew: "Oh, look! The king has come out to receive us." And his whole army came and presented itself to the king. But the Israelites had fled the field of battle and gone home.
10_19_8.NIV: So the king got up and took his seat in the gateway. When the men were told, “The king is sitting in the gateway,” they all came before him.David Returns to JerusalemMeanwhile, the Israelites had fled to their homes.
10_19_8.NRSVCE: Then the king got up and took his seat in the gate. The troops were all told, “See, the king is sitting in the gate”; and all the troops came before the king. David Recalled to Jerusalem Meanwhile, all the Israelites had fled to their homes.

10_19_9.NKJV: Now all the people were in a dispute throughout all the tribes of Israel, saying, “The king saved us from the hand of our enemies, he delivered us from the hand of the Philistines, and now he has fled from the land because of Absalom.
10_19_9.KJV: And all the people were at strife throughout all the tribes of Israel, saying, The king saved us out of the hand of our enemies, and he delivered us out of the hand of the Philistines; and now he is fled out of the land for Absalom.
10_19_9.ESV: And all the people were arguing throughout all the tribes of Israel, saying, “The king delivered us from the hand of our enemies and saved us from the hand of the Philistines, and now he has fled out of the land from Absalom.
10_19_9.NASB: All the people were quarreling throughout all the tribes of Israel, saying, 'The king delivered us from the hand of our enemies and saved us from the hand of the Philistines, but now he has fled out of the land from Absalom.
10_19_9.NLT: And throughout all the tribes of Israel there was much discussion and argument going on. The people were saying, “The king rescued us from our enemies and saved us from the Philistines, but Absalom chased him out of the country.
10_19_9.WEB: All the people were at strife throughout all the tribes of Israel, saying, “The king delivered us out of the hand of our enemies, and he saved us out of the hand of the Philistines; and now he has fled out of the land from Absalom.
10_19_9.CSB17: People throughout all the tribes of Israel were arguing among themselves, saying, “The king rescued us from the grasp of our enemies, and he saved us from the grasp of the Philistines, but now he has fled from the land because of Absalom.
10_19_9.AMP: All the people were quarreling throughout the tribes of Israel, saying, "The king rescued us from the hands of our enemies, and he saved us from the hands of the Philistines, but now he has fled out of the land from Absalom.
10_19_9.MSG: Meanwhile, the whole populace was now complaining to its leaders, "Wasn't it the king who saved us time and again from our enemies, and rescued us from the Philistines? And now he has had to flee the country on account of Absalom.
10_19_9.NIV: Throughout the tribes of Israel, the people were all arguing with each other, saying, “The king delivered us from the hand of our enemies; he is the one who rescued us from the hand of the Philistines. But now he has fled the country because of Absalom;
10_19_9.NRSVCE: All the people were disputing throughout all the tribes of Israel, saying, “The king delivered us from the hand of our enemies, and saved us from the hand of the Philistines; and now he has fled out of the land because of Absalom.

10_19_10.NKJV: But Absalom, whom we anointed over us, has died in battle. Now therefore, why do you say nothing about bringing back the king?”
10_19_10.KJV: And Absalom, whom we anointed over us, is dead in battle. Now therefore why speak ye not a word of bringing the king back? speak: Heb. are ye silent?
10_19_10.ESV: But Absalom, whom we anointed over us, is dead in battle. Now therefore why do you say nothing about bringing the king back?”
10_19_10.NASB: 'However, Absalom, whom we anointed over us, has died in battle. Now then, why are you silent about bringing the king back?'
10_19_10.NLT: Now Absalom, whom we anointed to rule over us, is dead. Why not ask David to come back and be our king again?”
10_19_10.WEB: Absalom, whom we anointed over us, is dead in battle. Now therefore why don’t you speak a word of bringing the king back?”
10_19_10.CSB17: But Absalom, the man we anointed over us, has died in battle. So why do you say nothing about restoring the king?”
10_19_10.AMP: And Absalom, whom we anointed over us, has died in battle. So now, why are you [leaders] doing nothing about bringing back the king?"
10_19_10.MSG: And now this Absalom whom we made king is dead in battle. So what are you waiting for? Why don't you bring the king back?"
10_19_10.NIV: and Absalom, whom we anointed to rule over us, has died in battle. So why do you say nothing about bringing the king back?”
10_19_10.NRSVCE: But Absalom, whom we anointed over us, is dead in battle. Now therefore why do you say nothing about bringing the king back?”

10_19_11.NKJV: So King David sent to Zadok and Abiathar the priests, saying, “Speak to the elders of Judah, saying, ‘Why are you the last to bring the king back to his house, since the words of all Israel have come to the king, to his very house?
10_19_11.KJV: And king David sent to Zadok and to Abiathar the priests, saying, Speak unto the elders of Judah, saying, Why are ye the last to bring the king back to his house? seeing the speech of all Israel is come to the king, even to his house.
10_19_11.ESV: And King David sent this message to Zadok and Abiathar the priests: “Say to the elders of Judah, ‘Why should you be the last to bring the king back to his house, when the word of all Israel has come to the king?
10_19_11.NASB: Then King David sent to Zadok and Abiathar the priests, saying, 'Speak to the elders of Judah, saying, 'Why are you the last to bring the king back to his house, since the word of all Israel has come to the king, [even] to his house?
10_19_11.NLT: Then King David sent Zadok and Abiathar, the priests, to say to the elders of Judah, “Why are you the last ones to welcome back the king into his palace? For I have heard that all Israel is ready.
10_19_11.WEB: King David sent to Zadok and to Abiathar the priests, saying, “Speak to the elders of Judah, saying, ‘Why are you the last to bring the king back to his house? Since the speech of all Israel has come to the king, to return him to his house.
10_19_11.CSB17: King David sent word to the priests Zadok and Abiathar: “Say to the elders of Judah, ‘Why should you be the last to restore the king to his palace? The talk of all Israel has reached the king at his house.

10_19_11.MSG: When David heard what was being said, he sent word to Zadok and Abiathar, the priests, "Ask the elders of Judah, 'Why are you so laggard in bringing the king back home?
10_19_11.NIV: King David sent this message to Zadok and Abiathar, the priests: “Ask the elders of Judah, ‘Why should you be the last to bring the king back to his palace, since what is being said throughout Israel has reached the king at his quarters?
10_19_11.NRSVCE: King David sent this message to the priests Zadok and Abiathar, “Say to the elders of Judah, ‘Why should you be the last to bring the king back to his house? The talk of all Israel has come to the king.

10_19_12.NKJV: You are my brethren, you are my bone and my flesh. Why then are you the last to bring back the king?’
10_19_12.KJV: Ye are my brethren, ye are my bones and my flesh: wherefore then are ye the last to bring back the king?
10_19_12.ESV: You are my brothers; you are my bone and my flesh. Why then should you be the last to bring back the king?’
10_19_12.NASB: 'You are my brothers; you are my bone and my flesh. Why then should you be the last to bring back the king?'
10_19_12.NLT: You are my relatives, my own tribe, my own flesh and blood! So why are you the last ones to welcome back the king?”
10_19_12.WEB: You are my brothers. You are my bone and my flesh. Why then are you the last to bring back the king?’
10_19_12.CSB17: You are my brothers, my flesh and blood. So why should you be the last to restore the king?’
10_19_12.AMP: You are my brothers (relatives, relations); you are my bone and my flesh. Why then are you the last to bring back the king?'
10_19_12.MSG: You're my brothers! You're my own flesh and blood! So why are you the last ones to bring the king back home?'
10_19_12.NIV: You are my brothers, my own flesh and blood. So why should you be the last to bring back the king?’
10_19_12.NRSVCE: You are my kin, you are my bone and my flesh; why then should you be the last to bring back the king?’

10_19_13.NKJV: And say to Amasa, ‘Are you not my bone and my flesh? God do so to me, and more also, if you are not commander of the army before me continually in place of Joab.’ ”
10_19_13.KJV: And say ye to Amasa, Art thou not of my bone, and of my flesh? God do so to me, and more also, if thou be not captain of the host before me continually in the room of Joab.
10_19_13.ESV: And say to Amasa, ‘Are you not my bone and my flesh? God do so to me and more also, if you are not commander of my army from now on in place of Joab.’”
10_19_13.NASB: 'Say to Amasa, 'Are you not my bone and my flesh? May God do so to me, and more also, if you will not be commander of the army before me continually in place of Joab.''
10_19_13.NLT: And David told them to tell Amasa, “Since you are my own flesh and blood, like Joab, may God strike me and even kill me if I do not appoint you as commander of my army in his place.”
10_19_13.WEB: Say to Amasa, ‘Aren’t you my bone and my flesh? God do so to me, and more also, if you aren’t captain of the army before me continually instead of Joab.’”
10_19_13.CSB17: And tell Amasa, ‘Aren’t you my flesh and blood? May God punish me and do so severely if you don’t become commander of my army from now on instead of Joab! ’”
10_19_13.AMP: Say to Amasa [the commander of Absalom's troops], 'Are you not my bone and my flesh? May God do so to me, and more also, if you will not be commander of my army from now on in place of Joab.'"
10_19_13.MSG: And tell Amasa, 'You, too, are my flesh and blood. As God is my witness, I'm making you the permanent commander of the army in place of Joab.' "
10_19_13.NIV: And say to Amasa, ‘Are you not my own flesh and blood? May God deal with me, be it ever so severely, if from now on you are not the commander of my army in place of Joab.’”
10_19_13.NRSVCE: And say to Amasa, ‘Are you not my bone and my flesh? So may God do to me, and more, if you are not the commander of my army from now on, in place of Joab.’”

10_19_14.NKJV: So he swayed the hearts of all the men of Judah, just as the heart of one man, so that they sent this word to the king: “Return, you and all your servants!”
10_19_14.KJV: And he bowed the heart of all the men of Judah, even as the heart of one man; so that they sent this word unto the king, Return thou, and all thy servants.
10_19_14.ESV: And he swayed the heart of all the men of Judah as one man, so that they sent word to the king, “Return, both you and all your servants.”
10_19_14.NASB: Thus he turned the hearts of all the men of Judah as one man, so that they sent [word] to the king, [saying], 'Return, you and all your servants.'
10_19_14.NLT: Then Amasa convinced all the men of Judah, and they responded unanimously. They sent word to the king, “Return to us, and bring back all who are with you.”
10_19_14.WEB: He bowed the heart of all the men of Judah, even as one man; so that they sent to the king, saying, “Return, you and all your servants.”
10_19_14.CSB17: So he won over all the men of Judah, and they unanimously sent word to the king: “Come back, you and all your servants.”
10_19_14.AMP: In this way he changed the hearts of all the men of Judah as one man, so they sent word to the king, "Return, you and all your servants."
10_19_14.MSG: He captured the hearts of everyone in Judah. They were unanimous in sending for the king: "Come back, you and all your servants."
10_19_14.NIV: He won over the hearts of all the men of Judah as though they were one man. They sent word to the king, “Return, you and all your men.”
10_19_14.NRSVCE: Amasa swayed the hearts of all the people of Judah as one, and they sent word to the king, “Return, both you and all your servants.”

10_19_15.NKJV: Then the king returned and came to the Jordan. And Judah came to Gilgal, to go to meet the king, to escort the king across the Jordan.
10_19_15.KJV: So the king returned, and came to Jordan. And Judah came to Gilgal, to go to meet the king, to conduct the king over Jordan.
10_19_15.ESV: So the king came back to the Jordan, and Judah came to Gilgal to meet the king and to bring the king over the Jordan.
10_19_15.NASB: The king then returned and came as far as the Jordan. And Judah came to Gilgal in order to go to meet the king, to bring the king across the Jordan.
10_19_15.NLT: So the king started back to Jerusalem. And when he arrived at the Jordan River, the people of Judah came to Gilgal to meet him and escort him across the river.
10_19_15.WEB: So the king returned, and came to the Jordan. Judah came to Gilgal, to go to meet the king, to bring the king over the Jordan.
10_19_15.CSB17: Then the king returned. When he arrived at the Jordan, Judah came to Gilgal to meet the king and escort him across the Jordan.
10_19_15.AMP: So David returned and came to the Jordan. And [supporters from] Judah came to Gilgal to meet the king, to escort him across the Jordan.
10_19_15.MSG: So the king returned. He arrived at the Jordan just as Judah reached Gilgal on their way to welcome the king and escort him across the Jordan.
10_19_15.NIV: Then the king returned and went as far as the Jordan.Now the men of Judah had come to Gilgal to go out and meet the king and bring him across the Jordan.
10_19_15.NRSVCE: So the king came back to the Jordan; and Judah came to Gilgal to meet the king and to bring him over the Jordan.

10_19_16.WEB: Shimei the son of Gera, the Benjamite, who was of Bahurim, hurried and came down with the men of Judah to meet king David.

10_19_17.NRSVCE: with him were a thousand people from Benjamin. And Ziba, the servant of the house of Saul, with his fifteen sons and his twenty servants, rushed down to the Jordan ahead of the king,

10_19_18.NLT: They crossed the shallows of the Jordan to bring the king’s household across the river, helping him in every way they could. David’s Mercy to Shimei As the king was about to cross the river, Shimei fell down before him.
10_19_18.WEB: A ferry boat went to bring over the king’s household, and to do what he thought good. Shimei the son of Gera fell down before the king, when he had come over the Jordan.

10_19_18.AMP: Then they [repeatedly] crossed the ford to bring over the king's household (family), and to do what pleased him. And Shimei the son of Gera fell down before the king as he was about to cross the Jordan,
10_19_18.MSG: and brought his entourage across, doing whatever they could to make the king comfortable.Shimei son of Gera bowed deeply in homage to the king as soon as he was across the Jordan

10_19_19.NKJV: Then he said to the king, “Do not let my lord impute iniquity to me, or remember what wrong your servant did on the day that my lord the king left Jerusalem, that the king should take it to heart.
10_19_19.KJV: And said unto the king, Let not my lord impute iniquity unto me, neither do thou remember that which thy servant did perversely the day that my lord the king went out of Jerusalem, that the king should take it to his heart.
10_19_19.ESV: and said to the king, “Let not my lord hold me guilty or remember how your servant did wrong on the day my lord the king left Jerusalem. Do not let the king take it to heart.
10_19_19.NASB: So he said to the king, 'Let not my lord consider me guilty, nor remember what your servant did wrong on the day when my lord the king came out from Jerusalem, so that the king would take [it] to heart.
10_19_19.NLT: “My lord the king, please forgive me,” he pleaded. “Forget the terrible thing your servant did when you left Jerusalem. May the king put it out of his mind.
10_19_19.WEB: He said to the king, “Don’t let my lord impute iniquity to me, or remember that which your servant did perversely the day that my lord the king went out of Jerusalem, that the king should take it to his heart.
10_19_19.CSB17: and said to him, “My lord, don’t hold me guilty, and don’t remember your servant’s wrongdoing on the day my lord the king left Jerusalem. May the king not take it to heart.
10_19_19.AMP: and said to the king, "Let not my lord consider me guilty, nor remember what your servant did wrong on the day my lord the king left Jerusalem, so that the king would take it to heart.
10_19_19.MSG: and said, "Don't think badly of me, my master! Overlook my irresponsible outburst on the day my master the king left Jerusalem—don't hold it against me!
10_19_19.NIV: and said to him, “May my lord not hold me guilty. Do not remember how your servant did wrong on the day my lord the king left Jerusalem. May the king put it out of his mind.
10_19_19.NRSVCE: and said to the king, “May my lord not hold me guilty or remember how your servant did wrong on the day my lord the king left Jerusalem; may the king not bear it in mind.

10_19_20.NKJV: For I, your servant, know that I have sinned. Therefore here I am, the first to come today of all the house of Joseph to go down to meet my lord the king.”
10_19_20.KJV: For thy servant doth know that I have sinned: therefore, behold, I am come the first this day of all the house of Joseph to go down to meet my lord the king.
10_19_20.ESV: For your servant knows that I have sinned. Therefore, behold, I have come this day, the first of all the house of Joseph to come down to meet my lord the king.”
10_19_20.NASB: 'For your servant knows that I have sinned; therefore behold, I have come today, the first of all the house of Joseph to go down to meet my lord the king.'
10_19_20.NLT: I know how much I sinned. That is why I have come here today, the very first person in all Israel to greet my lord the king.”
10_19_20.WEB: For your servant knows that I have sinned. Therefore behold, I have come today as the first of all the house of Joseph to go down to meet my lord the king.”
10_19_20.CSB17: For your servant knows that I have sinned. But look! Today I am the first one of the entire house of Joseph to come down to meet my lord the king.”
10_19_20.AMP: For your servant knows that I have sinned; therefore, behold, I have come today, the first of all the house of Joseph to come down to meet my lord the king."
10_19_20.MSG: I know I sinned, but look at me now—the first of all the tribe of Joseph to come down and welcome back my master the king!"
10_19_20.NIV: For I your servant know that I have sinned, but today I have come here as the first of the whole house of Joseph to come down and meet my lord the king.”
10_19_20.NRSVCE: For your servant knows that I have sinned; therefore, see, I have come this day, the first of all the house of Joseph to come down to meet my lord the king.”

10_19_21.NKJV: But Abishai the son of Zeruiah answered and said, “Shall not Shimei be put to death for this, because he cursed the LORD’s anointed?”
10_19_21.KJV: But Abishai the son of Zeruiah answered and said, Shall not Shimei be put to death for this, because he cursed the LORD'S anointed?
10_19_21.ESV: Abishai the son of Zeruiah answered, “Shall not Shimei be put to death for this, because he cursed the LORD's anointed?”
10_19_21.NASB: But Abishai the son of Zeruiah said, 'Should not Shimei be put to death for this, because he cursed the LORD'S anointed?'
10_19_21.NLT: Then Abishai son of Zeruiah said, “Shimei should die, for he cursed the LORD’s anointed king!”
10_19_21.WEB: But Abishai the son of Zeruiah answered, “Shouldn’t Shimei be put to death for this, because he cursed Yahweh’s anointed?”
10_19_21.CSB17: Abishai son of Zeruiah asked, “Shouldn’t Shimei be put to death for this, because he cursed the LORD’s anointed?”
10_19_21.AMP: But Abishai the son of Zeruiah said, "Should not Shimei be put to death for this, because he cursed the Lord's anointed?"
10_19_21.MSG: Abishai son of Zeruiah interrupted, "Enough of this! Shouldn't we kill him outright? Why, he cursed God's anointed!"
10_19_21.NIV: Then Abishai son of Zeruiah said, “Shouldn’t Shimei be put to death for this? He cursed the Lord’s anointed.”
10_19_21.NRSVCE: Abishai son of Zeruiah answered, “Shall not Shimei be put to death for this, because he cursed the LORD’s anointed?”

10_19_22.NKJV: And David said, “What have I to do with you, you sons of Zeruiah, that you should be adversaries to me today? Shall any man be put to death today in Israel? For do I not know that today I am king over Israel?”
10_19_22.KJV: And David said, What have I to do with you, ye sons of Zeruiah, that ye should this day be adversaries unto me? shall there any man be put to death this day in Israel? for do not I know that I am this day king over Israel?
10_19_22.ESV: But David said, “What have I to do with you, you sons of Zeruiah, that you should this day be as an adversary to me? Shall anyone be put to death in Israel this day? For do I not know that I am this day king over Israel?”
10_19_22.NASB: David then said, 'What have I to do with you, O sons of Zeruiah, that you should this day be an adversary to me? Should any man be put to death in Israel today? For do I not know that I am king over Israel today?'
10_19_22.NLT: “Who asked your opinion, you sons of Zeruiah!” David exclaimed. “Why have you become my adversary today? This is not a day for execution, for today I am once again the king of Israel!”
10_19_22.WEB: David said, “What have I to do with you, you sons of Zeruiah, that you should be adversaries to me today? Shall any man be put to death today in Israel? For don’t I know that I am king over Israel today?”
10_19_22.CSB17: David answered, “Sons of Zeruiah, do we agree on anything? Have you become my adversary today? Should any man be killed in Israel today? Am I not aware that today I’m king over Israel?”
10_19_22.AMP: David said, "What business is this of yours, you sons of Zeruiah, that you should be an adversary to me today? Should anyone be put to death in Israel today? For do I not know that today I am king over Israel?"
10_19_22.MSG: But David said, "What is it with you sons of Zeruiah? Why do you insist on being so contentious? Nobody is going to be killed today. I am again king over Israel!"
10_19_22.NIV: David replied, “What do you and I have in common, you sons of Zeruiah? This day you have become my adversaries! Should anyone be put to death in Israel today? Do I not know that today I am king over Israel?”
10_19_22.NRSVCE: But David said, “What have I to do with you, you sons of Zeruiah, that you should today become an adversary to me? Shall anyone be put to death in Israel this day? For do I not know that I am this day king over Israel?”

10_19_23.NKJV: Therefore the king said to Shimei, “You shall not die.” And the king swore to him.
10_19_23.KJV: Therefore the king said unto Shimei, Thou shalt not die. And the king sware unto him.
10_19_23.ESV: And the king said to Shimei, “You shall not die.” And the king gave him his oath.
10_19_23.NASB: The king said to Shimei, 'You shall not die.' Thus the king swore to him.
10_19_23.NLT: Then, turning to Shimei, David vowed, “Your life will be spared.”
10_19_23.WEB: The king said to Shimei, “You will not die.” The king swore to him.
10_19_23.CSB17: So the king said to Shimei, “You will not die.” Then the king gave him his oath.
10_19_23.AMP: Therefore the king said to Shimei, "You shall not be put to death." And so the king gave him his promise.
10_19_23.MSG: Then the king turned to Shimei, "You're not going to die." And the king gave him his word.
10_19_23.NIV: So the king said to Shimei, “You shall not die.” And the king promised him on oath.
10_19_23.NRSVCE: The king said to Shimei, “You shall not die.” And the king gave him his oath.

10_19_24.NKJV: Now Mephibosheth the son of Saul came down to meet the king. And he had not cared for his feet, nor trimmed his mustache, nor washed his clothes, from the day the king departed until the day he returned in peace.
10_19_24.KJV: And Mephibosheth the son of Saul came down to meet the king, and had neither dressed his feet, nor trimmed his beard, nor washed his clothes, from the day the king departed until the day he came again in peace.
10_19_24.ESV: And Mephibosheth the son of Saul came down to meet the king. He had neither taken care of his feet nor trimmed his beard nor washed his clothes, from the day the king departed until the day he came back in safety.
10_19_24.NASB: Then Mephibosheth the son of Saul came down to meet the king; and he had neither cared for his feet, nor trimmed his mustache, nor washed his clothes, from the day the king departed until the day he came [home] in peace.
10_19_24.NLT: Now Mephibosheth, Saul’s grandson, came down from Jerusalem to meet the king. He had not cared for his feet, trimmed his beard, or washed his clothes since the day the king left Jerusalem.
10_19_24.WEB: Mephibosheth the son of Saul came down to meet the king; and he had neither groomed his feet, nor trimmed his beard, nor washed his clothes, from the day the king departed until the day he came home in peace.
10_19_24.CSB17: Mephibosheth, Saul’s grandson, also went down to meet the king. He had not taken care of his feet, trimmed his mustache, or washed his clothes from the day the king left until the day he returned safely.
10_19_24.AMP: Then Mephibosheth the [grand]son of Saul came down to meet the king, but he had not cared for his feet, nor trimmed his mustache, nor washed his clothes from the day the king left until the day he returned in peace and safety.
10_19_24.MSG: Next Mephibosheth grandson of Saul arrived from Jerusalem to welcome the king. He hadn't combed his hair or trimmed his beard or washed his clothes from the day the king left until the day he returned safe and sound.
10_19_24.NIV: Mephibosheth, Saul’s grandson, also went down to meet the king. He had not taken care of his feet or trimmed his mustache or washed his clothes from the day the king left until the day he returned safely.
10_19_24.NRSVCE: Mephibosheth grandson of Saul came down to meet the king; he had not taken care of his feet, or trimmed his beard, or washed his clothes, from the day the king left until the day he came back in safety.

10_19_25.NKJV: So it was, when he had come to Jerusalem to meet the king, that the king said to him, “Why did you not go with me, Mephibosheth?”
10_19_25.KJV: And it came to pass, when he was come to Jerusalem to meet the king, that the king said unto him, Wherefore wentest not thou with me, Mephibosheth?
10_19_25.ESV: And when he came to Jerusalem to meet the king, the king said to him, “Why did you not go with me, Mephibosheth?”
10_19_25.NASB: It was when he came from Jerusalem to meet the king, that the king said to him, 'Why did you not go with me, Mephibosheth?'
10_19_25.NLT: “Why didn’t you come with me, Mephibosheth?” the king asked him.
10_19_25.WEB: When he had come to Jerusalem to meet the king, the king said to him, “Why didn’t you go with me, Mephibosheth?”
10_19_25.CSB17: When he came from Jerusalem to meet the king, the king asked him, “Mephibosheth, why didn’t you come with me?”
10_19_25.AMP: And when he came to Jerusalem to meet the king, the king said to him, "Why did you not go with me, Mephibosheth?"
10_19_25.MSG: The king said, "And why didn't you come with me, Mephibosheth?"
10_19_25.NIV: When he came from Jerusalem to meet the king, the king asked him, “Why didn’t you go with me, Mephibosheth?”
10_19_25.NRSVCE: When he came from Jerusalem to meet the king, the king said to him, “Why did you not go with me, Mephibosheth?”

10_19_26.NKJV: And he answered, “My lord, O king, my servant deceived me. For your servant said, ‘I will saddle a donkey for myself, that I may ride on it and go to the king,’ because your servant is lame.
10_19_26.KJV: And he answered, My lord, O king, my servant deceived me: for thy servant said, I will saddle me an ass, that I may ride thereon, and go to the king; because thy servant is lame.
10_19_26.ESV: He answered, “My lord, O king, my servant deceived me, for your servant said to him, ‘I will saddle a donkey for myself, that I may ride on it and go with the king.’ For your servant is lame.
10_19_26.NASB: So he answered, 'O my lord, the king, my servant deceived me; for your servant said, 'I will saddle a donkey for myself that I may ride on it and go with the king,' because your servant is lame.
10_19_26.NLT: Mephibosheth replied, “My lord the king, my servant Ziba deceived me. I told him, ‘Saddle my donkey so I can go with the king.’ For as you know I am crippled.
10_19_26.WEB: He answered, “My lord, O king, my servant deceived me. For your servant said, I will saddle me a donkey, that I may ride on it, and go with the king; because your servant is lame.
10_19_26.CSB17: “My lord the king,” he replied, “my servant Ziba betrayed me. Actually your servant said: ‘I’ll saddle the donkey for myself so that I may ride it and go with the king’ — for your servant is lame.
10_19_26.AMP: He said, "My lord the king, my servant [Ziba] betrayed me; for I said, 'Saddle a donkey for me so that I may ride on it and go with the king,' for your servant is lame [but he took the donkeys and left without me].
10_19_26.MSG: "My master the king," he said, "my servant betrayed me. I told him to saddle my donkey so I could ride it and go with the king, for, as you know, I am lame.
10_19_26.NIV: He said, “My lord the king, since I your servant am lame, I said, ‘I will have my donkey saddled and will ride on it, so I can go with the king.’ But Ziba my servant betrayed me.
10_19_26.NRSVCE: He answered, “My lord, O king, my servant deceived me; for your servant said to him, ‘Saddle a donkey for me, so that I may ride on it and go with the king.’ For your servant is lame.

10_19_27.NKJV: And he has slandered your servant to my lord the king, but my lord the king is like the angel of God. Therefore do what is good in your eyes.
10_19_27.KJV: And he hath slandered thy servant unto my lord the king; but my lord the king is as an angel of God: do therefore what is good in thine eyes.
10_19_27.ESV: He has slandered your servant to my lord the king. But my lord the king is like the angel of God; do therefore what seems good to you.
10_19_27.NASB: 'Moreover, he has slandered your servant to my lord the king; but my lord the king is like the angel of God, therefore do what is good in your sight.
10_19_27.NLT: Ziba has slandered me by saying that I refused to come. But I know that my lord the king is like an angel of God, so do what you think is best.
10_19_27.WEB: He has slandered your servant to my lord the king, but my lord the king is as an angel of God. Do therefore what is good in your eyes.
10_19_27.CSB17: Ziba slandered your servant to my lord the king. But my lord the king is like the angel of God, so do whatever you think best.
10_19_27.AMP: Further, he has slandered your servant to my lord the king; but my lord the king is like the angel of God; so do what is good in your eyes.
10_19_27.MSG: And then he lied to you about me. But my master the king has been like one of God's angels: he knew what was right and did it.
10_19_27.NIV: And he has slandered your servant to my lord the king. My lord the king is like an angel of God; so do whatever pleases you.
10_19_27.NRSVCE: He has slandered your servant to my lord the king. But my lord the king is like the angel of God; do therefore what seems good to you.

10_19_28.NKJV: For all my father’s house were but dead men before my lord the king. Yet you set your servant among those who eat at your own table. Therefore what right have I still to cry out anymore to the king?”
10_19_28.KJV: For all of my father's house were but dead men before my lord the king: yet didst thou set thy servant among them that did eat at thine own table. What right therefore have I yet to cry any more unto the king? dead: Heb. men of death
10_19_28.ESV: For all my father's house were but men doomed to death before my lord the king, but you set your servant among those who eat at your table. What further right have I, then, to cry to the king?”
10_19_28.NASB: 'For all my father's household was nothing but dead men before my lord the king; yet you set your servant among those who ate at your own table. What right do I have yet that I should complain anymore to the king?'
10_19_28.NLT: All my relatives and I could expect only death from you, my lord, but instead you have honored me by allowing me to eat at your own table! What more can I ask?”
10_19_28.WEB: For all my father’s house were but dead men before my lord the king; yet you set your servant among those who ate at your own table. What right therefore have I yet that I should cry any more to the king?”
10_19_28.CSB17: For my grandfather’s entire family deserves death from my lord the king, but you set your servant among those who eat at your table. So what further right do I have to keep on making appeals to the king?”
10_19_28.AMP: For were not all of my father's household (family) nothing but dead men before my lord the king; yet you set your servant among those who ate at your own table. So what right do I still have to cry out anymore to the king [for help]?"
10_19_28.MSG: Wasn't everyone in my father's house doomed? But you took me in and gave me a place at your table. What more could I ever expect or ask?"
10_19_28.NIV: All my grandfather’s descendants deserved nothing but death from my lord the king, but you gave your servant a place among those who eat at your table. So what right do I have to make any more appeals to the king?”
10_19_28.NRSVCE: For all my father’s house were doomed to death before my lord the king; but you set your servant among those who eat at your table. What further right have I, then, to appeal to the king?”

10_19_29.NKJV: So the king said to him, “Why do you speak anymore of your matters? I have said, ‘You and Ziba divide the land.’ ”
10_19_29.KJV: And the king said unto him, Why speakest thou any more of thy matters? I have said, Thou and Ziba divide the land.
10_19_29.ESV: And the king said to him, “Why speak any more of your affairs? I have decided: you and Ziba shall divide the land.”
10_19_29.NASB: So the king said to him, 'Why do you still speak of your affairs? I have decided, 'You and Ziba shall divide the land.''
10_19_29.NLT: “You’ve said enough,” David replied. “I’ve decided that you and Ziba will divide your land equally between you.”
10_19_29.WEB: The king said to him, “Why do you speak any more of your matters? I say, you and Ziba divide the land.”
10_19_29.CSB17: The king said to him, “Why keep on speaking about these matters of yours? I hereby declare: you and Ziba are to divide the land.”
10_19_29.AMP: The king said to him, "Why speak anymore of your affairs? I have said, 'You and Ziba shall divide the land.'"
10_19_29.MSG: "That's enough," said the king. "Say no more. Here's my decision: You and Ziba divide the property between you."
10_19_29.NIV: The king said to him, “Why say more? I order you and Ziba to divide the fields.”
10_19_29.NRSVCE: The king said to him, “Why speak any more of your affairs? I have decided: you and Ziba shall divide the land.”

10_19_30.NKJV: Then Mephibosheth said to the king, “Rather, let him take it all, inasmuch as my lord the king has come back in peace to his own house.”
10_19_30.KJV: And Mephibosheth said unto the king, Yea, let him take all, forasmuch as my lord the king is come again in peace unto his own house.
10_19_30.ESV: And Mephibosheth said to the king, “Oh, let him take it all, since my lord the king has come safely home.”
10_19_30.NASB: Mephibosheth said to the king, 'Let him even take it all, since my lord the king has come safely to his own house.'
10_19_30.NLT: “Give him all of it,” Mephibosheth said. “I am content just to have you safely back again, my lord the king!”
10_19_30.WEB: Mephibosheth said to the king, “Yes, let him take all, because my lord the king has come in peace to his own house.”
10_19_30.CSB17: Mephibosheth said to the king, “Instead, since my lord the king has come to his palace safely, let Ziba take it all!”
10_19_30.AMP: Mephibosheth said to the king, "Let him even take it all, since my lord the king has returned to his own house in safety and peace."
10_19_30.MSG: Mephibosheth said, "Oh, let him have it all! All I care about is that my master the king is home safe and sound!"
10_19_30.NIV: Mephibosheth said to the king, “Let him take everything, now that my lord the king has arrived home safely.”
10_19_30.NRSVCE: Mephibosheth said to the king, “Let him take it all, since my lord the king has arrived home safely.”

10_19_31.NKJV: And Barzillai the Gileadite came down from Rogelim and went across the Jordan with the king, to escort him across the Jordan.
10_19_31.KJV: And Barzillai the Gileadite came down from Rogelim, and went over Jordan with the king, to conduct him over Jordan.
10_19_31.ESV: Now Barzillai the Gileadite had come down from Rogelim, and he went on with the king to the Jordan, to escort him over the Jordan.
10_19_31.NASB: Now Barzillai the Gileadite had come down from Rogelim; and he went on to the Jordan with the king to escort him over the Jordan.
10_19_31.NLT: Barzillai of Gilead had come down from Rogelim to escort the king across the Jordan.
10_19_31.WEB: Barzillai the Gileadite came down from Rogelim; and he went over the Jordan with the king, to conduct him over the Jordan.
10_19_31.CSB17: Barzillai the Gileadite had come down from Rogelim and accompanied the king to the Jordan River to see him off at the Jordan.
10_19_31.AMP: Now Barzillai the Gileadite came down from Rogelim and went on to the Jordan with the king to escort him over the Jordan.
10_19_31.MSG: Barzillai the Gileadite had come down from Rogelim. He crossed the Jordan with the king to give him a good send-off.
10_19_31.NIV: Barzillai the Gileadite also came down from Rogelim to cross the Jordan with the king and to send him on his way from there.
10_19_31.NRSVCE: Now Barzillai the Gileadite had come down from Rogelim; he went on with the king to the Jordan, to escort him over the Jordan.

10_19_32.NKJV: Now Barzillai was a very aged man, eighty years old. And he had provided the king with supplies while he stayed at Mahanaim, for he was a very rich man.
10_19_32.KJV: Now Barzillai was a very aged man, even fourscore years old: and he had provided the king of sustenance while he lay at Mahanaim; for he was a very great man.
10_19_32.ESV: Barzillai was a very aged man, eighty years old. He had provided the king with food while he stayed at Mahanaim, for he was a very wealthy man.
10_19_32.NASB: Now Barzillai was very old, being eighty years old; and he had sustained the king while he stayed at Mahanaim, for he was a very great man.
10_19_32.NLT: He was very old — eighty years of age — and very wealthy. He was the one who had provided food for the king during his stay in Mahanaim.
10_19_32.WEB: Now Barzillai was a very aged man, even eighty years old. He had provided the king with sustenance while he stayed at Mahanaim; for he was a very great man.
10_19_32.CSB17: Barzillai was a very old man — eighty years old — and since he was a very wealthy man, he had provided for the needs of the king while he stayed in Mahanaim.
10_19_32.AMP: Barzillai was a very old man, eighty years old; and he had provided the king with food while he stayed at Mahanaim, for he was a very great and wealthy man.
10_19_32.MSG: Barzillai was a very old man—eighty years old! He had supplied the king's needs all the while he was in Mahanaim since he was very wealthy.
10_19_32.NIV: Now Barzillai was a very old man, eighty years of age. He had provided for the king during his stay in Mahanaim, for he was a very wealthy man.
10_19_32.NRSVCE: Barzillai was a very aged man, eighty years old. He had provided the king with food while he stayed at Mahanaim, for he was a very wealthy man.

10_19_33.NKJV: And the king said to Barzillai, “Come across with me, and I will provide for you while you are with me in Jerusalem.”
10_19_33.KJV: And the king said unto Barzillai, Come thou over with me, and I will feed thee with me in Jerusalem.
10_19_33.ESV: And the king said to Barzillai, “Come over with me, and I will provide for you with me in Jerusalem.”
10_19_33.NASB: The king said to Barzillai, 'You cross over with me and I will sustain you in Jerusalem with me.'
10_19_33.NLT: “Come across with me and live in Jerusalem,” the king said to Barzillai. “I will take care of you there.”
10_19_33.WEB: The king said to Barzillai, “Come over with me, and I will sustain you with me in Jerusalem.”
10_19_33.CSB17: The king said to Barzillai, “Cross over with me, and I’ll provide for you at my side in Jerusalem.”
10_19_33.AMP: The king said to Barzillai, "Cross over with me and I will provide for you in Jerusalem with me."
10_19_33.MSG: "Join me in Jerusalem," the king said to Barzillai. "Let me take care of you."
10_19_33.NIV: The king said to Barzillai, “Cross over with me and stay with me in Jerusalem, and I will provide for you.”
10_19_33.NRSVCE: The king said to Barzillai, “Come over with me, and I will provide for you in Jerusalem at my side.”

10_19_34.NKJV: But Barzillai said to the king, “How long have I to live, that I should go up with the king to Jerusalem?
10_19_34.KJV: And Barzillai said unto the king, How long have I to live , that I should go up with the king unto Jerusalem? How: Heb. How many days are the years of my life
10_19_34.ESV: But Barzillai said to the king, “How many years have I still to live, that I should go up with the king to Jerusalem?
10_19_34.NASB: But Barzillai said to the king, 'How long have I yet to live, that I should go up with the king to Jerusalem?
10_19_34.NLT: “No,” he replied, “I am far too old to go with the king to Jerusalem.
10_19_34.WEB: Barzillai said to the king, “How many are the days of the years of my life, that I should go up with the king to Jerusalem?
10_19_34.CSB17: Barzillai replied to the king, “How many years of my life are left that I should go up to Jerusalem with the king?
10_19_34.AMP: But Barzillai said to the king, "How much longer have I to live, that I should go up with the king to Jerusalem?
10_19_34.MSG: But Barzillai declined the offer, "How long do you think I'd live if I went with the king to Jerusalem?
10_19_34.NIV: But Barzillai answered the king, “How many more years will I live, that I should go up to Jerusalem with the king?
