10_19_34.NRSVCE: But Barzillai said to the king, “How many years have I still to live, that I should go up with the king to Jerusalem?

10_19_35.NKJV: I am today eighty years old. Can I discern between the good and bad? Can your servant taste what I eat or what I drink? Can I hear any longer the voice of singing men and singing women? Why then should your servant be a further burden to my lord the king?
10_19_35.KJV: I am this day fourscore years old: and can I discern between good and evil? can thy servant taste what I eat or what I drink? can I hear any more the voice of singing men and singing women? wherefore then should thy servant be yet a burden unto my lord the king?
10_19_35.ESV: I am this day eighty years old. Can I discern what is pleasant and what is not? Can your servant taste what he eats or what he drinks? Can I still listen to the voice of singing men and singing women? Why then should your servant be an added burden to my lord the king?
10_19_35.NASB: 'I am now eighty years old. Can I distinguish between good and bad? Or can your servant taste what I eat or what I drink? Or can I hear anymore the voice of singing men and women? Why then should your servant be an added burden to my lord the king?
10_19_35.NLT: I am eighty years old today, and I can no longer enjoy anything. Food and wine are no longer tasty, and I cannot hear the singers as they sing. I would only be a burden to my lord the king.
10_19_35.WEB: I am eighty years old, today. Can I discern between good and bad? Can your servant taste what I eat or what I drink? Can I hear the voice of singing men and singing women any more? Why then should your servant be a burden to my lord the king?
10_19_35.CSB17: I’m now eighty years old. Can I discern what is pleasant and what is not? Can your servant taste what he eats or drinks? Can I still hear the voice of male and female singers? Why should your servant be an added burden to my lord the king?
10_19_35.AMP: I am this day eighty years old. Can I [be useful to advise you to] discern between good and bad? Can your servant taste what I eat or drink? Can I still hear the voices of singing men and women? Why then should your servant be an added burden to my lord the king?
10_19_35.MSG: I'm eighty years old and not much good anymore to anyone. Can't taste food; can't hear music. So why add to the burdens of my master the king?
10_19_35.NIV: I am now eighty years old. Can I tell the difference between what is good and what is not? Can your servant taste what he eats and drinks? Can I still hear the voices of men and women singers? Why should your servant be an added burden to my lord the king?
10_19_35.NRSVCE: Today I am eighty years old; can I discern what is pleasant and what is not? Can your servant taste what he eats or what he drinks? Can I still listen to the voice of singing men and singing women? Why then should your servant be an added burden to my lord the king?

10_19_36.NKJV: Your servant will go a little way across the Jordan with the king. And why should the king repay me with such a reward?
10_19_36.KJV: Thy servant will go a little way over Jordan with the king: and why should the king recompense it me with such a reward?
10_19_36.ESV: Your servant will go a little way over the Jordan with the king. Why should the king repay me with such a reward?
10_19_36.NASB: 'Your servant would merely cross over the Jordan with the king. Why should the king compensate me [with] this reward?
10_19_36.NLT: Just to go across the Jordan River with the king is all the honor I need!
10_19_36.WEB: Your servant would but just go over the Jordan with the king. Why should the king repay me with such a reward?
10_19_36.CSB17: Since your servant is only going with the king a little way across the Jordan, why should the king repay me with such a reward?
10_19_36.AMP: Your servant would merely cross over the Jordan with the king. Why should the king compensate me with this reward?
10_19_36.MSG: I'll just go a little way across the Jordan with the king. But why would the king need to make a great thing of that?
10_19_36.NIV: Your servant will cross over the Jordan with the king for a short distance, but why should the king reward me in this way?
10_19_36.NRSVCE: Your servant will go a little way over the Jordan with the king. Why should the king recompense me with such a reward?

10_19_37.NKJV: Please let your servant turn back again, that I may die in my own city, near the grave of my father and mother. But here is your servant Chimham; let him cross over with my lord the king, and do for him what seems good to you.”
10_19_37.KJV: Let thy servant, I pray thee, turn back again, that I may die in mine own city, and be buried by the grave of my father and of my mother. But behold thy servant Chimham; let him go over with my lord the king; and do to him what shall seem good unto thee.
10_19_37.ESV: Please let your servant return, that I may die in my own city near the grave of my father and my mother. But here is your servant Chimham. Let him go over with my lord the king, and do for him whatever seems good to you.”
10_19_37.NASB: 'Please let your servant return, that I may die in my own city near the grave of my father and my mother. However, here is your servant Chimham, let him cross over with my lord the king, and do for him what is good in your sight.'
10_19_37.NLT: Then let me return again to die in my own town, where my father and mother are buried. But here is your servant, my son Kimham. Let him go with my lord the king and receive whatever you want to give him.”
10_19_37.WEB: Please let your servant turn back again, that I may die in my own city, by the grave of my father and my mother. But behold, your servant Chimham; let him go over with my lord the king; and do to him what shall seem good to you.”
10_19_37.CSB17: Please let your servant return so that I may die in my own city near the tomb of my father and mother. But here is your servant Chimham: let him cross over with my lord the king. Do for him what seems good to you.”
10_19_37.AMP: Please let your servant return, so that I may die in my own city [and be buried] by the grave of my father and mother. But here is your servant Chimham [my son]; let him cross over with my lord the king, and do for him what seems good to you."
10_19_37.MSG: Let me go back and die in my hometown and be buried with my father and mother. But my servant Kimham here; let him go with you in my place. But treat him well!"
10_19_37.NIV: Let your servant return, that I may die in my own town near the tomb of my father and mother. But here is your servant Kimham. Let him cross over with my lord the king. Do for him whatever pleases you.”
10_19_37.NRSVCE: Please let your servant return, so that I may die in my own town, near the graves of my father and my mother. But here is your servant Chimham; let him go over with my lord the king; and do for him whatever seems good to you.”

10_19_38.NKJV: And the king answered, “Chimham shall cross over with me, and I will do for him what seems good to you. Now whatever you request of me, I will do for you.”
10_19_38.KJV: And the king answered, Chimham shall go over with me, and I will do to him that which shall seem good unto thee: and whatsoever thou shalt require of me, that will I do for thee. require: Heb. choose
10_19_38.ESV: And the king answered, “Chimham shall go over with me, and I will do for him whatever seems good to you, and all that you desire of me I will do for you.”
10_19_38.NASB: The king answered, 'Chimham shall cross over with me, and I will do for him what is good in your sight; and whatever you require of me, I will do for you.'
10_19_38.NLT: “Good,” the king agreed. “Kimham will go with me, and I will help him in any way you would like. And I will do for you anything you want.”
10_19_38.WEB: The king answered, “Chimham shall go over with me, and I will do to him that which shall seem good to you. Whatever you request of me, that I will do for you.”
10_19_38.CSB17: The king replied, “Chimham will cross over with me, and I will do for him what seems good to you, and whatever you desire from me I will do for you.”
10_19_38.AMP: The king answered, "Chimham shall cross over with me, and I will do for him what seems good to you; and whatever you ask of me, I will do for you."
10_19_38.MSG: The king said, "That's settled; Kimham goes with me. And I will treat him well! If you think of anything else, I'll do that for you, too."
10_19_38.NIV: The king said, “Kimham shall cross over with me, and I will do for him whatever pleases you. And anything you desire from me I will do for you.”
10_19_38.NRSVCE: The king answered, “Chimham shall go over with me, and I will do for him whatever seems good to you; and all that you desire of me I will do for you.”

10_19_39.NKJV: Then all the people went over the Jordan. And when the king had crossed over, the king kissed Barzillai and blessed him, and he returned to his own place.
10_19_39.KJV: And all the people went over Jordan. And when the king was come over, the king kissed Barzillai, and blessed him; and he returned unto his own place.
10_19_39.ESV: Then all the people went over the Jordan, and the king went over. And the king kissed Barzillai and blessed him, and he returned to his own home.
10_19_39.NASB: All the people crossed over the Jordan and the king crossed too. The king then kissed Barzillai and blessed him, and he returned to his place.
10_19_39.NLT: So all the people crossed the Jordan with the king. After David had blessed Barzillai and kissed him, Barzillai returned to his own home.
10_19_39.WEB: All the people went over the Jordan, and the king went over. Then the king kissed Barzillai, and blessed him; and he returned to his own place.
10_19_39.CSB17: So all the people crossed the Jordan, and then the king crossed. The king kissed Barzillai and blessed him, and Barzillai returned to his home.
10_19_39.AMP: So all the people crossed over the Jordan. When the king had crossed over, he kissed Barzillai and blessed him, and he returned to his place.
10_19_39.MSG: The army crossed the Jordan but the king stayed. The king kissed and blessed Barzillai, who then returned home.
10_19_39.NIV: So all the people crossed the Jordan, and then the king crossed over. The king kissed Barzillai and gave him his blessing, and Barzillai returned to his home.
10_19_39.NRSVCE: Then all the people crossed over the Jordan, and the king crossed over; the king kissed Barzillai and blessed him, and he returned to his own home.

10_19_40.NKJV: Now the king went on to Gilgal, and Chimham went on with him. And all the people of Judah escorted the king, and also half the people of Israel.
10_19_40.KJV: Then the king went on to Gilgal, and Chimham went on with him: and all the people of Judah conducted the king, and also half the people of Israel. Chimham: Heb. Chimhan
10_19_40.ESV: The king went on to Gilgal, and Chimham went on with him. All the people of Judah, and also half the people of Israel, brought the king on his way.
10_19_40.NASB: Now the king went on to Gilgal, and Chimham went on with him; and all the people of Judah and also half the people of Israel accompanied the king.
10_19_40.NLT: The king then crossed over to Gilgal, taking Kimham with him. All the troops of Judah and half the troops of Israel escorted the king on his way.
10_19_40.WEB: So the king went over to Gilgal, and Chimham went over with him. All the people of Judah brought the king over, and also half the people of Israel.
10_19_40.CSB17: The king went on to Gilgal, and Chimham went with him. All the troops of Judah and half of Israel’s escorted the king.
10_19_40.AMP: Then the king went on to Gilgal, and Chimham went on with him; and all the people of Judah and also half the people of Israel accompanied the king.
10_19_40.MSG: Then the king, Kimham with him, crossed over at Gilgal.The whole army of Judah and half the army of Israel processed with the king.
10_19_40.NIV: When the king crossed over to Gilgal, Kimham crossed with him. All the troops of Judah and half the troops of Israel had taken the king over.
10_19_40.NRSVCE: The king went on to Gilgal, and Chimham went on with him; all the people of Judah, and also half the people of Israel, brought the king on his way.

10_19_41.NKJV: Just then all the men of Israel came to the king, and said to the king, “Why have our brethren, the men of Judah, stolen you away and brought the king, his household, and all David’s men with him across the Jordan?”
10_19_41.KJV: And, behold, all the men of Israel came to the king, and said unto the king, Why have our brethren the men of Judah stolen thee away, and have brought the king, and his household, and all David's men with him, over Jordan?
10_19_41.ESV: Then all the men of Israel came to the king and said to the king, “Why have our brothers the men of Judah stolen you away and brought the king and his household over the Jordan, and all David's men with him?”
10_19_41.NASB: And behold, all the men of Israel came to the king and said to the king, 'Why had our brothers the men of Judah stolen you away, and brought the king and his household and all David's men with him over the Jordan?'
10_19_41.NLT: But all the men of Israel complained to the king, “The men of Judah stole the king and didn’t give us the honor of helping take you, your household, and all your men across the Jordan.”
10_19_41.WEB: Behold, all the men of Israel came to the king, and said to the king, “Why have our brothers the men of Judah stolen you away, and brought the king, and his household, over the Jordan, and all David’s men with him?”
10_19_41.CSB17: Suddenly, all the men of Israel came to the king. They asked him, “Why did our brothers, the men of Judah, take you away secretly and transport the king and his household across the Jordan, along with all of David’s men?”
10_19_41.AMP: And all the men of Israel came to the king and said to him, "Why have our brothers (relatives), the men [from the tribe] of Judah, stolen you away and brought the king and his household and all David's men with him over the Jordan [instead of waiting for us to arrive]?"
10_19_41.MSG: The men of Israel came to the king and said, "Why have our brothers, the men of Judah, taken over as if they owned the king, escorting the king and his family and close associates across the Jordan?"
10_19_41.NIV: Soon all the men of Israel were coming to the king and saying to him, “Why did our brothers, the men of Judah, steal the king away and bring him and his household across the Jordan, together with all his men?”
10_19_41.NRSVCE: Then all the people of Israel came to the king, and said to him, “Why have our kindred the people of Judah stolen you away, and brought the king and his household over the Jordan, and all David’s men with him?”

10_19_42.NKJV: So all the men of Judah answered the men of Israel, “Because the king is a close relative of ours. Why then are you angry over this matter? Have we ever eaten at the king’s expense? Or has he given us any gift?”
10_19_42.KJV: And all the men of Judah answered the men of Israel, Because the king is near of kin to us: wherefore then be ye angry for this matter? have we eaten at all of the king's cost? or hath he given us any gift?
10_19_42.ESV: All the men of Judah answered the men of Israel, “Because the king is our close relative. Why then are you angry over this matter? Have we eaten at all at the king's expense? Or has he given us any gift?”
10_19_42.NASB: Then all the men of Judah answered the men of Israel, 'Because the king is a close relative to us. Why then are you angry about this matter? Have we eaten at all at the king's [expense], or has anything been taken for us?'
10_19_42.NLT: The men of Judah replied, “The king is one of our own kinsmen. Why should this make you angry? We haven’t eaten any of the king’s food or received any special favors!”
10_19_42.WEB: All the men of Judah answered the men of Israel, “Because the king is a close relative to us. Why then are you angry about this matter? Have we eaten at all at the king’s cost? Or has he given us any gift?”
10_19_42.CSB17: All the men of Judah responded to the men of Israel, “Because the king is our relative. Why does this make you angry? Have we ever eaten anything of the king’s or been honored at all?”
10_19_42.AMP: Then all the men of Judah answered the men of Israel, "Because the king is a close relative to us. So why then are you angry about this matter? Have we eaten at all at the king's expense? Or has anything been taken for us?"
10_19_42.MSG: The men of Judah retorted, "Because the king is related to us, that's why! But why make a scene? You don't see us getting treated special because of it, do you?"
10_19_42.NIV: All the men of Judah answered the men of Israel, “We did this because the king is closely related to us. Why are you angry about it? Have we eaten any of the king’s provisions? Have we taken anything for ourselves?”
10_19_42.NRSVCE: All the people of Judah answered the people of Israel, “Because the king is near of kin to us. Why then are you angry over this matter? Have we eaten at all at the king’s expense? Or has he given us any gift?”

10_19_43.NKJV: And the men of Israel answered the men of Judah, and said, “We have ten shares in the king; therefore we also have more right to David than you. Why then do you despise us—were we not the first to advise bringing back our king?” Yet the words of the men of Judah were fiercer than the words of the men of Israel.
10_19_43.KJV: And the men of Israel answered the men of Judah, and said, We have ten parts in the king, and we have also more right in David than ye: why then did ye despise us, that our advice should not be first had in bringing back our king? And the words of the men of Judah were fiercer than the words of the men of Israel. despise: Heb. set us at light
10_19_43.ESV: And the men of Israel answered the men of Judah, “We have ten shares in the king, and in David also we have more than you. Why then did you despise us? Were we not the first to speak of bringing back our king?” But the words of the men of Judah were fiercer than the words of the men of Israel.
10_19_43.NASB: But the men of Israel answered the men of Judah and said, 'We have ten parts in the king, therefore we also have more [claim] on David than you. Why then did you treat us with contempt? Was it not our advice first to bring back our king?' Yet the words of the men of Judah were harsher than the words of the men of Israel.
10_19_43.NLT: “But there are ten tribes in Israel,” the others replied. “So we have ten times as much right to the king as you do. What right do you have to treat us with such contempt? Weren’t we the first to speak of bringing him back to be our king again?” The argument continued back and forth, and the men of Judah spoke even more harshly than the men of Israel.
10_19_43.WEB: The men of Israel answered the men of Judah, and said, “We have ten parts in the king, and we have also more claim to David than you. Why then did you despise us, that our advice should not be first had in bringing back our king?” The words of the men of Judah were fiercer than the words of the men of Israel.
10_19_43.CSB17: The men of Israel answered the men of Judah: “We have ten shares in the king, so we have a greater claim to David than you. Why then do you despise us? Weren’t we the first to speak of restoring our king?” But the words of the men of Judah were harsher than those of the men of Israel.
10_19_43.AMP: Then the men of Israel answered the men of Judah, "We have ten [tribes'] shares in the king, and we have more claim on David than you. Why then did you treat us with contempt and ignore us [by rushing ahead]? Were we not the first to speak of bringing back our king?" But the words of the men of Judah were harsher than those of the men of Israel.
10_19_43.MSG: The men of Israel shot back, "We have ten shares in the king to your one. Besides we're the firstborn—so why are we having to play second fiddle? It was our idea to bring him back."But the men of Judah took a harder line than the men of Israel.
10_19_43.NIV: Then the men of Israel answered the men of Judah, “We have ten shares in the king; and besides, we have a greater claim on David than you have. So why do you treat us with contempt? Were we not the first to speak of bringing back our king?”But the men of Judah responded even more harshly than the men of Israel.
10_19_43.NRSVCE: But the people of Israel answered the people of Judah, “We have ten shares in the king, and in David also we have more than you. Why then did you despise us? Were we not the first to speak of bringing back our king?” But the words of the people of Judah were fiercer than the words of the people of Israel.

10_20_1.NKJV: And there happened to be there a rebel, whose name was Sheba the son of Bichri, a Benjamite. And he blew a trumpet, and said: “We have no share in David, Nor do we have inheritance in the son of Jesse; Every man to his tents, O Israel!”
10_20_1.KJV: And there happened to be there a man of Belial, whose name was Sheba, the son of Bichri, a Benjamite: and he blew a trumpet, and said, We have no part in David, neither have we inheritance in the son of Jesse: every man to his tents, O Israel.
10_20_1.ESV: Now there happened to be there a worthless man, whose name was Sheba, the son of Bichri, a Benjaminite. And he blew the trumpet and said, “We have no portion in David, and we have no inheritance in the son of Jesse; every man to his tents, O Israel!”
10_20_1.NASB: Now a worthless fellow happened to be there whose name was Sheba, the son of Bichri, a Benjamite; and he blew the trumpet and said, 'We have no portion in David, Nor do we have inheritance in the son of Jesse; Every man to his tents, O Israel!'
10_20_1.NLT: There happened to be a troublemaker there named Sheba son of Bicri, a man from the tribe of Benjamin. Sheba blew a ram’s horn and began to chant: “Down with the dynasty of David!We have no interest in the son of Jesse.Come on, you men of Israel,back to your homes!”
10_20_1.WEB: There happened to be there a base fellow, whose name was Sheba, the son of Bichri, a Benjamite; and he blew the trumpet, and said, “We have no portion in David, neither have we inheritance in the son of Jesse. Every man to his tents, Israel!”
10_20_1.CSB17: Now a wicked man, a Benjaminite named Sheba son of Bichri, happened to be there. He blew the ram’s horn and shouted: We have no portion in David, no inheritance in Jesse’s son. Each man to his tent, Israel!
10_20_1.AMP: There happened to be there a worthless and wicked man named Sheba the son of Bichri, a Benjamite. He blew a trumpet [to call Israel to revolt] and said, "We have no portion in David And no inheritance in the son of Jesse, Every man to his tents, O Israel!"
10_20_1.MSG: Just then a good-for-nothing named Sheba son of Bicri the Benjaminite blew a blast on the ram's horn trumpet, calling out,We've got nothing to do with David,there's no future for us with the son of Jesse!Let's get out of here, Israel—head for your tents!
10_20_1.NIV: Sheba Rebels Against DavidNow a troublemaker named Sheba son of Bicri, a Benjamite, happened to be there. He sounded the trumpet and shouted,“We have no share in David,no part in Jesse’s son!Every man to his tent, O Israel!”
10_20_1.NRSVCE: Now a scoundrel named Sheba son of Bichri, a Benjaminite, happened to be there. He sounded the trumpet and cried out, “We have no portion in David, no share in the son of Jesse! Everyone to your tents, O Israel!”

10_20_2.NKJV: So every man of Israel deserted David, and followed Sheba the son of Bichri. But the men of Judah, from the Jordan as far as Jerusalem, remained loyal to their king.
10_20_2.KJV: So every man of Israel went up from after David, and followed Sheba the son of Bichri: but the men of Judah clave unto their king, from Jordan even to Jerusalem.
10_20_2.ESV: So all the men of Israel withdrew from David and followed Sheba the son of Bichri. But the men of Judah followed their king steadfastly from the Jordan to Jerusalem.
10_20_2.NASB: So all the men of Israel withdrew from following David [and] followed Sheba the son of Bichri; but the men of Judah remained steadfast to their king, from the Jordan even to Jerusalem.
10_20_2.NLT: So all the men of Israel deserted David and followed Sheba son of Bicri. But the men of Judah stayed with their king and escorted him from the Jordan River to Jerusalem.
10_20_2.WEB: So all the men of Israel went up from following David, and followed Sheba the son of Bichri; but the men of Judah joined with their king, from the Jordan even to Jerusalem.
10_20_2.CSB17: So all the men of Israel deserted David and followed Sheba son of Bichri, but the men of Judah from the Jordan all the way to Jerusalem remained loyal to their king.
10_20_2.AMP: So all the men of Israel deserted David and followed Sheba the son of Bichri; but the men of Judah stayed faithfully with their king, from the Jordan to Jerusalem.
10_20_2.MSG: So all the men of Israel deserted David and followed Sheba son of Bicri. But the men of Judah stayed committed, sticking with their king all the way from the Jordan to Jerusalem.
10_20_2.NIV: So all the men of Israel deserted David to follow Sheba son of Bicri. But the men of Judah stayed by their king all the way from the Jordan to Jerusalem.
10_20_2.NRSVCE: So all the people of Israel withdrew from David and followed Sheba son of Bichri; but the people of Judah followed their king steadfastly from the Jordan to Jerusalem.

10_20_3.NKJV: Now David came to his house at Jerusalem. And the king took the ten women, his concubines whom he had left to keep the house, and put them in seclusion and supported them, but did not go in to them. So they were shut up to the day of their death, living in widowhood.
10_20_3.KJV: And David came to his house at Jerusalem; and the king took the ten women his concubines, whom he had left to keep the house, and put them in ward, and fed them, but went not in unto them. So they were shut up unto the day of their death, living in widowhood. ward: Heb. an house of ward shut: Heb. bound living: Heb. in widowhood of life
10_20_3.ESV: And David came to his house at Jerusalem. And the king took the ten concubines whom he had left to care for the house and put them in a house under guard and provided for them, but did not go in to them. So they were shut up until the day of their death, living as if in widowhood.
10_20_3.NASB: Then David came to his house at Jerusalem, and the king took the ten women, the concubines whom he had left to keep the house, and placed them under guard and provided them with sustenance, but did not go in to them. So they were shut up until the day of their death, living as widows.
10_20_3.NLT: When David came to his palace in Jerusalem, he took the ten concubines he had left to look after the palace and placed them in seclusion. Their needs were provided for, but he no longer slept with them. So each of them lived like a widow until she died.
10_20_3.WEB: David came to his house at Jerusalem; and the king took the ten women his concubines, whom he had left to keep the house, and put them in custody, and provided them with sustenance, but didn’t go in to them. So they were shut up to the day of their death, living in widowhood.
10_20_3.CSB17: When David came to his palace in Jerusalem, he took the ten concubines he had left to take care of the palace and placed them under guard. He provided for them, but he was not intimate with them. They were confined until the day of their death, living as widows.
10_20_3.AMP: Then David came to his house (palace) at Jerusalem, and the king took the ten women, his concubines whom he had left to take care of the house, and placed them under guard and provided for them, but did not go in to them. So they were confined, and lived as widows until the day of their death.
10_20_3.MSG: When David arrived home in Jerusalem, the king took the ten concubines he had left to watch the palace and placed them in seclusion, under guard. He provided for their needs but didn't visit them. They were virtual prisoners until they died, widows as long as they lived.
10_20_3.NIV: When David returned to his palace in Jerusalem, he took the ten concubines he had left to take care of the palace and put them in a house under guard. He provided for them, but did not lie with them. They were kept in confinement till the day of their death, living as widows.
10_20_3.NRSVCE: David came to his house at Jerusalem; and the king took the ten concubines whom he had left to look after the house, and put them in a house under guard, and provided for them, but did not go in to them. So they were shut up until the day of their death, living as if in widowhood.

10_20_4.NKJV: And the king said to Amasa, “Assemble the men of Judah for me within three days, and be present here yourself.”
10_20_4.KJV: Then said the king to Amasa, Assemble me the men of Judah within three days, and be thou here present. Assemble: Heb. Call
10_20_4.ESV: Then the king said to Amasa, “Call the men of Judah together to me within three days, and be here yourself.”
10_20_4.NASB: Then the king said to Amasa, 'Call out the men of Judah for me within three days, and be present here yourself.'
10_20_4.NLT: Then the king told Amasa, “Mobilize the army of Judah within three days, and report back at that time.”
10_20_4.WEB: Then the king said to Amasa, “Call me the men of Judah together within three days, and be here present.”
10_20_4.CSB17: The king said to Amasa, “Summon the men of Judah to me within three days and be here yourself.”
10_20_4.AMP: Now the king said to Amasa [the commander of his army], "Summon the [fighting] men of Judah to me within three days, and be present here yourself."
10_20_4.MSG: The king ordered Amasa, "Muster the men of Judah for me in three days; then report in."
10_20_4.NIV: Then the king said to Amasa, “Summon the men of Judah to come to me within three days, and be here yourself.”
10_20_4.NRSVCE: Then the king said to Amasa, “Call the men of Judah together to me within three days, and be here yourself.”

10_20_5.NKJV: So Amasa went to assemble the men of Judah. But he delayed longer than the set time which David had appointed him.
10_20_5.KJV: So Amasa went to assemble the men of Judah: but he tarried longer than the set time which he had appointed him. Assemble: Heb. Call
10_20_5.ESV: So Amasa went to summon Judah, but he delayed beyond the set time that had been appointed him.
10_20_5.NASB: So Amasa went to call out [the men of] Judah, but he delayed longer than the set time which he had appointed him.
10_20_5.NLT: So Amasa went out to notify Judah, but it took him longer than the time he had been given.
10_20_5.WEB: So Amasa went to call the men of Judah together; but he stayed longer than the set time which he had appointed him.
10_20_5.CSB17: Amasa went to summon Judah, but he took longer than the time allotted him.
10_20_5.AMP: So Amasa went to summon [the fighting men of] Judah, but he delayed longer than the time which David had set for him.
10_20_5.MSG: Amasa went to carry out his orders, but he was late reporting back.
10_20_5.NIV: But when Amasa went to summon Judah, he took longer than the time the king had set for him.
10_20_5.NRSVCE: So Amasa went to summon Judah; but he delayed beyond the set time that had been appointed him.

10_20_6.NKJV: And David said to Abishai, “Now Sheba the son of Bichri will do us more harm than Absalom. Take your lord’s servants and pursue him, lest he find for himself fortified cities, and escape us.”
10_20_6.KJV: And David said to Abishai, Now shall Sheba the son of Bichri do us more harm than did Absalom: take thou thy lord's servants, and pursue after him, lest he get him fenced cities, and escape us. escape: Heb. deliver himself from our eyes
10_20_6.ESV: And David said to Abishai, “Now Sheba the son of Bichri will do us more harm than Absalom. Take your lord's servants and pursue him, lest he get himself to fortified cities and escape from us.”
10_20_6.NASB: And David said to Abishai, 'Now Sheba the son of Bichri will do us more harm than Absalom; take your lord's servants and pursue him, so that he does not find for himself fortified cities and escape from our sight.'
10_20_6.NLT: Then David said to Abishai, “Sheba son of Bicri is going to hurt us more than Absalom did. Quick, take my troops and chase after him before he gets into a fortified town where we can’t reach him.”
10_20_6.WEB: David said to Abishai, “Now Sheba the son of Bichri will do us more harm than Absalom did. Take your lord’s servants, and pursue after him, lest he get himself fortified cities, and escape out of our sight.”
10_20_6.CSB17: So David said to Abishai, “Sheba son of Bichri will do more harm to us than Absalom. Take your lord’s soldiers and pursue him, or he will find fortified cities and elude us.”
10_20_6.AMP: And David said to Abishai [his nephew], "Now Sheba the son of Bichri will do us more harm than Absalom did. Take your lord's servants and pursue him, so that he does not find fortified cities for himself and escape from our sight."
10_20_6.MSG: So David told Abishai, "Sheba son of Bicri is going to hurt us even worse than Absalom did. Take your master's servants and hunt him down before he gets holed up in some fortress city where we can't get to him."
10_20_6.NIV: David said to Abishai, “Now Sheba son of Bicri will do us more harm than Absalom did. Take your master’s men and pursue him, or he will find fortified cities and escape from us.”
10_20_6.NRSVCE: David said to Abishai, “Now Sheba son of Bichri will do us more harm than Absalom; take your lord’s servants and pursue him, or he will find fortified cities for himself, and escape from us.”

10_20_7.NKJV: So Joab’s men, with the Cherethites, the Pelethites, and all the mighty men, went out after him. And they went out of Jerusalem to pursue Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_7.KJV: And there went out after him Joab's men, and the Cherethites, and the Pelethites, and all the mighty men: and they went out of Jerusalem, to pursue after Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_7.ESV: And there went out after him Joab's men and the Cherethites and the Pelethites, and all the mighty men. They went out from Jerusalem to pursue Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_7.NASB: So Joab's men went out after him, along with the Cherethites and the Pelethites and all the mighty men; and they went out from Jerusalem to pursue Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_7.NLT: So Abishai and Joab, together with the king’s bodyguard and all the mighty warriors, set out from Jerusalem to go after Sheba.
10_20_7.WEB: Joab’s men went out after him, and the Cherethites and the Pelethites, and all the mighty men; and they went out of Jerusalem, to pursue Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_7.CSB17: So Joab’s men, the Cherethites, the Pelethites, and all the warriors marched out under Abishai’s command; they left Jerusalem to pursue Sheba son of Bichri.
10_20_7.AMP: So Joab's men went after him, along with [David's bodyguards] the Cherethites and Pelethites and all the warriors; they went out from Jerusalem to pursue Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_7.MSG: So under Abishai's command, all the best men—Joab's men and the Kerethites and Pelethites—left Jerusalem to hunt down Sheba son of Bicri.
10_20_7.NIV: So Joab’s men and the Kerethites and Pelethites and all the mighty warriors went out under the command of Abishai. They marched out from Jerusalem to pursue Sheba son of Bicri.
10_20_7.NRSVCE: Joab’s men went out after him, along with the Cherethites, the Pelethites, and all the warriors; they went out from Jerusalem to pursue Sheba son of Bichri.

10_20_8.NKJV: When they were at the large stone which is in Gibeon, Amasa came before them. Now Joab was dressed in battle armor; on it was a belt with a sword fastened in its sheath at his hips; and as he was going forward, it fell out.
10_20_8.KJV: When they were at the great stone which is in Gibeon, Amasa went before them. And Joab's garment that he had put on was girded unto him, and upon it a girdle with a sword fastened upon his loins in the sheath thereof; and as he went forth it fell out.
10_20_8.ESV: When they were at the great stone that is in Gibeon, Amasa came to meet them. Now Joab was wearing a soldier's garment, and over it was a belt with a sword in its sheath fastened on his thigh, and as he went forward it fell out.
10_20_8.NASB: When they were at the large stone which is in Gibeon, Amasa came to meet them. Now Joab was dressed in his military attire, and over it was a belt with a sword in its sheath fastened at his waist; and as he went forward, it fell out.
10_20_8.NLT: As they arrived at the great stone in Gibeon, Amasa met them. Joab was wearing his military tunic with a dagger strapped to his belt. As he stepped forward to greet Amasa, he slipped the dagger from its sheath.
10_20_8.WEB: When they were at the great stone which is in Gibeon, Amasa came to meet them. Joab was clothed in his apparel of war that he had put on, and on it was a sash with a sword fastened on his waist in its sheath; and as he went along it fell out.
10_20_8.CSB17: They were at the great stone in Gibeon when Amasa joined them. Joab was wearing his uniform and over it was a belt around his waist with a sword in its sheath. As he approached, the sword fell out.
10_20_8.AMP: When they were at the great stone in Gibeon, Amasa came to meet them. Now Joab was wearing his military uniform, and over it he had a belt with a sheathed sword strapped around his hips; and as he went forward, it fell out.
10_20_8.MSG: They were near the boulder at Gibeon when Amasa came their way. Joab was wearing a tunic with a sheathed sword strapped on his waist, but the sword slipped out and fell to the ground.
10_20_8.NIV: While they were at the great rock in Gibeon, Amasa came to meet them. Joab was wearing his military tunic, and strapped over it at his waist was a belt with a dagger in its sheath. As he stepped forward, it dropped out of its sheath.
10_20_8.NRSVCE: When they were at the large stone that is in Gibeon, Amasa came to meet them. Now Joab was wearing a soldier’s garment and over it was a belt with a sword in its sheath fastened at his waist; as he went forward it fell out.

10_20_9.NKJV: Then Joab said to Amasa, “Are you in health, my brother?” And Joab took Amasa by the beard with his right hand to kiss him.
10_20_9.KJV: And Joab said to Amasa, Art thou in health, my brother? And Joab took Amasa by the beard with the right hand to kiss him.
10_20_9.ESV: And Joab said to Amasa, “Is it well with you, my brother?” And Joab took Amasa by the beard with his right hand to kiss him.
10_20_9.NASB: Joab said to Amasa, 'Is it well with you, my brother?' And Joab took Amasa by the beard with his right hand to kiss him.
10_20_9.NLT: “How are you, my cousin?” Joab said and took him by the beard with his right hand as though to kiss him.
10_20_9.WEB: Joab said to Amasa, “Is it well with you, my brother?” Joab took Amasa by the beard with his right hand to kiss him.
10_20_9.CSB17: Joab asked Amasa, “Are you well, my brother?” Then with his right hand Joab grabbed Amasa by the beard to kiss him.
10_20_9.AMP: Joab said to Amasa, "Is it going well with you, my brother?" And with his right hand Joab took hold of Amasa by the beard [as if] to kiss him [in greeting].
10_20_9.MSG: Joab greeted Amasa, "How are you, brother?" and took Amasa's beard in his right hand as if to kiss him.
10_20_9.NIV: Joab said to Amasa, “How are you, my brother?” Then Joab took Amasa by the beard with his right hand to kiss him.
10_20_9.NRSVCE: Joab said to Amasa, “Is it well with you, my brother?” And Joab took Amasa by the beard with his right hand to kiss him.

10_20_10.NKJV: But Amasa did not notice the sword that was in Joab’s hand. And he struck him with it in the stomach, and his entrails poured out on the ground; and he did not strike him again. Thus he died. Then Joab and Abishai his brother pursued Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_10.KJV: But Amasa took no heed to the sword that was in Joab's hand: so he smote him therewith in the fifth rib, and shed out his bowels to the ground, and struck him not again; and he died. So Joab and Abishai his brother pursued after Sheba the son of Bichri. struck: Heb. doubled not his stroke
10_20_10.ESV: But Amasa did not observe the sword that was in Joab's hand. So Joab struck him with it in the stomach and spilled his entrails to the ground without striking a second blow, and he died. Then Joab and Abishai his brother pursued Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_10.NASB: But Amasa was not on guard against the sword which was in Joab's hand so he struck him in the belly with it and poured out his inward parts on the ground, and did not [strike] him again, and he died. Then Joab and Abishai his brother pursued Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_10.NLT: Amasa didn’t notice the dagger in his left hand, and Joab stabbed him in the stomach with it so that his insides gushed out onto the ground. Joab did not need to strike again, and Amasa soon died. Joab and his brother Abishai left him lying there and continued after Sheba.
10_20_10.WEB: But Amasa took no heed to the sword that was in Joab’s hand. So he struck him with it in the body, and shed out his bowels to the ground, and didn’t strike him again; and he died. Joab and Abishai his brother pursued Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_10.CSB17: Amasa was not on guard against the sword in Joab’s hand, and Joab stabbed him in the stomach with it and spilled his intestines out on the ground. Joab did not stab him again, and Amasa died. Joab and his brother Abishai pursued Sheba son of Bichri.
10_20_10.AMP: But Amasa [who had replaced Joab as David's commander] was off guard and not attentive to the sword in Joab's hand. So Joab struck Amasa in the abdomen with the sword, spilling his intestines to the ground. Without another blow Amasa died. Then Joab and Abishai his brother pursued Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_10.MSG: Amasa didn't notice the sword in Joab's other hand. Joab stuck him in the belly and his guts spilled to the ground. A second blow wasn't needed; he was dead. Then Joab and his brother Abishai continued to chase Sheba son of Bicri.
10_20_10.NIV: Amasa was not on his guard against the dagger in Joab’s hand, and Joab plunged it into his belly, and his intestines spilled out on the ground. Without being stabbed again, Amasa died. Then Joab and his brother Abishai pursued Sheba son of Bicri.
10_20_10.NRSVCE: But Amasa did not notice the sword in Joab’s hand; Joab struck him in the belly so that his entrails poured out on the ground, and he died. He did not strike a second blow. Then Joab and his brother Abishai pursued Sheba son of Bichri.

10_20_11.NKJV: Meanwhile one of Joab’s men stood near Amasa, and said, “Whoever favors Joab and whoever is for David—follow Joab!”
10_20_11.KJV: And one of Joab's men stood by him, and said, He that favoureth Joab, and he that is for David, let him go after Joab.
10_20_11.ESV: And one of Joab's young men took his stand by Amasa and said, “Whoever favors Joab, and whoever is for David, let him follow Joab.”
10_20_11.NASB: Now there stood by him one of Joab's young men, and said, 'Whoever favors Joab and whoever is for David, [let him] follow Joab.'
10_20_11.NLT: One of Joab’s young men shouted to Amasa’s troops, “If you are for Joab and David, come and follow Joab.”
10_20_11.WEB: One of Joab’s young men stood by him, and said, “He who favors Joab, and he who is for David, let him follow Joab!”
10_20_11.CSB17: One of Joab’s young men had stood over Amasa saying, “Whoever favors Joab and whoever is for David, follow Joab!”
10_20_11.AMP: Now one of Joab's young men stood by him and said, "Whoever favors Joab and is for David, let him follow Joab!"
10_20_11.MSG: One of Joab's soldiers took up his post over the body and called out, "Everyone who sides with Joab and supports David, follow Joab!"
10_20_11.NIV: One of Joab’s men stood beside Amasa and said, “Whoever favors Joab, and whoever is for David, let him follow Joab!”
10_20_11.NRSVCE: And one of Joab’s men took his stand by Amasa, and said, “Whoever favors Joab, and whoever is for David, let him follow Joab.”

10_20_12.NKJV: But Amasa wallowed in his blood in the middle of the highway. And when the man saw that all the people stood still, he moved Amasa from the highway to the field and threw a garment over him, when he saw that everyone who came upon him halted.
10_20_12.KJV: And Amasa wallowed in blood in the midst of the highway. And when the man saw that all the people stood still, he removed Amasa out of the highway into the field, and cast a cloth upon him, when he saw that every one that came by him stood still.
10_20_12.ESV: And Amasa lay wallowing in his blood in the highway. And anyone who came by, seeing him, stopped. And when the man saw that all the people stopped, he carried Amasa out of the highway into the field and threw a garment over him.
10_20_12.NASB: But Amasa lay wallowing in [his] blood in the middle of the highway. And when the man saw that all the people stood still, he removed Amasa from the highway into the field and threw a garment over him when he saw that everyone who came by him stood still.
10_20_12.NLT: But Amasa lay in his blood in the middle of the road, and Joab’s man saw that everyone was stopping to stare at him. So he pulled him off the road into a field and threw a cloak over him.
10_20_12.WEB: Amasa lay wallowing in his blood in the middle of the highway. When the man saw that all the people stood still, he carried Amasa out of the highway into the field, and cast a garment over him, when he saw that everyone who came by him stood still.
10_20_12.CSB17: Now Amasa had been writhing in his blood in the middle of the highway, and the man had seen that all the troops stopped. So he moved Amasa from the highway to the field and threw a garment over him because he realized that all those who encountered Amasa were stopping.
10_20_12.AMP: But Amasa was wallowing in his blood in the middle of the highway. And when the man saw that all the people who came by stopped [to look], he moved Amasa from the highway into the field and threw a garment over him when he saw that everyone who came by Amasa stopped.
10_20_12.MSG: Amasa was lying in a pool of blood in the middle of the road; the man realized that the whole army was going to stop and take a look, so he pulled Amasa's corpse off the road into the field and threw a blanket over him so it wouldn't collect spectators.
10_20_12.NIV: Amasa lay wallowing in his blood in the middle of the road, and the man saw that all the troops came to a halt there. When he realized that everyone who came up to Amasa stopped, he dragged him from the road into a field and threw a garment over him.
10_20_12.NRSVCE: Amasa lay wallowing in his blood on the highway, and the man saw that all the people were stopping. Since he saw that all who came by him were stopping, he carried Amasa from the highway into a field, and threw a garment over him.

10_20_13.NKJV: When he was removed from the highway, all the people went on after Joab to pursue Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_13.KJV: When he was removed out of the highway, all the people went on after Joab, to pursue after Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_13.ESV: When he was taken out of the highway, all the people went on after Joab to pursue Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_13.NASB: As soon as he was removed from the highway, all the men passed on after Joab to pursue Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_13.NLT: With Amasa’s body out of the way, everyone went on with Joab to capture Sheba son of Bicri.
10_20_13.WEB: When he was removed out of the highway, all the people went on after Joab, to pursue Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_13.CSB17: When he was removed from the highway, all the men passed by and followed Joab to pursue Sheba son of Bichri.
10_20_13.AMP: When [the body of] Amasa was removed from the highway, everyone followed after Joab to pursue Sheba the son of Bichri.
10_20_13.MSG: As soon as he'd gotten him off the road, the traffic flowed normally, following Joab in the chase after Sheba son of Bicri.
10_20_13.NIV: After Amasa had been removed from the road, all the men went on with Joab to pursue Sheba son of Bicri.
10_20_13.NRSVCE: Once he was removed from the highway, all the people went on after Joab to pursue Sheba son of Bichri.

10_20_14.NKJV: And he went through all the tribes of Israel to Abel and Beth Maachah and all the Berites. So they were gathered together and also went after Sheba.
10_20_14.KJV: And he went through all the tribes of Israel unto Abel, and to Bethmaachah, and all the Berites: and they were gathered together , and went also after him.
10_20_14.ESV: And Sheba passed through all the tribes of Israel to Abel of Beth-maacah, and all the Bichrites assembled and followed him in.
10_20_14.NASB: Now he went through all the tribes of Israel to Abel, even Beth-maacah, and all the Berites; and they were gathered together and also went after him.
10_20_14.NLT: Meanwhile, Sheba traveled through all the tribes of Israel and eventually came to the town of Abel-beth-maacah. All the members of his own clan, the Bicrites, assembled for battle and followed him into the town.
10_20_14.WEB: He went through all the tribes of Israel to Abel, and to Beth Maacah, and all the Berites. They were gathered together, and went also after him.
10_20_14.CSB17: Sheba passed through all the tribes of Israel to Abel of Beth-maacah. All the Berites came together and followed him.
10_20_14.AMP: Now Joab went through all the tribes of Israel to Abel, even Beth-maacah, and all the Berites; and they assembled and also went after Sheba.
10_20_14.MSG: Sheba passed through all the tribes of Israel as far as Abel Beth Maacah; all the Bicrites clustered and followed him into the city.
10_20_14.NIV: Sheba passed through all the tribes of Israel to Abel Beth Maacah and through the entire region of the Berites, who gathered together and followed him.
10_20_14.NRSVCE: Sheba passed through all the tribes of Israel to Abel of Beth-maacah; and all the Bichrites assembled, and followed him inside.

10_20_15.NKJV: Then they came and besieged him in Abel of Beth Maachah; and they cast up a siege mound against the city, and it stood by the rampart. And all the people who were with Joab battered the wall to throw it down.
10_20_15.KJV: And they came and besieged him in Abel of Bethmaachah, and they cast up a bank against the city, and it stood in the trench: and all the people that were with Joab battered the wall, to throw it down. in the trench: or, against the outmost wall battered: Heb. marred to throw down
10_20_15.ESV: And all the men who were with Joab came and besieged him in Abel of Beth-maacah. They cast up a mound against the city, and it stood against the rampart, and they were battering the wall to throw it down.
10_20_15.NASB: They came and besieged him in Abel Beth-maacah, and they cast up a siege ramp against the city, and it stood by the rampart; and all the people who were with Joab were wreaking destruction in order to topple the wall.
10_20_15.NLT: When Joab’s forces arrived, they attacked Abel-beth-maacah. They built a siege ramp against the town’s fortifications and began battering down the wall.
10_20_15.WEB: They came and besieged him in Abel of Beth Maacah, and they cast up a mound against the city, and it stood against the rampart; and all the people who were with Joab battered the wall, to throw it down.
10_20_15.CSB17: Joab’s troops came and besieged Sheba in Abel of Beth-maacah. They built a siege ramp against the outer wall of the city. While all the troops with Joab were battering the wall to make it collapse,
10_20_15.AMP: And the army of Joab came and besieged Sheba in Abel Beth-maacah, and they built up an assault ramp against the city, and it stood against the outer rampart; and all the people who were with Joab were wreaking destruction to make the wall fall.
10_20_15.MSG: Joab's army arrived and laid siege to Sheba in Abel Beth Maacah. They built a siege-ramp up against the city's fortification. The plan was to knock down the wall.
10_20_15.NIV: All the troops with Joab came and besieged Sheba in Abel Beth Maacah. They built a siege ramp up to the city, and it stood against the outer fortifications. While they were battering the wall to bring it down,
10_20_15.NRSVCE: Joab’s forces came and besieged him in Abel of Beth-maacah; they threw up a siege ramp against the city, and it stood against the rampart. Joab’s forces were battering the wall to break it down.

10_20_16.NKJV: Then a wise woman cried out from the city, “Hear, hear! Please say to Joab, ‘Come nearby, that I may speak with you.’ ”

10_21_1.NKJV: Now there was a famine in the days of David for three years, year after year; and David inquired of the LORD. And the LORD answered, “It is because of Saul and his bloodthirsty house, because he killed the Gibeonites.”
10_21_1.KJV: Then there was a famine in the days of David three years, year after year; and David enquired of the LORD. And the LORD answered, It is for Saul, and for his bloody house, because he slew the Gibeonites. enquired: Heb. sought the face, etc
10_21_1.ESV: Now there was a famine in the days of David for three years, year after year. And David sought the face of the LORD. And the LORD said, “There is bloodguilt on Saul and on his house, because he put the Gibeonites to death.”
10_21_1.NASB: Now there was a famine in the days of David for three years, year after year; and David sought the presence of the LORD. And the LORD said, 'It is for Saul and his bloody house, because he put the Gibeonites to death.'
10_21_1.NLT: There was a famine during David’s reign that lasted for three years, so David asked the LORD about it. And the LORD said, “The famine has come because Saul and his family are guilty of murdering the Gibeonites.”
10_21_1.WEB: There was a famine in the days of David for three years, year after year; and David sought the face of Yahweh. Yahweh said, “It is for Saul, and for his bloody house, because he put the Gibeonites to death.”
10_21_1.CSB17: During David’s reign there was a famine for three successive years, so David inquired of the LORD. The LORD answered, “It is due to Saul and to his bloody family, because he killed the Gibeonites.”
10_21_1.AMP: There was famine in the days of David for three consecutive years; and David sought the presence (face) of the LORD [asking the reason]. The LORD replied, "It is because of Saul and his bloody house, because he put the Gibeonites to death."
10_21_1.MSG: There was a famine in David's time. It went on year after year after year—three years. David went to God seeking the reason.God said, "This is because there is blood on Saul and his house, from the time he massacred the Gibeonites."
10_21_1.NIV: The Gibeonites AvengedDuring the reign of David, there was a famine for three successive years; so David sought the face of the Lord. The Lord said, “It is on account of Saul and his blood-stained house; it is because he put the Gibeonites to death.”
10_21_1.NRSVCE: Now there was a famine in the days of David for three years, year after year; and David inquired of the LORD. The LORD said, “There is bloodguilt on Saul and on his house, because he put the Gibeonites to death.”

10_21_2.NKJV: So the king called the Gibeonites and spoke to them. Now the Gibeonites were not of the children of Israel, but of the remnant of the Amorites; the children of Israel had sworn protection to them, but Saul had sought to kill them in his zeal for the children of Israel and Judah.
10_21_2.KJV: And the king called the Gibeonites, and said unto them; (now the Gibeonites were not of the children of Israel, but of the remnant of the Amorites; and the children of Israel had sworn unto them: and Saul sought to slay them in his zeal to the children of Israel and Judah.)
10_21_2.ESV: So the king called the Gibeonites and spoke to them. Now the Gibeonites were not of the people of Israel but of the remnant of the Amorites. Although the people of Israel had sworn to spare them, Saul had sought to strike them down in his zeal for the people of Israel and Judah.
10_21_2.NASB: So the king called the Gibeonites and spoke to them (now the Gibeonites were not of the sons of Israel but of the remnant of the Amorites, and the sons of Israel made a covenant with them, but Saul had sought to kill them in his zeal for the sons of Israel and Judah).
10_21_2.NLT: So the king summoned the Gibeonites. They were not part of Israel but were all that was left of the nation of the Amorites. The people of Israel had sworn not to kill them, but Saul, in his zeal for Israel and Judah, had tried to wipe them out.
10_21_2.WEB: The king called the Gibeonites, and said to them (now the Gibeonites were not of the children of Israel, but of the remnant of the Amorites, and the children of Israel had sworn to them; and Saul sought to kill them in his zeal for the children of Israel and Judah);
10_21_2.CSB17: The Gibeonites were not Israelites but rather a remnant of the Amorites. The Israelites had taken an oath concerning them, but Saul had tried to kill them in his zeal for the Israelites and Judah. So David summoned the Gibeonites and spoke to them.
10_21_2.AMP: So the king called the Gibeonites and spoke to them (now the Gibeonites were not of the sons (descendants) of Israel but of the remnant (survivors) of the Amorites. The Israelites had sworn [an oath] to [spare] them, but Saul in his zeal for the sons of Israel and Judah had sought to strike down the Gibeonites).
10_21_2.MSG: So the king called the Gibeonites together for consultation. (The Gibeonites were not part of Israel; they were what was left of the Amorites, and protected by a treaty with Israel. But Saul, a fanatic for the honor of Israel and Judah, tried to kill them off.)
10_21_2.NIV: The king summoned the Gibeonites and spoke to them. (Now the Gibeonites were not a part of Israel but were survivors of the Amorites; the Israelites had sworn to spare them, but Saul in his zeal for Israel and Judah had tried to annihilate them.)
10_21_2.NRSVCE: So the king called the Gibeonites and spoke to them. (Now the Gibeonites were not of the people of Israel, but of the remnant of the Amorites; although the people of Israel had sworn to spare them, Saul had tried to wipe them out in his zeal for the people of Israel and Judah.)

10_21_3.NKJV: Therefore David said to the Gibeonites, “What shall I do for you? And with what shall I make atonement, that you may bless the inheritance of the LORD?”

10_21_3.ESV: And David said to the Gibeonites, “What shall I do for you? And how shall I make atonement, that you may bless the heritage of the LORD?”
10_21_3.NASB: Thus David said to the Gibeonites, 'What should I do for you? And how can I make atonement that you may bless the inheritance of the LORD?'
10_21_3.NLT: David asked them, “What can I do for you? How can I make amends so that you will bless the LORD’s people again?”
10_21_3.WEB: and David said to the Gibeonites, “What should I do for you? And with what should I make atonement, that you may bless Yahweh’s inheritance?”
10_21_3.CSB17: He asked the Gibeonites, “What should I do for you? How can I make atonement so that you will bring a blessing on the LORD’s inheritance?”
10_21_3.AMP: So David said to the Gibeonites, "What should I do for you? How can I make it good so that you will bless the Lord's inheritance (Israel)?"
10_21_3.MSG: David addressed the Gibeonites: "What can I do for you? How can I compensate you so that you will bless God's legacy of land and people?"
10_21_3.NIV: David asked the Gibeonites, “What shall I do for you? How shall I make amends so that you will bless the Lord’s inheritance?”
10_21_3.NRSVCE: David said to the Gibeonites, “What shall I do for you? How shall I make expiation, that you may bless the heritage of the LORD?”

10_21_4.NKJV: And the Gibeonites said to him, “We will have no silver or gold from Saul or from his house, nor shall you kill any man in Israel for us.” So he said, “Whatever you say, I will do for you.”

10_22_13.NKJV: From the brightness before Him Coals of fire were kindled.
10_22_13.KJV: Through the brightness before him were coals of fire kindled.
10_22_13.ESV: Out of the brightness before him coals of fire flamed forth.
10_22_13.NASB: 'From the brightness before Him Coals of fire were kindled.

10_22_13.AMP: "Out of the brightness before Him Coals of fire were set aflame.
10_22_13.MSG: But his cloud-brightness burst through,a grand comet of fireworks.
10_22_13.NIV: Out of the brightness of his presencebolts of lightning blazed forth.
10_22_13.NRSVCE: Out of the brightness before him coals of fire flamed forth.

10_22_14.NKJV: “The LORD thundered from heaven, And the Most High uttered His voice.
10_22_14.KJV: The LORD thundered from heaven, and the most High uttered his voice.
10_22_14.ESV: The LORD thundered from heaven, and the Most High uttered his voice.
10_22_14.NASB: 'The LORD thundered from heaven, And the Most High uttered His voice.
10_22_14.NLT: The LORD thundered from heaven;the voice of the Most High resounded.
10_22_14.WEB: Yahweh thundered from heaven. The Most High uttered his voice.
10_22_14.CSB17: The LORD thundered from heaven; the Most High made his voice heard.
10_22_14.AMP: "The LORD thundered from heaven, And the Most High uttered His voice.
10_22_14.MSG: Then God thundered out of heaven;the High God gave a great shout.
10_22_14.NIV: The Lord thundered from heaven;the voice of the Most High resounded.
10_22_14.NRSVCE: The LORD thundered from heaven; the Most High uttered his voice.

10_22_15.NKJV: He sent out arrows and scattered them; Lightning bolts, and He vanquished them.

10_22_15.ESV: And he sent out arrows and scattered them; lightning, and routed them.
10_22_15.NASB: 'And He sent out arrows, and scattered them, Lightning, and routed them.
10_22_15.NLT: He shot arrows and scattered his enemies;his lightning flashed, and they were confused.
10_22_15.WEB: He sent out arrows, and scattered them; lightning, and confused them.
10_22_15.CSB17: He shot arrows and scattered them; he hurled lightning bolts and routed them.
10_22_15.AMP: "He sent out arrows and scattered them; Lightning, and confused them.
10_22_15.MSG: God shot his arrows—pandemonium!He hurled his lightnings—a rout!
10_22_15.NIV: He shot arrows and scattered the enemies,bolts of lightning and routed them.
10_22_15.NRSVCE: He sent out arrows, and scattered them — lightning, and routed them.

10_22_16.NKJV: Then the channels of the sea were seen, The foundations of the world were uncovered, At the rebuke of the LORD, At the blast of the breath of His nostrils.
10_22_16.KJV: And the channels of the sea appeared, the foundations of the world were discovered, at the rebuking of the LORD, at the blast of the breath of his nostrils.
10_22_16.ESV: Then the channels of the sea were seen; the foundations of the world were laid bare, at the rebuke of the LORD, at the blast of the breath of his nostrils.
10_22_16.NASB: 'Then the channels of the sea appeared, The foundations of the world were laid bare By the rebuke of the LORD, At the blast of the breath of His nostrils.
10_22_16.NLT: Then at the command of the LORD,at the blast of his breath,the bottom of the sea could be seen,and the foundations of the earth were laid bare.
10_22_16.WEB: Then the channels of the sea appeared. The foundations of the world were laid bare by Yahweh’s rebuke, at the blast of the breath of his nostrils.
10_22_16.CSB17: The depths of the sea became visible, the foundations of the world were exposed at the rebuke of the LORD, at the blast of the breath of his nostrils.
10_22_16.AMP: "The channels of the sea appeared, The foundations of the world were uncovered At the rebuke of the LORD, At the blast of the breath of His nostrils.
10_22_16.MSG: The secret sources of ocean were exposed,the hidden depths of earth lay uncoveredThe moment God roared in protest,let loose his hurricane anger.
10_22_16.NIV: The valleys of the sea were exposedand the foundations of the earth laid bareat the rebuke of the Lord,at the blast of breath from his nostrils.
10_22_16.NRSVCE: Then the channels of the sea were seen, the foundations of the world were laid bare at the rebuke of the LORD, at the blast of the breath of his nostrils.

10_22_17.NKJV: “He sent from above, He took me, He drew me out of many waters.
10_22_17.KJV: He sent from above, he took me; he drew me out of many waters; many: or, great
10_22_17.ESV: “He sent from on high, he took me; he drew me out of many waters.
10_22_17.NASB: 'He sent from on high, He took me; He drew me out of many waters.
10_22_17.NLT: “He reached down from heaven and rescued me;he drew me out of deep waters.
10_22_17.WEB: He sent from on high and he took me. He drew me out of many waters.
10_22_17.CSB17: He reached down from on high and took hold of me; he pulled me out of deep water.
10_22_17.AMP: "He sent from above, He took me; He drew me out of great waters.
10_22_17.MSG: But me he caught—reached all the wayfrom sky to sea; he pulled me out
10_22_17.NIV: “He reached down from on high and took hold of me;he drew me out of deep waters.
10_22_17.NRSVCE: He reached from on high, he took me, he drew me out of mighty waters.

10_22_18.NKJV: He delivered me from my strong enemy, From those who hated me; For they were too strong for me.
10_22_18.KJV: He delivered me from my strong enemy, and from them that hated me: for they were too strong for me.
10_22_18.ESV: He rescued me from my strong enemy, from those who hated me, for they were too mighty for me.
10_22_18.NASB: 'He delivered me from my strong enemy, From those who hated me, for they were too strong for me.
10_22_18.NLT: He rescued me from my powerful enemies,from those who hated me and were too strong for me.
10_22_18.WEB: He delivered me from my strong enemy, from those who hated me, for they were too mighty for me.
10_22_18.CSB17: He rescued me from my powerful enemy and from those who hated me, for they were too strong for me.
10_22_18.AMP: "He rescued me from my strong enemy, From those who hated me, for they were too mighty for me.
10_22_18.MSG: Of that ocean of hate, that enemy chaos,the void in which I was drowning.
10_22_18.NIV: He rescued me from my powerful enemy,from my foes, who were too strong for me.
10_22_18.NRSVCE: He delivered me from my strong enemy, from those who hated me; for they were too mighty for me.

10_22_19.NKJV: They confronted me in the day of my calamity, But the LORD was my support.
10_22_19.KJV: They prevented me in the day of my calamity: but the LORD was my stay.
10_22_19.ESV: They confronted me in the day of my calamity, but the LORD was my support.
10_22_19.NASB: 'They confronted me in the day of my calamity, But the LORD was my support.
10_22_19.NLT: They attacked me at a moment when I was in distress,but the LORD supported me.
10_22_19.WEB: They came on me in the day of my calamity, but Yahweh was my support.
10_22_19.CSB17: They confronted me in the day of my calamity, but the LORD was my support.
10_22_19.AMP: "They came upon me in the day of my calamity, But the LORD was my support.
10_22_19.MSG: They hit me when I was down,but God stuck by me.
10_22_19.NIV: They confronted me in the day of my disaster,but the Lord was my support.
10_22_19.NRSVCE: They came upon me in the day of my calamity, but the LORD was my stay.

10_22_20.NKJV: He also brought me out into a broad place; He delivered me because He delighted in me.
10_22_20.KJV: He brought me forth also into a large place: he delivered me, because he delighted in me.
10_22_20.ESV: He brought me out into a broad place; he rescued me, because he delighted in me.
10_22_20.NASB: 'He also brought me forth into a broad place; He rescued me, because He delighted in me.
10_22_20.NLT: He led me to a place of safety;he rescued me because he delights in me.
10_22_20.WEB: He also brought me out into a large place. He delivered me, because he delighted in me.
10_22_20.CSB17: He brought me out to a spacious place; he rescued me because he delighted in me.
10_22_20.AMP: "He also brought me out to an open place; He rescued me because He delighted in me.
10_22_20.MSG: He stood me up on a wide-open field;I stood there saved—surprised to be loved!
10_22_20.NIV: He brought me out into a spacious place;he rescued me because he delighted in me.
10_22_20.NRSVCE: He brought me out into a broad place; he delivered me, because he delighted in me.

10_22_21.NKJV: “The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness; According to the cleanness of my hands He has recompensed me.
10_22_21.KJV: The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness: according to the cleanness of my hands hath he recompensed me.
10_22_21.ESV: “The LORD dealt with me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands he rewarded me.
10_22_21.NASB: 'The LORD has rewarded me according to my righteousness; According to the cleanness of my hands He has recompensed me.
10_22_21.NLT: The LORD rewarded me for doing right;he restored me because of my innocence.
10_22_21.WEB: Yahweh rewarded me according to my righteousness. He rewarded me according to the cleanness of my hands.
10_22_21.CSB17: The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness; he repaid me according to the cleanness of my hands.
10_22_21.AMP: "The LORD has dealt with me according to my righteousness; According to the cleanness of my hands He has rewarded me.
10_22_21.MSG: God made my life completewhen I placed all the pieces before him.When I cleaned up my act,he gave me a fresh start.
10_22_21.NIV: “The Lord has dealt with me according to my righteousness;according to the cleanness of my hands he has rewarded me.
10_22_21.NRSVCE: The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands he recompensed me.

10_22_22.NKJV: For I have kept the ways of the LORD, And have not wickedly departed from my God.
10_22_22.KJV: For I have kept the ways of the LORD, and have not wickedly departed from my God.
10_22_22.ESV: For I have kept the ways of the LORD and have not wickedly departed from my God.
10_22_22.NASB: 'For I have kept the ways of the LORD, And have not acted wickedly against my God.
10_22_22.NLT: For I have kept the ways of the LORD;I have not turned from my God to follow evil.
10_22_22.WEB: For I have kept Yahweh’s ways, and have not wickedly departed from my God.
10_22_22.CSB17: For I have kept the ways of the LORD and have not turned from my God to wickedness.
10_22_22.AMP: "For I have kept the ways of the LORD, And have not acted wickedly against my God.
10_22_22.MSG: Indeed, I've kept alert to God's ways;I haven't taken God for granted.
10_22_22.NIV: For I have kept the ways of the Lord;I have not done evil by turning from my God.
10_22_22.NRSVCE: For I have kept the ways of the LORD, and have not wickedly departed from my God.

10_22_23.NKJV: For all His judgments were before me; And as for His statutes, I did not depart from them.
10_22_23.KJV: For all his judgments were before me: and as for his statutes, I did not depart from them.
10_22_23.ESV: For all his rules were before me, and from his statutes I did not turn aside.
10_22_23.NASB: 'For all His ordinances [were] before me, And [as for] His statutes, I did not depart from them.
10_22_23.NLT: I have followed all his regulations;I have never abandoned his decrees.
10_22_23.WEB: For all his ordinances were before me. As for his statutes, I did not depart from them.
10_22_23.CSB17: Indeed, I let all his ordinances guide me and have not disregarded his statutes.
10_22_23.AMP: "For all His judgments (legal decisions) were before me, And from His statutes I did not turn aside.

10_22_23.NIV: All his laws are before me;I have not turned away from his decrees.
10_22_23.NRSVCE: For all his ordinances were before me, and from his statutes I did not turn aside.

10_22_24.NKJV: I was also blameless before Him, And I kept myself from my iniquity.
10_22_24.KJV: I was also upright before him, and have kept myself from mine iniquity. before: Heb. to
10_22_24.ESV: I was blameless before him, and I kept myself from guilt.
10_22_24.NASB: 'I was also blameless toward Him, And I kept myself from my iniquity.
10_22_24.NLT: I am blameless before God;I have kept myself from sin.
10_22_24.WEB: I was also perfect toward him. I kept myself from my iniquity.
10_22_24.CSB17: I was blameless before him and kept myself from my iniquity.
10_22_24.AMP: "I was also blameless before Him, And kept myself from wrongdoing.
10_22_24.MSG: I feel put back together,and I'm watching my step.
10_22_24.NIV: I have been blameless before himand have kept myself from sin.
10_22_24.NRSVCE: I was blameless before him, and I kept myself from guilt.

10_22_25.NKJV: Therefore the LORD has recompensed me according to my righteousness, According to my cleanness in His eyes.
10_22_25.KJV: Therefore the LORD hath recompensed me according to my righteousness; according to my cleanness in his eye sight . in: Heb. before his eyes
10_22_25.ESV: And the LORD has rewarded me according to my righteousness, according to my cleanness in his sight.
10_22_25.NASB: 'Therefore the LORD has recompensed me according to my righteousness, According to my cleanness before His eyes.
10_22_25.NLT: The LORD rewarded me for doing right.He has seen my innocence.

10_22_25.CSB17: So the LORD repaid me according to my righteousness, according to my cleanness in his sight.
10_22_25.AMP: "Therefore the LORD has rewarded me according to my righteousness, According to my cleanness in His sight.
10_22_25.MSG: God rewrote the text of my lifewhen I opened the book of my heart to his eyes.
10_22_25.NIV: The Lord has rewarded me according to my righteousness,according to my cleanness in his sight.
10_22_25.NRSVCE: Therefore the LORD has recompensed me according to my righteousness, according to my cleanness in his sight.

10_22_26.NKJV: “With the merciful You will show Yourself merciful; With a blameless man You will show Yourself blameless;
