10_22_26.ESV: “With the merciful you show yourself merciful; with the blameless man you show yourself blameless;
10_22_26.NASB: 'With the kind You show Yourself kind, With the blameless You show Yourself blameless;
10_22_26.NLT: “To the faithful you show yourself faithful;to those with integrity you show integrity.
10_22_26.WEB: With the merciful you will show yourself merciful. With the perfect man you will show yourself perfect.
10_22_26.CSB17: With the faithful you prove yourself faithful, with the blameless you prove yourself blameless,
10_22_26.AMP: "With the loving and loyal You show Yourself loving and loyal, With the blameless You show Yourself blameless.
10_22_26.MSG: You stick by people who stick with you,you're straight with people who're straight with you,
10_22_26.NIV: “To the faithful you show yourself faithful,to the blameless you show yourself blameless,
10_22_26.NRSVCE: With the loyal you show yourself loyal; with the blameless you show yourself blameless;

10_22_27.NKJV: With the pure You will show Yourself pure; And with the devious You will show Yourself shrewd.
10_22_27.KJV: With the pure thou wilt shew thyself pure; and with the froward thou wilt shew thyself unsavoury. shew thyself unsavoury: or, wrestle
10_22_27.ESV: with the purified you deal purely, and with the crooked you make yourself seem tortuous.
10_22_27.NASB: With the pure You show Yourself pure, And with the perverted You show Yourself astute.
10_22_27.NLT: To the pure you show yourself pure,but to the crooked you show yourself shrewd.
10_22_27.WEB: With the pure you will show yourself pure. With the crooked you will show yourself shrewd.
10_22_27.CSB17: with the pure you prove yourself pure; but with the crooked you prove yourself shrewd.
10_22_27.AMP: "With the pure You show Yourself pure, With the perverted You show Yourself astute.
10_22_27.MSG: You're good to good people,you shrewdly work around the bad ones.
10_22_27.NIV: to the pure you show yourself pure,but to the crooked you show yourself shrewd.
10_22_27.NRSVCE: with the pure you show yourself pure, and with the crooked you show yourself perverse.

10_22_28.NKJV: You will save the humble people; But Your eyes are on the haughty, that You may bring them down.
10_22_28.KJV: And the afflicted people thou wilt save: but thine eyes are upon the haughty, that thou mayest bring them down.
10_22_28.ESV: You save a humble people, but your eyes are on the haughty to bring them down.
10_22_28.NASB: 'And You save an afflicted people; But Your eyes are on the haughty [whom] You abase.
10_22_28.NLT: You rescue the humble,but your eyes watch the proud and humiliate them.
10_22_28.WEB: You will save the afflicted people, But your eyes are on the haughty, that you may bring them down.
10_22_28.CSB17: You rescue an oppressed people, but your eyes are set against the proud — you humble them.
10_22_28.AMP: "And You save the afflicted people; But Your eyes are on the haughty whom You abase (humiliate).
10_22_28.MSG: You take the side of the down-and-out,but the stuck-up you take down a peg.
10_22_28.NIV: You save the humble,but your eyes are on the haughty to bring them low.
10_22_28.NRSVCE: You deliver a humble people, but your eyes are upon the haughty to bring them down.

10_22_29.NKJV: “For You are my lamp, O LORD; The LORD shall enlighten my darkness.
10_22_29.KJV: For thou art my lamp, O LORD: and the LORD will lighten my darkness. lamp: or, candle
10_22_29.ESV: For you are my lamp, O LORD, and my God lightens my darkness.
10_22_29.NASB: 'For You are my lamp, O LORD; And the LORD illumines my darkness.
10_22_29.NLT: O LORD, you are my lamp.The LORD lights up my darkness.
10_22_29.WEB: For you are my lamp, Yahweh. Yahweh will light up my darkness.
10_22_29.CSB17: LORD, you are my lamp; the LORD illuminates my darkness.
10_22_29.AMP: "For You, O LORD, are my lamp; The LORD illumines and dispels my darkness.
10_22_29.MSG: Suddenly, God, your light floods my path,God drives out the darkness.
10_22_29.NIV: You are my lamp, O Lord;the Lord turns my darkness into light.
10_22_29.NRSVCE: Indeed, you are my lamp, O LORD, the LORD lightens my darkness.

10_22_30.NKJV: For by You I can run against a troop; By my God I can leap over a wall.
10_22_30.KJV: For by thee I have run through a troop: by my God have I leaped over a wall. run: or, broken
10_22_30.ESV: For by you I can run against a troop, and by my God I can leap over a wall.
10_22_30.NASB: 'For by You I can run upon a troop; By my God I can leap over a wall.
10_22_30.NLT: In your strength I can crush an army;with my God I can scale any wall.
10_22_30.WEB: For by you, I run against a troop. By my God, I leap over a wall.
10_22_30.CSB17: With you I can attack a barricade, and with my God I can leap over a wall.
10_22_30.AMP: "For by You I can run upon a troop; By my God I can leap over a wall.
10_22_30.MSG: I smash the bands of marauders,I vault the high fences.
10_22_30.NIV: With your help I can advance against a troop;with my God I can scale a wall.
10_22_30.NRSVCE: By you I can crush a troop, and by my God I can leap over a wall.

10_22_31.NKJV: As for God, His way is perfect; The word of the LORD is proven; He is a shield to all who trust in Him.
10_22_31.KJV: As for God, his way is perfect; the word of the LORD is tried: he is a buckler to all them that trust in him. tried: or, refined
10_22_31.ESV: This God — his way is perfect; the word of the LORD proves true; he is a shield for all those who take refuge in him.
10_22_31.NASB: 'As for God, His way is blameless; The word of the LORD is tested; He is a shield to all who take refuge in Him.
10_22_31.NLT: “God’s way is perfect.All the LORD’s promises prove true.He is a shield for all who look to him for protection.
10_22_31.WEB: As for God, his way is perfect. Yahweh’s word is tested. He is a shield to all those who take refuge in him.
10_22_31.CSB17: God — his way is perfect; the word of the LORD is pure. He is a shield to all who take refuge in him.
10_22_31.AMP: "As for God, His way is blameless and perfect; The word of the LORD is tested. He is a shield to all those who take refuge and trust in Him.
10_22_31.MSG: What a God! His roadstretches straight and smooth.Every God-direction is road-tested.Everyone who runs toward himMakes it.
10_22_31.NIV: “As for God, his way is perfect;the word of the Lord is flawless.He is a shieldfor all who take refuge in him.
10_22_31.NRSVCE: This God — his way is perfect; the promise of the LORD proves true; he is a shield for all who take refuge in him.

10_22_32.NKJV: “For who is God, except the LORD? And who is a rock, except our God?
10_22_32.KJV: For who is God, save the LORD? and who is a rock, save our God?
10_22_32.ESV: “For who is God, but the LORD? And who is a rock, except our God?
10_22_32.NASB: 'For who is God, besides the LORD? And who is a rock, besides our God?
10_22_32.NLT: For who is God except the LORD?Who but our God is a solid rock?
10_22_32.WEB: For who is God, besides Yahweh? Who is a rock, besides our God?
10_22_32.CSB17: For who is God besides the LORD? And who is a rock? Only our God.
10_22_32.AMP: "For who is God, besides the LORD? And who is a rock, besides our God?
10_22_32.MSG: Is there any god like God?Are we not at bedrock?
10_22_32.NIV: For who is God besides the Lord?And who is the Rock except our God?
10_22_32.NRSVCE: For who is God, but the LORD? And who is a rock, except our God?

10_22_33.NKJV: God is my strength and power, And He makes my way perfect.
10_22_33.KJV: God is my strength and power: and he maketh my way perfect. maketh: Heb. riddeth, or, looseth
10_22_33.ESV: This God is my strong refuge and has made my way blameless.
10_22_33.NASB: 'God is my strong fortress; And He sets the blameless in His way.
10_22_33.NLT: God is my strong fortress,and he makes my way perfect.
10_22_33.WEB: God is my strong fortress. He makes my way perfect.
10_22_33.CSB17: God is my strong refuge; he makes my way perfect.
10_22_33.AMP: "God is my strong fortress; He sets the blameless in His way.
10_22_33.MSG: Is not this the God who armed me well,then aimed me in the right direction?
10_22_33.NIV: It is God who arms me with strengthand makes my way perfect.
10_22_33.NRSVCE: The God who has girded me with strength has opened wide my path.

10_22_34.NKJV: He makes my feet like the feet of deer, And sets me on my high places.
10_22_34.KJV: He maketh my feet like hinds' feet: and setteth me upon my high places. maketh: Heb. equalleth
10_22_34.ESV: He made my feet like the feet of a deer and set me secure on the heights.
10_22_34.NASB: 'He makes my feet like hinds' [feet], And sets me on my high places.
10_22_34.NLT: He makes me as surefooted as a deer,enabling me to stand on mountain heights.
10_22_34.WEB: He makes his feet like hinds’ feet, and sets me on my high places.
10_22_34.CSB17: He makes my feet like the feet of a deer and sets me securely on the heights.
10_22_34.AMP: "He makes my feet like the doe's feet [firm and swift]; He sets me [secure and confident] on my high places.
10_22_34.MSG: Now I run like a deer;I'm king of the mountain.
10_22_34.NIV: He makes my feet like the feet of a deer;he enables me to stand on the heights.
10_22_34.NRSVCE: He made my feet like the feet of deer, and set me secure on the heights.

10_22_35.NKJV: He teaches my hands to make war, So that my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
10_22_35.KJV: He teacheth my hands to war; so that a bow of steel is broken by mine arms. to war: Heb. for the war
10_22_35.ESV: He trains my hands for war, so that my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
10_22_35.NASB: 'He trains my hands for battle, So that my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
10_22_35.NLT: He trains my hands for battle;he strengthens my arm to draw a bronze bow.
10_22_35.WEB: He teaches my hands to war, so that my arms bend a bow of brass.
10_22_35.CSB17: He trains my hands for war; my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
10_22_35.AMP: "He trains my hands for war, So that my arms can bend (pull back) a bow of bronze.
10_22_35.MSG: He shows me how to fight;I can bend a bronze bow!
10_22_35.NIV: He trains my hands for battle;my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
10_22_35.NRSVCE: He trains my hands for war, so that my arms can bend a bow of bronze.

10_22_36.NKJV: “You have also given me the shield of Your salvation; Your gentleness has made me great.

10_23_5.NASB: 'Truly is not my house so with God? For He has made an everlasting covenant with me, Ordered in all things, and secured; For all my salvation and all [my] desire, Will He not indeed make [it] grow?

10_23_5.WEB: Most certainly my house is not so with God, yet he has made with me an everlasting covenant, ordered in all things, and sure, for it is all my salvation, and all my desire, although he doesn’t make it grow.
10_23_5.CSB17: Is it not true my house is with God? For he has established a permanent covenant with me, ordered and secured in every detail. Will he not bring about my whole salvation and my every desire?
10_23_5.AMP: "Truly is not my house so [blessed] with God? For He has made an everlasting covenant with me, Ordered in all things, and secured. For will He not cause to grow and prosper All my salvation and my every wish? Will He not make it grow and prosper?
10_23_5.MSG: And this is just how my regime has been,for God guaranteed his covenant with me,Spelled it out plainlyand kept every promised word—My entire salvation,my every desire.
10_23_5.NIV: “Is not my house right with God?Has he not made with me an everlasting covenant,arranged and secured in every part?Will he not bring to fruition my salvationand grant me my every desire?
10_23_5.NRSVCE: Is not my house like this with God? For he has made with me an everlasting covenant, ordered in all things and secure. Will he not cause to prosper all my help and my desire?

10_23_6.NKJV: But the sons of rebellion shall all be as thorns thrust away, Because they cannot be taken with hands.
10_23_6.KJV: But the sons of Belial shall be all of them as thorns thrust away, because they cannot be taken with hands:

10_23_6.NASB: 'But the worthless, every one of them will be thrust away like thorns, Because they cannot be taken in hand;
10_23_6.NLT: But the godless are like thorns to be thrown away,for they tear the hand that touches them.

10_23_6.AMP: "But the wicked and worthless are all to be thrown away like thorns, Because they cannot be taken with the hand;
10_23_6.MSG: But the devil's henchmen are like thornsculled and piled as trash;
10_23_6.NIV: But evil men are all to be cast aside like thorns,which are not gathered with the hand.
10_23_6.NRSVCE: But the godless are all like thorns that are thrown away; for they cannot be picked up with the hand;

10_23_7.NKJV: But the man who touches them Must be armed with iron and the shaft of a spear, And they shall be utterly burned with fire in their place.”
10_23_7.KJV: But the man that shall touch them must be fenced with iron and the staff of a spear; and they shall be utterly burned with fire in the same place. fenced: Heb. filled
10_23_7.ESV: but the man who touches them arms himself with iron and the shaft of a spear, and they are utterly consumed with fire.”
10_23_7.NASB: But the man who touches them Must be armed with iron and the shaft of a spear, And they will be completely burned with fire in [their] place.'
10_23_7.NLT: One must use iron tools to chop them down;they will be totally consumed by fire.”
10_23_7.WEB: But the man who touches them must be armed with iron and the staff of a spear. They will be utterly burned with fire in their place.”
10_23_7.CSB17: The man who touches them must be armed with iron and the shaft of a spear. They will be completely burned up on the spot.

10_23_7.MSG: Better not try to touch them;keep your distance with a rake or hoe.They'll make a glorious bonfire!

10_23_7.NRSVCE: to touch them one uses an iron bar or the shaft of a spear. And they are entirely consumed in fire on the spot.

10_23_8.NKJV: These are the names of the mighty men whom David had: Josheb-Basshebeth the Tachmonite, chief among the captains. He was called Adino the Eznite, because he had killed eight hundred men at one time.
10_23_8.KJV: These be the names of the mighty men whom David had: The Tachmonite that sat in the seat , chief among the captains; the same was Adino the Eznite: he lift up his spear against eight hundred, whom he slew at one time. The Tachmonite: or, Joshebbassebet the Tachmonite, head of the three. whom he: Heb. slain. he lift: See 1.Ch.11.11. whom he: Heb. slain
10_23_8.ESV: These are the names of the mighty men whom David had: Josheb-basshebeth a Tahchemonite; he was chief of the three. He wielded his spear against eight hundred whom he killed at one time.
10_23_8.NASB: These are the names of the mighty men whom David had: Josheb-basshebeth a Tahchemonite, chief of the captains, he was [called] Adino the Eznite, because of eight hundred slain [by him] at one time;
10_23_8.NLT: These are the names of David’s mightiest warriors. The first was Jashobeam the Hacmonite, who was leader of the Three — the three mightiest warriors among David’s men. He once used his spear to kill 800 enemy warriors in a single battle.
10_23_8.WEB: These are the names of the mighty men whom David had: Josheb Basshebeth a Tahchemonite, chief of the captains; the same was Adino the Eznite, against eight hundred slain at one time.
10_23_8.CSB17: These are the names of David’s warriors: Josheb-basshebeth the Tahchemonite was chief of the officers. He wielded his spear against eight hundred men that he killed at one time.
10_23_8.AMP: These are the names of the mighty men (warriors) whom David had: Josheb-basshebeth, a Tahchemonite, chief of the captains, also called Adino the Eznite (spear) because of the eight hundred men killed [by him] at one time.
10_23_8.MSG: This is the listing of David's top men.Josheb-Basshebeth, the Tahkemonite. He was chief of the Three. He once put his spear to work against eight hundred—killed them all in a day.
10_23_8.NIV: David’s Mighty MenThese are the names of David’s mighty men:Josheb-Basshebeth, a Tahkemonite, was chief of the Three; he raised his spear against eight hundred men, whom he killed in one encounter.
10_23_8.NRSVCE: These are the names of the warriors whom David had: Josheb-basshebeth a Tahchemonite; he was chief of the Three; he wielded his spear against eight hundred whom he killed at one time.

10_23_9.NKJV: And after him was Eleazar the son of Dodo, the Ahohite, one of the three mighty men with David when they defied the Philistines who were gathered there for battle, and the men of Israel had retreated.

10_23_9.ESV: And next to him among the three mighty men was Eleazar the son of Dodo, son of Ahohi. He was with David when they defied the Philistines who were gathered there for battle, and the men of Israel withdrew.
10_23_9.NASB: and after him was Eleazar the son of Dodo the Ahohite, one of the three mighty men with David when they defied the Philistines who were gathered there to battle and the men of Israel had withdrawn.
10_23_9.NLT: Next in rank among the Three was Eleazar son of Dodai, a descendant of Ahoah. Once Eleazar and David stood together against the Philistines when the entire Israelite army had fled.
10_23_9.WEB: After him was Eleazar the son of Dodai the son of an Ahohite, one of the three mighty men with David, when they defied the Philistines who were there gathered together to battle, and the men of Israel had gone away.
10_23_9.CSB17: After him, Eleazar son of Dodo son of an Ahohite was among the three warriors with David when they defied the Philistines. The men of Israel retreated in the place they had gathered for battle,

10_23_9.MSG: Eleazar son of Dodai the Ahohite was the next of the elite Three. He was with David when the Philistines poked fun at them at Pas Dammim. When the Philistines drew up for battle, Israel retreated.
10_23_9.NIV: Next to him was Eleazar son of Dodai the Ahohite. As one of the three mighty men, he was with David when they taunted the Philistines gathered at Pas Dammim for battle. Then the men of Israel retreated,

10_23_10.NKJV: He arose and attacked the Philistines until his hand was weary, and his hand stuck to the sword. The LORD brought about a great victory that day; and the people returned after him only to plunder.
10_23_10.KJV: He arose, and smote the Philistines until his hand was weary, and his hand clave unto the sword: and the LORD wrought a great victory that day; and the people returned after him only to spoil.
10_23_10.ESV: He rose and struck down the Philistines until his hand was weary, and his hand clung to the sword. And the LORD brought about a great victory that day, and the men returned after him only to strip the slain.
10_23_10.NASB: He arose and struck the Philistines until his hand was weary and clung to the sword, and the LORD brought about a great victory that day; and the people returned after him only to strip [the slain].
10_23_10.NLT: He killed Philistines until his hand was too tired to lift his sword, and the LORD gave him a great victory that day. The rest of the army did not return until it was time to collect the plunder!
10_23_10.WEB: He arose and struck the Philistines until his hand was weary, and his hand froze to the sword; and Yahweh worked a great victory that day; and the people returned after him only to take plunder.
10_23_10.CSB17: but Eleazar stood his ground and attacked the Philistines until his hand was tired and stuck to his sword. The LORD brought about a great victory that day. Then the troops came back to him, but only to plunder the dead.
10_23_10.AMP: Eleazar stood up and struck down the Philistines until his hand was weary and clung to the sword. The LORD brought about a great victory that day; the people returned after him only to take the spoil [of the slain].
10_23_10.MSG: But Eleazar stood his ground and killed Philistines right and left until he was exhausted—but he never let go of his sword! A big win for God that day. The army then rejoined Eleazar, but all there was left to do was the clean-up.
10_23_10.NIV: but he stood his ground and struck down the Philistines till his hand grew tired and froze to the sword. The Lord brought about a great victory that day. The troops returned to Eleazar, but only to strip the dead.
10_23_10.NRSVCE: but he stood his ground. He struck down the Philistines until his arm grew weary, though his hand clung to the sword. The LORD brought about a great victory that day. Then the people came back to him — but only to strip the dead.

10_23_12.KJV: But he stood in the midst of the ground, and defended it, and slew the Philistines: and the LORD wrought a great victory.

10_23_13.NKJV: Then three of the thirty chief men went down at harvest time and came to David at the cave of Adullam. And the troop of Philistines encamped in the Valley of Rephaim.
10_23_13.KJV: And three of the thirty chief went down, and came to David in the harvest time unto the cave of Adullam: and the troop of the Philistines pitched in the valley of Rephaim. three: or, the three captains over the thirty
10_23_13.ESV: And three of the thirty chief men went down and came about harvest time to David at the cave of Adullam, when a band of Philistines was encamped in the Valley of Rephaim.
10_23_13.NASB: Then three of the thirty chief men went down and came to David in the harvest time to the cave of Adullam, while the troop of the Philistines was camping in the valley of Rephaim.
10_23_13.NLT: Once during the harvest, when David was at the cave of Adullam, the Philistine army was camped in the valley of Rephaim. The Three (who were among the Thirty — an elite group among David’s fighting men) went down to meet him there.
10_23_13.WEB: Three of the thirty chief men went down, and came to David in the harvest time to the cave of Adullam; and the troop of the Philistines was encamped in the valley of Rephaim.
10_23_13.CSB17: Three of the thirty leading warriors went down at harvest time and came to David at the cave of Adullam, while a company of Philistines was camping in the Valley of Rephaim.

10_23_15.NKJV: And David said with longing, “Oh, that someone would give me a drink of the water from the well of Bethlehem, which is by the gate!”
10_23_15.KJV: And David longed, and said, Oh that one would give me drink of the water of the well of Bethlehem, which is by the gate!
10_23_15.ESV: And David said longingly, “Oh, that someone would give me water to drink from the well of Bethlehem that is by the gate!”
10_23_15.NASB: David had a craving and said, 'Oh that someone would give me water to drink from the well of Bethlehem which is by the gate!'
10_23_15.NLT: David remarked longingly to his men, “Oh, how I would love some of that good water from the well by the gate in Bethlehem.”
10_23_15.WEB: David longed, and said, “Oh that someone would give me water to drink from the well of Bethlehem, which is by the gate!”
10_23_15.CSB17: David was extremely thirsty and said, “If only someone would bring me water to drink from the well at the city gate of Bethlehem!”
10_23_15.AMP: And David had a craving and said, "Oh that someone would give me water to drink from the well of Bethlehem, which is by the gate!"
10_23_15.MSG: David had a sudden craving and said, "Would I ever like a drink of water from the well at the gate of Bethlehem!"
10_23_15.NIV: David longed for water and said, “Oh, that someone would get me a drink of water from the well near the gate of Bethlehem!”
10_23_15.NRSVCE: David said longingly, “O that someone would give me water to drink from the well of Bethlehem that is by the gate!”

10_23_16.NKJV: So the three mighty men broke through the camp of the Philistines, drew water from the well of Bethlehem that was by the gate, and took it and brought it to David. Nevertheless he would not drink it, but poured it out to the LORD.
10_23_16.KJV: And the three mighty men brake through the host of the Philistines, and drew water out of the well of Bethlehem, that was by the gate, and took it, and brought it to David: nevertheless he would not drink thereof, but poured it out unto the LORD.
10_23_16.ESV: Then the three mighty men broke through the camp of the Philistines and drew water out of the well of Bethlehem that was by the gate and carried and brought it to David. But he would not drink of it. He poured it out to the LORD
10_23_16.NASB: So the three mighty men broke through the camp of the Philistines, and drew water from the well of Bethlehem which was by the gate, and took [it] and brought [it] to David. Nevertheless he would not drink it, but poured it out to the LORD;
10_23_16.NLT: So the Three broke through the Philistine lines, drew some water from the well by the gate in Bethlehem, and brought it back to David. But he refused to drink it. Instead, he poured it out as an offering to the LORD.
10_23_16.WEB: The three mighty men broke through the army of the Philistines, and drew water out of the well of Bethlehem that was by the gate, and took it and brought it to David; but he would not drink of it, but poured it out to Yahweh.
10_23_16.CSB17: So three of the warriors broke through the Philistine camp and drew water from the well at the gate of Bethlehem. They brought it back to David, but he refused to drink it. Instead, he poured it out to the LORD.

10_23_16.MSG: So the Three penetrated the Philistine lines, drew water from the well at the gate of Bethlehem, and brought it back to David. But David wouldn't drink it; he poured it out as an offering to God,

10_23_17.NKJV: And he said, “Far be it from me, O LORD, that I should do this! Is this not the blood of the men who went in jeopardy of their lives?” Therefore he would not drink it. These things were done by the three mighty men.
10_23_17.KJV: And he said, Be it far from me, O LORD, that I should do this: is not this the blood of the men that went in jeopardy of their lives? therefore he would not drink it. These things did these three mighty men.
10_23_17.ESV: and said, “Far be it from me, O LORD, that I should do this. Shall I drink the blood of the men who went at the risk of their lives?” Therefore he would not drink it. These things the three mighty men did.
10_23_17.NASB: and he said, 'Be it far from me, O LORD, that I should do this. [Shall I drink] the blood of the men who went in [jeopardy] of their lives?' Therefore he would not drink it. These things the three mighty men did.
10_23_17.NLT: “The LORD forbid that I should drink this!” he exclaimed. “This water is as precious as the blood of these men who risked their lives to bring it to me.” So David did not drink it. These are examples of the exploits of the Three.
10_23_17.WEB: He said, “Be it far from me, Yahweh, that I should do this! Isn’t this the blood of the men who risked their lives to go?” Therefore he would not drink it. The three mighty men did these things.
10_23_17.CSB17: David said, “LORD, I would never do such a thing! Is this not the blood of men who risked their lives?” So he refused to drink it. Such were the exploits of the three warriors.
10_23_17.AMP: And he said, "Far be it from me, O LORD, that I should drink this. [Is it not the same as] the blood of the men who went at the risk of their lives?" So he would not drink it. These things the three mighty men did.
10_23_17.MSG: saying, "There is no way, God, that I'll drink this! This isn't mere water, it's their life-blood—they risked their very lives to bring it!" So David refused to drink it.This is the sort of thing that the Three did.
10_23_17.NIV: “Far be it from me, O Lord, to do this!” he said. “Is it not the blood of men who went at the risk of their lives?” And David would not drink it.Such were the exploits of the three mighty men.

10_23_18.NKJV: Now Abishai the brother of Joab, the son of Zeruiah, was chief of another three. He lifted his spear against three hundred men, killed them, and won a name among these three.
10_23_18.KJV: And Abishai, the brother of Joab, the son of Zeruiah, was chief among three. And he lifted up his spear against three hundred, and slew them, and had the name among three. and slew them: Heb. slain
10_23_18.ESV: Now Abishai, the brother of Joab, the son of Zeruiah, was chief of the thirty. And he wielded his spear against three hundred men and killed them and won a name beside the three.
10_23_18.NASB: Abishai, the brother of Joab, the son of Zeruiah, was chief of the thirty. And he swung his spear against three hundred and killed [them], and had a name as well as the three.
10_23_18.NLT: Abishai son of Zeruiah, the brother of Joab, was the leader of the Thirty. He once used his spear to kill 300 enemy warriors in a single battle. It was by such feats that he became as famous as the Three.
10_23_18.WEB: Abishai, the brother of Joab, the son of Zeruiah, was chief of the three. He lifted up his spear against three hundred and killed them, and had a name among the three.
10_23_18.CSB17: Abishai, Joab’s brother and son of Zeruiah, was leader of the Three. He wielded his spear against three hundred men and killed them, gaining a reputation among the Three.
10_23_18.AMP: Now Abishai the brother of Joab the son of Zeruiah was chief of the thirty. He wielded his spear against three hundred men and killed them, and gained a reputation beside the three.
10_23_18.MSG: Abishai brother of Joab and son of Zeruiah was the head of the Thirty. He once got credit for killing three hundred with his spear, but he was never named in the same breath as the Three.
10_23_18.NIV: Abishai the brother of Joab son of Zeruiah was chief of the Three. He raised his spear against three hundred men, whom he killed, and so he became as famous as the Three.
10_23_18.NRSVCE: Now Abishai son of Zeruiah, the brother of Joab, was chief of the Thirty. With his spear he fought against three hundred men and killed them, and won a name beside the Three.

10_23_19.NKJV: Was he not the most honored of three? Therefore he became their captain. However, he did not attain to the first three.

10_23_19.ESV: He was the most renowned of the thirty and became their commander, but he did not attain to the three.
10_23_19.NASB: He was most honored of the thirty, therefore he became their commander; however, he did not attain to the three.

10_23_19.WEB: Wasn’t he most honorable of the three? therefore he was made their captain. However he wasn’t included as one of the three.
10_23_19.CSB17: Was he not more honored than the Three? He became their commander even though he did not become one of the Three.
10_23_19.AMP: He was the most honored of the thirty, so he became their commander; however, he did not attain to the [greatness of the] three.
10_23_19.MSG: He was the most respected of the Thirty and was their captain, but never got included among the Three.
10_23_19.NIV: Was he not held in greater honor than the Three? He became their commander, even though he was not included among them.
10_23_19.NRSVCE: He was the most renowned of the Thirty, and became their commander; but he did not attain to the Three.

10_23_20.NKJV: Benaiah was the son of Jehoiada, the son of a valiant man from Kabzeel, who had done many deeds. He had killed two lion-like heroes of Moab. He also had gone down and killed a lion in the midst of a pit on a snowy day.
10_23_20.KJV: And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, the son of a valiant man , of Kabzeel, who had done many acts, he slew two lionlike men of Moab: he went down also and slew a lion in the midst of a pit in time of snow: who: Heb. great of acts lionlike: Heb. lion of God
10_23_20.ESV: And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was a valiant man of Kabzeel, a doer of great deeds. He struck down two ariels of Moab. He also went down and struck down a lion in a pit on a day when snow had fallen.
10_23_20.NASB: Then Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, the son of a valiant man of Kabzeel, who had done mighty deeds, killed the two [sons of] Ariel of Moab. He also went down and killed a lion in the middle of a pit on a snowy day.
10_23_20.NLT: There was also Benaiah son of Jehoiada, a valiant warrior from Kabzeel. He did many heroic deeds, which included killing two champions of Moab. Another time, on a snowy day, he chased a lion down into a pit and killed it.
10_23_20.WEB: Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, the son of a valiant man of Kabzeel, who had done mighty deeds, killed the two sons of Ariel of Moab. He also went down and killed a lion in the middle of a pit in a time of snow.
10_23_20.CSB17: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was the son of a brave man from Kabzeel, a man of many exploits. Benaiah killed two sons of Ariel of Moab, and he went down into a pit on a snowy day and killed a lion.
10_23_20.AMP: Then Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, the son of a valiant man of Kabzeel, who had done many notable acts, killed two [famous] warriors of Moab. He also went down and killed a lion in the middle of a pit on a snowy day.
10_23_20.MSG: Benaiah son of Jehoiada from Kabzeel was a vigorous man who accomplished a great deal. He once killed two lion cubs in Moab. Another time, on a snowy day, he climbed down into a pit and killed a lion.
10_23_20.NIV: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was a valiant fighter from Kabzeel, who performed great exploits. He struck down two of Moab’s best men. He also went down into a pit on a snowy day and killed a lion.
10_23_20.NRSVCE: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was a valiant warrior from Kabzeel, a doer of great deeds; he struck down two sons of Ariel of Moab. He also went down and killed a lion in a pit on a day when snow had fallen.

10_23_21.NKJV: And he killed an Egyptian, a spectacular man. The Egyptian had a spear in his hand; so he went down to him with a staff, wrested the spear out of the Egyptian’s hand, and killed him with his own spear.
10_23_21.KJV: And he slew an Egyptian, a goodly man: and the Egyptian had a spear in his hand; but he went down to him with a staff, and plucked the spear out of the Egyptian's hand, and slew him with his own spear. a goodly: Heb. a man of countenance, or, sight: called I.Chr.I.23. a man of great stature
10_23_21.ESV: And he struck down an Egyptian, a handsome man. The Egyptian had a spear in his hand, but Benaiah went down to him with a staff and snatched the spear out of the Egyptian's hand and killed him with his own spear.
10_23_21.NASB: He killed an Egyptian, an impressive man. Now the Egyptian [had] a spear in his hand, but he went down to him with a club and snatched the spear from the Egyptian's hand and killed him with his own spear.
10_23_21.NLT: Once, armed only with a club, he killed an imposing Egyptian warrior who was armed with a spear. Benaiah wrenched the spear from the Egyptian’s hand and killed him with it.
10_23_21.WEB: He killed a huge Egyptian, and the Egyptian had a spear in his hand; but he went down to him with a staff, and plucked the spear out of the Egyptian’s hand, and killed him with his own spear.
10_23_21.CSB17: He also killed an Egyptian, an impressive man. Even though the Egyptian had a spear in his hand, Benaiah went down to him with a club, snatched the spear out of the Egyptian’s hand, and then killed him with his own spear.
10_23_21.AMP: And he killed an Egyptian, an impressive and handsome man. The Egyptian had a spear in his hand, but Benaiah went down to him with a club, snatched the spear out of the Egyptian's hand and killed the man with his own spear.
10_23_21.MSG: Another time he killed a formidable Egyptian. The Egyptian was armed with a spear and Benaiah went against him with nothing but a walking stick; he seized the spear from his grip and killed him with his own spear.
10_23_21.NIV: And he struck down a huge Egyptian. Although the Egyptian had a spear in his hand, Benaiah went against him with a club. He snatched the spear from the Egyptian’s hand and killed him with his own spear.
10_23_21.NRSVCE: And he killed an Egyptian, a handsome man. The Egyptian had a spear in his hand; but Benaiah went against him with a staff, snatched the spear out of the Egyptian’s hand, and killed him with his own spear.

10_23_22.ESV: These things did Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and won a name beside the three mighty men.

10_23_22.NLT: Deeds like these made Benaiah as famous as the Three mightiest warriors.

10_23_22.CSB17: These were the exploits of Benaiah son of Jehoiada, who had a reputation among the three warriors.

10_23_22.NRSVCE: Such were the things Benaiah son of Jehoiada did, and won a name beside the three warriors.

10_24_1.NKJV: Again the anger of the LORD was aroused against Israel, and He moved David against them to say, “Go, number Israel and Judah.”
10_24_1.KJV: And again the anger of the LORD was kindled against Israel, and he moved David against them to say, Go, number Israel and Judah.
10_24_1.ESV: Again the anger of the LORD was kindled against Israel, and he incited David against them, saying, “Go, number Israel and Judah.”
10_24_1.NASB: Now again the anger of the LORD burned against Israel, and it incited David against them to say, 'Go, number Israel and Judah.'
10_24_1.NLT: Once again the anger of the LORD burned against Israel, and he caused David to harm them by taking a census. “Go and count the people of Israel and Judah,” the LORD told him.
10_24_1.WEB: Again Yahweh’s anger burned against Israel, and he moved David against them, saying, “Go, count Israel and Judah.”

10_24_1.AMP: Now again the anger of the LORD burned against Israel, and He incited David against them to say, "Go, count [the people of] Israel and Judah."
10_24_1.MSG: Once again God's anger blazed out against Israel. He tested David by telling him, "Go and take a census of Israel and Judah."
10_24_1.NIV: David Counts the Fighting MenAgain the anger of the Lord burned against Israel, and he incited David against them, saying, “Go and take a census of Israel and Judah.”
10_24_1.NRSVCE: Again the anger of the LORD was kindled against Israel, and he incited David against them, saying, “Go, count the people of Israel and Judah.”

10_24_2.NKJV: So the king said to Joab the commander of the army who was with him, “Now go throughout all the tribes of Israel, from Dan to Beersheba, and count the people, that I may know the number of the people.”
10_24_2.KJV: For the king said to Joab the captain of the host, which was with him, Go now through all the tribes of Israel, from Dan even to Beersheba, and number ye the people, that I may know the number of the people. Go: or, Compass
10_24_2.ESV: So the king said to Joab, the commander of the army, who was with him, “Go through all the tribes of Israel, from Dan to Beersheba, and number the people, that I may know the number of the people.”
10_24_2.NASB: The king said to Joab the commander of the army who was with him, 'Go about now through all the tribes of Israel, from Dan to Beersheba, and register the people, that I may know the number of the people.'
10_24_2.NLT: So the king said to Joab and the commanders of the army, “Take a census of all the tribes of Israel — from Dan in the north to Beersheba in the south — so I may know how many people there are.”
10_24_2.WEB: The king said to Joab the captain of the army, who was with him, “Now go back and forth through all the tribes of Israel, from Dan even to Beersheba, and count the people, that I may know the sum of the people.”
10_24_2.CSB17: So the king said to Joab, the commander of his army, “Go through all the tribes of Israel from Dan to Beer-sheba and register the troops so I can know their number.”
10_24_2.AMP: So the king said to Joab the commander of the army who was with him, "Go now through all the tribes of Israel, from Dan [in the north] to Beersheba [in the south], and conduct a census of the people, so that I may know the number of the people."
10_24_2.MSG: So David gave orders to Joab and the army officers under him, "Canvass all the tribes of Israel, from Dan to Beersheba, and get a count of the population. I want to know the number."
10_24_2.NIV: So the king said to Joab and the army commanders with him, “Go throughout the tribes of Israel from Dan to Beersheba and enroll the fighting men, so that I may know how many there are.”
10_24_2.NRSVCE: So the king said to Joab and the commanders of the army, who were with him, “Go through all the tribes of Israel, from Dan to Beer-sheba, and take a census of the people, so that I may know how many there are.”

10_24_3.NKJV: And Joab said to the king, “Now may the LORD your God add to the people a hundred times more than there are, and may the eyes of my lord the king see it. But why does my lord the king desire this thing?”
10_24_3.KJV: And Joab said unto the king, Now the LORD thy God add unto the people, how many soever they be, an hundredfold , and that the eyes of my lord the king may see it: but why doth my lord the king delight in this thing?
10_24_3.ESV: But Joab said to the king, “May the LORD your God add to the people a hundred times as many as they are, while the eyes of my lord the king still see it, but why does my lord the king delight in this thing?”
10_24_3.NASB: But Joab said to the king, 'Now may the LORD your God add to the people a hundred times as many as they are, while the eyes of my lord the king [still] see; but why does my lord the king delight in this thing?'
10_24_3.NLT: But Joab replied to the king, “May the LORD your God let you live to see a hundred times as many people as there are now! But why, my lord the king, do you want to do this?”
10_24_3.WEB: Joab said to the king, “Now may Yahweh your God add to the people, however many they may be, one hundred times; and may the eyes of my lord the king see it. But why does my lord the king delight in this thing?”
10_24_3.CSB17: Joab replied to the king, “May the LORD your God multiply the troops a hundred times more than they are — while my lord the king looks on! But why does my lord the king want to do this?”
10_24_3.AMP: But Joab said to the king, "May the LORD your God add to the people a hundred times as many as there are, and let the eyes of my lord the king see it; but why does my lord the king want to do this thing?"
10_24_3.MSG: But Joab resisted the king: "May your God multiply people by the hundreds right before the eyes of my master the king, but why on earth would you do a thing like this?"
10_24_3.NIV: But Joab replied to the king, “May the Lord your God multiply the troops a hundred times over, and may the eyes of my lord the king see it. But why does my lord the king want to do such a thing?”
10_24_3.NRSVCE: But Joab said to the king, “May the LORD your God increase the number of the people a hundredfold, while the eyes of my lord the king can still see it! But why does my lord the king want to do this?”

10_24_4.NKJV: Nevertheless the king’s word prevailed against Joab and against the captains of the army. Therefore Joab and the captains of the army went out from the presence of the king to count the people of Israel.
10_24_4.KJV: Notwithstanding the king's word prevailed against Joab, and against the captains of the host. And Joab and the captains of the host went out from the presence of the king, to number the people of Israel.
10_24_4.ESV: But the king's word prevailed against Joab and the commanders of the army. So Joab and the commanders of the army went out from the presence of the king to number the people of Israel.
10_24_4.NASB: Nevertheless, the king's word prevailed against Joab and against the commanders of the army. So Joab and the commanders of the army went out from the presence of the king to register the people of Israel.
10_24_4.NLT: But the king insisted that they take the census, so Joab and the commanders of the army went out to count the people of Israel.
10_24_4.WEB: Notwithstanding, the king’s word prevailed against Joab, and against the captains of the army. Joab and the captains of the army went out from the presence of the king to count the people of Israel.
10_24_4.CSB17: Yet the king’s order prevailed over Joab and the commanders of the army. So Joab and the commanders of the army left the king’s presence to register the troops of Israel.
10_24_4.AMP: Nevertheless, the king's word prevailed against Joab and the commanders of the army. So they went from the king's presence to take a census of the people of Israel.
10_24_4.MSG: Nevertheless, the king insisted, and so Joab and the army officers left the king to take a census of Israel.
10_24_4.NIV: The king’s word, however, overruled Joab and the army commanders; so they left the presence of the king to enroll the fighting men of Israel.
10_24_4.NRSVCE: But the king’s word prevailed against Joab and the commanders of the army. So Joab and the commanders of the army went out from the presence of the king to take a census of the people of Israel.

10_24_5.NKJV: And they crossed over the Jordan and camped in Aroer, on the right side of the town which is in the midst of the ravine of Gad, and toward Jazer.

10_24_5.ESV: They crossed the Jordan and began from Aroer, and from the city that is in the middle of the valley, toward Gad and on to Jazer.
10_24_5.NASB: They crossed the Jordan and camped in Aroer, on the right side of the city that is in the middle of the valley of Gad and toward Jazer.
10_24_5.NLT: First they crossed the Jordan and camped at Aroer, south of the town in the valley, in the direction of Gad. Then they went on to Jazer,
10_24_5.WEB: They passed over the Jordan, and encamped in Aroer, on the right side of the city that is in the middle of the valley of Gad, and to Jazer;
10_24_5.CSB17: They crossed the Jordan and camped in Aroer, south of the town in the middle of the valley, and then proceeded toward Gad and Jazer.
10_24_5.AMP: They crossed over the Jordan and camped in Aroer, on the south side of the city which is in the middle of the river valley [of the Arnon] toward Gad, and on toward Jazer.
10_24_5.MSG: They crossed the Jordan and began with Aroer and the town in the canyon of the Gadites near Jazer,
10_24_5.NIV: After crossing the Jordan, they camped near Aroer, south of the town in the gorge, and then went through Gad and on to Jazer.
10_24_5.NRSVCE: They crossed the Jordan, and began from Aroer and from the city that is in the middle of the valley, toward Gad and on to Jazer.

10_24_6.ESV: Then they came to Gilead, and to Kadesh in the land of the Hittites; and they came to Dan, and from Dan they went around to Sidon,
10_24_6.NASB: Then they came to Gilead and to the land of Tahtim-hodshi, and they came to Dan-jaan and around to Sidon,
10_24_6.NLT: then to Gilead in the land of Tahtim-hodshi and to Dan-jaan and around to Sidon.
10_24_6.WEB: then they came to Gilead, and to the land of Tahtim Hodshi; and they came to Dan Jaan, and around to Sidon,
10_24_6.CSB17: They went to Gilead and to the land of the Hittites and continued on to Dan-jaan and around to Sidon.
10_24_6.AMP: Then they came to Gilead and to the land of Tahtim-hodshi, and they came to Dan-jaan and around to Sidon,
10_24_6.MSG: proceeded through Gilead, passed Hermon, then on to Dan, but detoured Sidon.

10_24_6.NRSVCE: Then they came to Gilead, and to Kadesh in the land of the Hittites; and they came to Dan, and from Dan they went around to Sidon,

10_24_7.NKJV: and they came to the stronghold of Tyre and to all the cities of the Hivites and the Canaanites. Then they went out to South Judah as far as Beersheba.
10_24_7.KJV: And came to the strong hold of Tyre, and to all the cities of the Hivites, and of the Canaanites: and they went out to the south of Judah, even to Beersheba.
10_24_7.ESV: and came to the fortress of Tyre and to all the cities of the Hivites and Canaanites; and they went out to the Negeb of Judah at Beersheba.
10_24_7.NASB: and came to the fortress of Tyre and to all the cities of the Hivites and of the Canaanites, and they went out to the south of Judah, [to] Beersheba.
10_24_7.NLT: Then they came to the fortress of Tyre, and all the towns of the Hivites and Canaanites. Finally, they went south to Judah as far as Beersheba.
10_24_7.WEB: and came to the stronghold of Tyre, and to all the cities of the Hivites, and of the Canaanites; and they went out to the south of Judah, at Beersheba.
10_24_7.CSB17: They went to the fortress of Tyre and all the cities of the Hivites and Canaanites. Afterward, they went to the Negev of Judah at Beer-sheba.
10_24_7.AMP: and they came to the stronghold of Tyre and to all the cities of the Hivites and Canaanites, and they went out to the south of Judah, to Beersheba.
10_24_7.MSG: They covered Fort Tyre and all the Hivite and Canaanite cities, and finally reached the Negev of Judah at Beersheba.
10_24_7.NIV: Then they went toward the fortress of Tyre and all the towns of the Hivites and Canaanites. Finally, they went on to Beersheba in the Negev of Judah.
10_24_7.NRSVCE: and came to the fortress of Tyre and to all the cities of the Hivites and Canaanites; and they went out to the Negeb of Judah at Beer-sheba.

10_24_8.NKJV: So when they had gone through all the land, they came to Jerusalem at the end of nine months and twenty days.
10_24_8.KJV: So when they had gone through all the land, they came to Jerusalem at the end of nine months and twenty days.
10_24_8.ESV: So when they had gone through all the land, they came to Jerusalem at the end of nine months and twenty days.
10_24_8.NASB: So when they had gone about through the whole land, they came to Jerusalem at the end of nine months and twenty days.
10_24_8.NLT: Having gone through the entire land for nine months and twenty days, they returned to Jerusalem.
10_24_8.WEB: So when they had gone back and forth through all the land, they came to Jerusalem at the end of nine months and twenty days.
10_24_8.CSB17: When they had gone through the whole land, they returned to Jerusalem at the end of nine months and twenty days.
10_24_8.AMP: So when they had gone about through all the land [taking the census], they came to Jerusalem at the end of nine months and twenty days.
10_24_8.MSG: They canvassed the whole country and after nine months and twenty days arrived back in Jerusalem.
10_24_8.NIV: After they had gone through the entire land, they came back to Jerusalem at the end of nine months and twenty days.
10_24_8.NRSVCE: So when they had gone through all the land, they came back to Jerusalem at the end of nine months and twenty days.

10_24_9.NKJV: Then Joab gave the sum of the number of the people to the king. And there were in Israel eight hundred thousand valiant men who drew the sword, and the men of Judah were five hundred thousand men.
10_24_9.KJV: And Joab gave up the sum of the number of the people unto the king: and there were in Israel eight hundred thousand valiant men that drew the sword; and the men of Judah were five hundred thousand men.
10_24_9.ESV: And Joab gave the sum of the numbering of the people to the king: in Israel there were 800,000 valiant men who drew the sword, and the men of Judah were 500,000.
10_24_9.NASB: And Joab gave the number of the registration of the people to the king; and there were in Israel eight hundred thousand valiant men who drew the sword, and the men of Judah were five hundred thousand men.
10_24_9.NLT: Joab reported the number of people to the king. There were 800,000 capable warriors in Israel who could handle a sword, and 500,000 in Judah.
10_24_9.WEB: Joab gave up the sum of the counting of the people to the king: and there were in Israel eight hundred thousand valiant men who drew the sword; and the men of Judah were five hundred thousand men.
10_24_9.CSB17: Joab gave the king the total of the registration of the troops. There were eight hundred thousand valiant armed men from Israel and five hundred thousand men from Judah.
10_24_9.AMP: And Joab gave the sum of the census of the people to the king. In Israel there were 800,000 valiant men who drew the sword, and the men of Judah were 500,000.
10_24_9.MSG: Joab gave the results of the census to the king: 800,000 able-bodied fighting men in Israel; in Judah 500,000.
10_24_9.NIV: Joab reported the number of the fighting men to the king: In Israel there were eight hundred thousand able-bodied men who could handle a sword, and in Judah five hundred thousand.
10_24_9.NRSVCE: Joab reported to the king the number of those who had been recorded: in Israel there were eight hundred thousand soldiers able to draw the sword, and those of Judah were five hundred thousand.

10_24_10.NKJV: And David’s heart condemned him after he had numbered the people. So David said to the LORD, “I have sinned greatly in what I have done; but now, I pray, O LORD, take away the iniquity of Your servant, for I have done very foolishly.”
10_24_10.KJV: And David's heart smote him after that he had numbered the people. And David said unto the LORD, I have sinned greatly in that I have done: and now, I beseech thee, O LORD, take away the iniquity of thy servant; for I have done very foolishly.
10_24_10.ESV: But David's heart struck him after he had numbered the people. And David said to the LORD, “I have sinned greatly in what I have done. But now, O LORD, please take away the iniquity of your servant, for I have done very foolishly.”
10_24_10.NASB: Now David's heart troubled him after he had numbered the people. So David said to the LORD, 'I have sinned greatly in what I have done. But now, O LORD, please take away the iniquity of Your servant, for I have acted very foolishly.'
10_24_10.NLT: But after he had taken the census, David’s conscience began to bother him. And he said to the LORD, “I have sinned greatly by taking this census. Please forgive my guilt, LORD, for doing this foolish thing.”
10_24_10.WEB: David’s heart struck him after he had counted the people. David said to Yahweh, “I have sinned greatly in that which I have done. But now, Yahweh, put away, I beg you, the iniquity of your servant; for I have done very foolishly.”
10_24_10.CSB17: David’s conscience troubled him after he had taken a census of the troops. He said to the LORD, “I have sinned greatly in what I’ve done. Now, LORD, because I’ve been very foolish, please take away your servant’s guilt.”
10_24_10.AMP: But David's heart (conscience) troubled him after he had counted the people. David said to the LORD, "I have sinned greatly in what I have done. But now, O LORD, please take away the sin of Your servant, for I have acted very foolishly."
10_24_10.MSG: But when it was all done, David was overwhelmed with guilt because he had counted the people, replacing trust with statistics. And David prayed to God, "I have sinned badly in what I have just done. But now God forgive my guilt—I've been really stupid."
10_24_10.NIV: David was conscience-stricken after he had counted the fighting men, and he said to the Lord, “I have sinned greatly in what I have done. Now, O Lord, I beg you, take away the guilt of your servant. I have done a very foolish thing.”
10_24_10.NRSVCE: But afterward, David was stricken to the heart because he had numbered the people. David said to the LORD, “I have sinned greatly in what I have done. But now, O LORD, I pray you, take away the guilt of your servant; for I have done very foolishly.”

10_24_11.NKJV: Now when David arose in the morning, the word of the LORD came to the prophet Gad, David’s seer, saying,

10_24_11.ESV: And when David arose in the morning, the word of the LORD came to the prophet Gad, David's seer, saying,
10_24_11.NASB: When David arose in the morning, the word of the LORD came to the prophet Gad, David's seer, saying,
10_24_11.NLT: The next morning the word of the LORD came to the prophet Gad, who was David’s seer. This was the message:
10_24_11.WEB: When David rose up in the morning, Yahweh’s word came to the prophet Gad, David’s seer, saying,
10_24_11.CSB17: When David got up in the morning, the word of the LORD had come to the prophet Gad, David’s seer:
10_24_11.AMP: When David got up in the morning, the word of the LORD came to the prophet Gad, David's seer, saying,
10_24_11.MSG: When David got up the next morning, the word of God had already come to Gad the prophet, David's spiritual advisor,
10_24_11.NIV: Before David got up the next morning, the word of the Lord had come to Gad the prophet, David’s seer:
10_24_11.NRSVCE: When David rose in the morning, the word of the LORD came to the prophet Gad, David’s seer, saying,

10_24_12.NKJV: “Go and tell David, ‘Thus says the LORD: “I offer you three things; choose one of them for yourself, that I may do it to you.” ’ ”
10_24_12.KJV: Go and say unto David, Thus saith the LORD, I offer thee three things; choose thee one of them, that I may do it unto thee.
10_24_12.ESV: “Go and say to David, ‘Thus says the LORD, Three things I offer you. Choose one of them, that I may do it to you.’”
10_24_12.NASB: 'Go and speak to David, 'Thus the LORD says, 'I am offering you three things; choose for yourself one of them, which I will do to you.'''
10_24_12.NLT: “Go and say to David, ‘This is what the LORD says: I will give you three choices. Choose one of these punishments, and I will inflict it on you.’”
10_24_12.WEB: “Go and speak to David, ‘Yahweh says, “I offer you three things. Choose one of them, that I may do it to you.”’”
10_24_12.CSB17: “Go and say to David, ‘This is what the LORD says: I am offering you three choices. Choose one of them, and I will do it to you.’”
10_24_12.AMP: "Go and say to David, 'Thus says the LORD, "I am giving you three choices; select one of them for yourself, and I will do it to you."'"
10_24_12.MSG: "Go and give David this message: 'God has spoken thus: There are three things I can do to you; choose one out of the three and I'll see that it's done.' "
10_24_12.NIV: “Go and tell David, ‘This is what the Lord says: I am giving you three options. Choose one of them for me to carry out against you.’”
10_24_12.NRSVCE: “Go and say to David: Thus says the LORD: Three things I offer you; choose one of them, and I will do it to you.”

10_24_13.NKJV: So Gad came to David and told him; and he said to him, “Shall seven years of famine come to you in your land? Or shall you flee three months before your enemies, while they pursue you? Or shall there be three days’ plague in your land? Now consider and see what answer I should take back to Him who sent me.”
10_24_13.KJV: So Gad came to David, and told him, and said unto him, Shall seven years of famine come unto thee in thy land? or wilt thou flee three months before thine enemies, while they pursue thee? or that there be three days' pestilence in thy land? now advise, and see what answer I shall return to him that sent me.
10_24_13.ESV: So Gad came to David and told him, and said to him, “Shall three years of famine come to you in your land? Or will you flee three months before your foes while they pursue you? Or shall there be three days' pestilence in your land? Now consider, and decide what answer I shall return to him who sent me.”
10_24_13.NASB: So Gad came to David and told him, and said to him, 'Shall seven years of famine come to you in your land? Or will you flee three months before your foes while they pursue you? Or shall there be three days' pestilence in your land? Now consider and see what answer I shall return to Him who sent me.'
10_24_13.NLT: So Gad came to David and asked him, “Will you choose three years of famine throughout your land, three months of fleeing from your enemies, or three days of severe plague throughout your land? Think this over and decide what answer I should give the LORD who sent me.”
10_24_13.WEB: So Gad came to David, and told him, and said to him, “Shall seven years of famine come to you in your land? Or will you flee three months before your foes while they pursue you? Or shall there be three days’ pestilence in your land? Now answer, and consider what answer I shall return to him who sent me.”
10_24_13.CSB17: So Gad went to David, told him the choices, and asked him, “Do you want three years of famine to come on your land, to flee from your foes three months while they pursue you, or to have a plague in your land three days? Now, consider carefully what answer I should take back to the one who sent me.”
10_24_13.AMP: So Gad came to David and told him, and said to him, "Shall seven years of famine come to you in your land? Or will you flee three months before your enemies as they pursue you? Or shall there be three days of pestilence (plague) in your land? Now consider this and decide what answer I shall return to Him who sent me."
10_24_13.MSG: Gad came to deliver the message: "Do you want three years of famine in the land, or three months of running from your enemies while they chase you down, or three days of an epidemic on the country? Think it over and make up your mind. What shall I tell the one who sent me?"
10_24_13.NIV: So Gad went to David and said to him, “Shall there come upon you three years of famine in your land? Or three months of fleeing from your enemies while they pursue you? Or three days of plague in your land? Now then, think it over and decide how I should answer the one who sent me.”
10_24_13.NRSVCE: So Gad came to David and told him; he asked him, “Shall three years of famine come to you on your land? Or will you flee three months before your foes while they pursue you? Or shall there be three days’ pestilence in your land? Now consider, and decide what answer I shall return to the one who sent me.”

10_24_14.NKJV: And David said to Gad, “I am in great distress. Please let us fall into the hand of the LORD, for His mercies are great; but do not let me fall into the hand of man.”
10_24_14.KJV: And David said unto Gad, I am in a great strait: let us fall now into the hand of the LORD; for his mercies are great: and let me not fall into the hand of man. are great: or, are many
10_24_14.ESV: Then David said to Gad, “I am in great distress. Let us fall into the hand of the LORD, for his mercy is great; but let me not fall into the hand of man.”
10_24_14.NASB: Then David said to Gad, 'I am in great distress. Let us now fall into the hand of the LORD for His mercies are great, but do not let me fall into the hand of man.'
